I’m set up to use the GitHub plugin. Send me one concrete target and I’ll take it from there:

- PR URL or `owner/repo#123`
- issue URL or `owner/repo#123`
- repo name like `owner/repo` for triage
- branch/PR plus what checks are failing
- repo/branch plus the code change you want prepared

Quick note: this scratch workspace doesn’t appear to have `git` available on PATH, so for local branch work I may need either a repo checkout here or to use the GitHub connector directly where possible.

Ja, ich sehe in deinem GitHub-Zugriff den Account `DopeChicka` und dort das Repository:

[DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG](https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG)

Es ist `public`, nicht archiviert, Default-Branch ist `main`.

> # Role
> You are a senior backend engineer building a production-grade compliance auditing platform.
>
> # Project Name
> NorthAccessBFSG
>
> # Goal
> Initialize the backend foundation for the system **NorthAccessBFSG**, a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.
>
> You must create a minimal, production-ready, fully runnable backend scaffold.
>
> # Scope (STRICT MINIMUM VIABLE FOUNDATION)
>
> ## 1. Backend Core
> - FastAPI application
> - Health endpoint: GET /health → {"status": "ok"}
>
> ## 2. Database Layer
> Use PostgreSQL with SQLAlchemy (or SQLModel)
>
> Create only these core models:
>
> ### Lead
> - id (uuid/string)
> - domain
> - company_name
> - created_at
>
> ### Scan
> - id
> - lead_id (FK)
> - status (pending/running/done)
> - created_at
>
> ### Finding
> - id
> - scan_id (FK)
> - rule_id
> - severity
> - created_at
>
> ## 3. Infrastructure
> - PostgreSQL connection setup
> - environment-based config (.env support)
> - dependency injection for DB session
>
> ## 4. Project Structure
> Create clean modular structure:
>
> - app/
>   - main.py
>   - api/
>   - core/
>   - models/
>   - db/
>
> ## 5. Docker Setup
> Provide:
> - Dockerfile for API
> - docker-compose.yml including:
>   - API service
>   - PostgreSQL service
>
> ## 6. Startup behavior
> - Tables must be created automatically (or via simple init step)
> - App must start cleanly via docker-compose up
>
> # IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS
> - DO NOT implement Playwright
> - DO NOT implement Celery / Redis
> - DO NOT implement compliance mapping yet
> - DO NOT implement reporting or CLI
> - Keep system intentionally minimal and bootable
>
> # Output Requirements
> Return:
>
> 1. Full project folder structure
> 2. All required Python code
> 3. Dockerfile
> 4. docker-compose.yml
> 5. Minimal explanation of how to run
>
> # Validation Criteria
> System is correct only if:
>
> - docker-compose up starts successfully
> - API responds on /health
> - DB container is connected
> - tables exist or are auto-created
> - no missing imports or circular dependencies
>
> # Success Definition
> A developer can clone the project and immediately run:
>
> docker-compose up
>
> and see:
> - FastAPI running
> - PostgreSQL running
> - /health endpoint responding

>
> Ich halte den Umfang bewusst eng: FastAPI, SQLAlchemy/Postgres, drei Kernmodelle, Session-DI, Docker Compose und automatisches `create_all` beim API-Start. Keine Scanner-, Worker- oder Reporting-Schichten.
>

>     {
>       "path": "README.md",
>       "mode": "100644",
>       "type": "blob",
>       "content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nMinimal FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    health.py\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  models/\n    __init__.py\n    finding.py\n    lead.py\n    scan.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```text\nhttp://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the `leads`, `scans`, and `findings` tables on startup.\n"
>     },
>     {
>       "path": ".env.example",
>       "mode": "100644",
>       "type": "blob",
>       "content": "DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@localhost:5432/northaccessbfsg\nENVIRONMENT=development\n"
>     },

>       "path": ".gitignore",
>       "mode": "100644",
>       "type": "blob",
>       "content": "__pycache__/\n*.py[cod]\n*.egg-info/\n.pytest_cache/\n.mypy_cache/\n.ruff_cache/\n.venv/\n.env\n.env.*\n!.env.example\n.DS_Store\n"
>     },

>       "content": "services:\n  api:\n    build: .\n    environment:\n      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@db:5432/northaccessbfsg\n      ENVIRONMENT: development\n    ports:\n      - \"8000:8000\"\n    depends_on:\n      db:\n        condition: service_healthy\n\n  db:\n    image: postgres:16-alpine\n    environment:\n      POSTGRES_USER: northaccess\n      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: northaccess\n      POSTGRES_DB: northaccessbfsg\n    ports:\n      - \"5432:5432\"\n    volumes:\n      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD-SHELL\", \"pg_isready -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg\"]\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 10\n\nvolumes:\n  postgres_data:\n"

>       "content": "from contextlib import asynccontextmanager\nfrom collections.abc import AsyncIterator\n\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\n\nfrom app.api.health import router as health_router\nfrom app.core.config import settings\nfrom app.db.init_db import init_db\n\n\n@asynccontextmanager\nasync def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:\n    init_db()\n    yield\n\n\napp = FastAPI(\n    title=settings.app_name,\n    version=\"0.1.0\",\n    lifespan=lifespan,\n)\n\napp.include_router(health_router)\n"

>       "content": "from fastapi import APIRouter\n\nrouter = APIRouter(tags=[\"health\"])\n\n\n@router.get(\"/health\")\ndef health() -> dict[str, str]:\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n"
>     },

>       "path": "app/core/__init__.py",

>       "path": "app/core/config.py",
>       "mode": "100644",
>       "type": "blob",
>       "content": "from functools import lru_cache\n\nfrom pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict\n\n\nclass Settings(BaseSettings):\n    app_name: str = \"NorthAccessBFSG API\"\n    environment: str = \"development\"\n    database_url: str = (\n        \"postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@localhost:5432/northaccessbfsg\"\n    )\n\n    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(\n        env_file=\".env\",\n        env_file_encoding=\"utf-8\",\n        extra=\"ignore\",\n    )\n\n\n@lru_cache\ndef get_settings() -> Settings:\n    return Settings()\n\n\nsettings = get_settings()\n"
>     },

>       "content": "from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase\n\n\nclass Base(DeclarativeBase):\n    pass\n"

>       "path": "app/db/init_db.py",
>       "mode": "100644",
>       "type": "blob",
>       "content": "from app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.db.session import engine\nfrom app.models import Finding, Lead, Scan  # noqa: F401\n\n\ndef init_db() -> None:\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n"
>     },
>     {
>       "path": "app/db/session.py",
>       "mode": "100644",
>       "type": "blob",
>       "content": "from collections.abc import Generator\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\n\nfrom app.core.config import settings\n\nengine = create_engine(settings.database_url, pool_pre_ping=True)\nSessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)\n\n\ndef get_db() -> Generator[Session, None, None]:\n    db = SessionLocal()\n    try:\n        yield db\n    finally:\n        db.close()\n"
>     },
>     {
>       "path": "app/models/__init__.py",
>       "mode": "100644",
>       "type": "blob",
>       "content": "from app.models.finding import Finding\nfrom app.models.lead import Lead\nfrom app.models.scan import Scan, ScanStatus\n\n__all__ = [\"Finding\", \"Lead\", \"Scan\", \"ScanStatus\"]\n"
>     },
>     {
>       "path": "app/models/lead.py",
>       "mode": "100644",
>       "type": "blob",
>       "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, String, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass Lead(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"leads\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    domain: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False, index=True)\n    company_name: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n\n    scans: Mapped[list[Scan]] = relationship(\n        back_populates=\"lead\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n"
>     },
>     {
>       "path": "app/models/scan.py",
>       "mode": "100644",
>       "type": "blob",
>       "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey, String, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass ScanStatus(str, enum.Enum):\n    pending = \"pending\"\n    running = \"running\"\n    done = \"done\"\n\n\nclass Scan(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"scans\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    lead_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"leads.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n    )\n    status: Mapped[ScanStatus] = mapped_column(\n        Enum(ScanStatus, name=\"scan_status\"), default=ScanStatus.pending, nullable=False\n    )\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n\n    lead: Mapped[Lead] = relationship(back_populates=\"scans\")\n    findings: Mapped[list[Finding]] = relationship(\n        back_populates=\"scan\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n"
>     },
>     {
>       "path": "app/models/finding.py",

>       "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, ForeignKey, String, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass Finding(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"findings\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    scan_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"scans.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n    )\n    rule_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100), nullable=False, index=True)\n    severity: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(50), nullable=False)\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n\n    scan: Mapped[Scan] = relationship(back_populates=\"findings\")\n"
>     }

Erledigt: Ich habe im Repo `DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG` eine Branch `codex/backend-foundation` erstellt und einen Draft-PR geöffnet:

[PR #1: Initialize FastAPI backend foundation](https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/1)

**Struktur**
```text
app/
  main.py
  api/health.py
  core/config.py
  db/base.py
  db/init_db.py
  db/session.py
  models/lead.py
  models/scan.py
  models/finding.py
Dockerfile
docker-compose.yml
requirements.txt
.env.example
```

Enthalten sind FastAPI mit `GET /health`, PostgreSQL/SQLAlchemy-Setup, `.env`-basierte Config, DB-Session-DI, die drei Modelle `Lead`, `Scan`, `Finding`, automatisches `create_all` beim Start und Docker Compose für API + Postgres.

Start:
```bash
docker-compose up --build
curl http://localhost:8000/health
```

Erwartet:
```json
{"status":"ok"}
```

Hinweis: Ich konnte `docker-compose` in dieser Scratch-Umgebung nicht lokal ausführen; der PR wurde per Repo-Diff und Import-/Dependency-Review geprüft.

> push live.

Done. `NorthAccessBFSG` is now live on `main`.

PR #1 is closed and marked merged by GitHub: [Initialize FastAPI backend foundation](https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/1)

`main` now contains the FastAPI backend, Postgres setup, Dockerfile, `docker-compose.yml`, and `/health` endpoint. Small caveat: this pushes it live to GitHub `main`; no external hosting platform is configured yet.

> # Role
> You are a senior backend engineer extending an existing FastAPI project called NorthAccessBFSG.
>
> # Goal
> Implement a production-ready asynchronous scan execution system using Celery + Redis.
>
> This system will be responsible for executing BFSG accessibility scans in the background.
>
> # Context
> Existing project already has:
> - FastAPI app
> - PostgreSQL models (Lead, Scan, Finding)
> - Docker setup
>
> You MUST extend this system without breaking existing functionality.
>
> # Scope (STRICT)
>
> ## 1. Add Redis + Celery infrastructure
> - Add Redis service to docker-compose
> - Configure Celery app inside /app/core/celery.py
>
> ## 2. Create worker system
> Add worker module:
> - app/workers/scan_worker.py
>
> Worker must:
> - accept scan_id
> - update Scan status to "running"
> - simulate scan execution (placeholder logic for now)
> - update Scan status to "done"
>
> ## 3. Scan orchestration service
> Add:
> - app/services/scan_service.py
>
> Must:
> - create scan job
> - push task to Celery queue
> - persist scan lifecycle updates
>
> ## 4. API endpoint
> Extend API:
>
> POST /scans/run/{lead_id}
>
> Behavior:
> - creates Scan entry
> - triggers Celery task
> - returns scan_id immediately
>
> ## 5. Database updates
> Ensure Scan model supports:
> - status: pending | running | done | failed
> - timestamps for lifecycle tracking
>
> ## 6. Docker updates
> Update docker-compose.yml:
> - add redis service
> - add celery worker service
>
> Worker must be able to run independently:
> celery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info
>
> # Constraints
> - DO NOT implement Playwright yet
> - DO NOT implement compliance engine yet
> - DO NOT implement reporting yet
> - DO NOT modify existing models unnecessarily
> - Keep changes incremental and safe
>
> # Success Criteria
> System is correct if:
>
> - docker-compose up starts API + DB + Redis + Worker
> - POST /scans/run/{lead_id} triggers async task
> - Scan status transitions: pending → running → done
> - Worker logs show task execution
> - No blocking API calls
>
> # Output Required
> 1. Code changes (files + structure)
> 2. docker-compose.yml updates
> 3. Celery configuration
> 4. Example request flow
> 5. Minimal run instructions

<details><summary>14 previous messages</summary>

> {"content": "from contextlib import asynccontextmanager\nfrom collections.abc import AsyncIterator\n\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\n\nfrom app.api.health import router as health_router\nfrom app.core.config import settings\nfrom app.db.init_db import init_db\n\n\n@asynccontextmanager\nasync def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:\n    init_db()\n    yield\n\n\napp = FastAPI(\n    title=settings.app_name,\n    version=\"0.1.0\",\n    lifespan=lifespan,\n)\n\napp.include_router(health_router)\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "b854988c323c60378334f06e2e684073e15e8c57", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/main.py", "display_title": "main.py"}

> ```text
> {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey, String, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass ScanStatus(str, enum.Enum):\n    pending = \"pending\"\n    running = \"running\"\n    done = \"done\"\n\n\nclass Scan(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"scans\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    lead_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"leads.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n    )\n    status: Mapped[ScanStatus] = mapped_column(\n        Enum(ScanStatus, name=\"scan_status\"), default=ScanStatus.pending, nullable=False\n    )\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n\n    lead: Mapped[\"Lead\"] = relationship(\"Lead\", back_populates=\"scans\")\n    findings: Mapped[list[\"Finding\"]] = relationship(\n        \"Finding\", back_populates=\"scan\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "d978deb987ca6fba1d2109adf5f143d997300da1", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/models/scan.py", "display_title": "scan.py"}
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "from collections.abc import Generator\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\n\nfrom app.core.config import settings\n\nengine = create_engine(settings.database_url, pool_pre_ping=True)\nSessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)\n\n\ndef get_db() -> Generator[Session, None, None]:\n    db = SessionLocal()\n    try:\n        yield db\n    finally:\n        db.close()\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "199bb49dbbc8f05c5b513dfcdb9faadbcee9c6f0", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/db/session.py", "display_title": "session.py"}

> ````text
> {"content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nMinimal FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    health.py\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  models/\n    __init__.py\n    finding.py\n    lead.py\n    scan.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```text\nhttp://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the `leads`, `scans`, and `findings` tables on startup.\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "35a77bc70fff6752721657ee46f11b4fe7692416", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/README.md", "display_title": "README.md"}
> ````

> ```text
> {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, String, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass Lead(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"leads\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    domain: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False, index=True)\n    company_name: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n\n    scans: Mapped[list[\"Scan\"]] = relationship(\n        \"Scan\", back_populates=\"lead\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "98fcb5437d3be5e415a7e72bd42ae752da2a1a04", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/models/lead.py", "display_title": "lead.py"}

>   "content": "from functools import lru_cache\n\nfrom pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict\n\n\nclass Settings(BaseSettings):\n    app_name: str = \"NorthAccessBFSG API\"\n    environment: str = \"development\"\n    database_url: str = (\n        \"postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@localhost:5432/northaccessbfsg\"\n    )\n    celery_broker_url: str = \"redis://localhost:6379/0\"\n    celery_result_backend: str = \"redis://localhost:6379/1\"\n\n    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(\n        env_file=\".env\",\n        env_file_encoding=\"utf-8\",\n        extra=\"ignore\",\n    )\n\n\n@lru_cache\ndef get_settings() -> Settings:\n    return Settings()\n\n\nsettings = get_settings()\n"
> }

> Die Celery-App ist drin, der GitHub-Write war nur auffällig langsam. Ich mache jetzt die fachliche Schicht: Scan-Service, Worker-Task und den API-Router.
>

>
> codex_apps.github_create_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/celery-scan-worker",
>   "path": "app/workers/scan_worker.py",
>   "message": "Add asynchronous scan worker",
>   "content": "import logging\nimport time\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\n\nfrom app.core.celery import celery_app\nfrom app.db.session import SessionLocal\nfrom app.models import Scan, ScanStatus\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n@celery_app.task(name=\"app.workers.scan_worker.run_scan\")\ndef run_scan_task(scan_id: str) -> str:\n    db = SessionLocal()\n    try:\n        scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n        if scan is None:\n            logger.warning(\"Scan %s was not found; skipping task\", scan_id)\n            return scan_id\n\n        scan.status = ScanStatus.running\n        scan.started_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n        db.commit()\n\n        logger.info(\"Started placeholder accessibility scan %s\", scan_id)\n        time.sleep(2)\n\n        scan.status = ScanStatus.done\n        scan.completed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n        db.commit()\n\n        logger.info(\"Completed placeholder accessibility scan %s\", scan_id)\n        return scan_id\n    except Exception:\n        db.rollback()\n        _mark_scan_failed(db, scan_id)\n        logger.exception(\"Scan task %s failed\", scan_id)\n        raise\n    finally:\n        db.close()\n\n\ndef _mark_scan_failed(db, scan_id: str) -> None:\n    scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n    if scan is None:\n        return\n\n    scan.status = ScanStatus.failed\n    scan.failed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n    db.commit()\n"
> }
> ```
>

> ```

> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_create_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/celery-scan-worker",
>   "path": "app/services/scan_service.py",
>   "message": "Add scan orchestration service",
>   "content": "from datetime import UTC, datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n\nfrom app.models import Lead, Scan, ScanStatus\nfrom app.workers.scan_worker import run_scan_task\n\n\nclass LeadNotFoundError(Exception):\n    pass\n\n\nclass ScanQueueError(Exception):\n    pass\n\n\ndef create_scan_job(db: Session, lead_id: str) -> Scan:\n    lead = db.get(Lead, lead_id)\n    if lead is None:\n        raise LeadNotFoundError(f\"Lead {lead_id} was not found\")\n\n    scan = Scan(lead_id=lead.id, status=ScanStatus.pending)\n    db.add(scan)\n    db.commit()\n    db.refresh(scan)\n\n    try:\n        run_scan_task.delay(scan.id)\n    except Exception as exc:\n        scan.status = ScanStatus.failed\n        scan.failed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n        db.commit()\n        raise ScanQueueError(\"Could not enqueue scan task\") from exc\n\n    return scan\n"
> }
> ```
>

> ```

> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_create_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/celery-scan-worker",
>   "path": "app/api/scans.py",
>   "message": "Add scan execution endpoint",
>   "content": "from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, status\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n\nfrom app.db.session import get_db\nfrom app.services.scan_service import LeadNotFoundError, ScanQueueError, create_scan_job\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/scans\", tags=[\"scans\"])\n\n\n@router.post(\"/run/{lead_id}\", status_code=status.HTTP_202_ACCEPTED)\ndef run_scan(lead_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, str]:\n    try:\n        scan = create_scan_job(db, lead_id)\n    except LeadNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=\"Lead not found\"\n        ) from exc\n    except ScanQueueError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=status.HTTP_503_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,\n            detail=\"Scan queue is unavailable\",\n        ) from exc\n\n    return {\"scan_id\": scan.id}\n"
> }
> ```

>
> Der nicht-blockierende Pfad ist jetzt angelegt: API erstellt nur den Datensatz und queued den Celery-Task. Als Nächstes erweitere ich das Scan-Modell und die Startinitialisierung, damit frische und bereits vorhandene lokale DBs die neuen Status-/Zeitfelder kennen.
>
> <details><summary>Called 3 tools</summary>
>
> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_update_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/celery-scan-worker",
>   "path": "app/models/scan.py",
>   "sha": "d978deb987ca6fba1d2109adf5f143d997300da1",
>   "message": "Extend scan lifecycle model",
>   "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey, String, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass ScanStatus(str, enum.Enum):\n    pending = \"pending\"\n    running = \"running\"\n    done = \"done\"\n    failed = \"failed\"\n\n\nclass Scan(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"scans\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    lead_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"leads.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n    )\n    status: Mapped[ScanStatus] = mapped_column(\n        Enum(ScanStatus, name=\"scan_status\"), default=ScanStatus.pending, nullable=False\n    )\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n    started_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True))\n    completed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True))\n    failed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True))\n\n    lead: Mapped[\"Lead\"] = relationship(\"Lead\", back_populates=\"scans\")\n    findings: Mapped[list[\"Finding\"]] = relationship(\n        \"Finding\", back_populates=\"scan\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n"
> }
> ```

>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/celery-scan-worker",
>   "path": "app/db/init_db.py",
>   "sha": "04201335adf244490870df2c5221026864c476eb",
>   "message": "Initialize scan lifecycle schema",
>   "content": "from sqlalchemy import text\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.db.session import engine\nfrom app.models import Finding, Lead, Scan  # noqa: F401\n\n\n_SCAN_SCHEMA_UPDATES = (\n    \"ALTER TYPE scan_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'failed'\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS started_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS failed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n)\n\n\ndef init_db() -> None:\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n    _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema()\n\n\ndef _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema() -> None:\n    if engine.dialect.name != \"postgresql\":\n        return\n\n    with engine.begin() as connection:\n        for statement in _SCAN_SCHEMA_UPDATES:\n            connection.execute(text(statement))\n"
> }
> ```
>

>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/celery-scan-worker",
>   "path": "app/main.py",
>   "sha": "b854988c323c60378334f06e2e684073e15e8c57",
>   "message": "Register scan API router",
>   "content": "from collections.abc import AsyncIterator\nfrom contextlib import asynccontextmanager\n\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\n\nfrom app.api.health import router as health_router\nfrom app.api.scans import router as scans_router\nfrom app.core.config import settings\nfrom app.db.init_db import init_db\n\n\n@asynccontextmanager\nasync def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:\n    init_db()\n    yield\n\n\napp = FastAPI(\n    title=settings.app_name,\n    version=\"0.1.0\",\n    lifespan=lifespan,\n)\n\napp.include_router(health_router)\napp.include_router(scans_router)\n"
> }
> ```

>
> Die App verdrahtet jetzt den neuen Router. Ich aktualisiere zum Schluss Compose und README, damit API, DB, Redis und Worker gemeinsam starten.
>
> <details><summary>Called 4 tools</summary>
>
> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_update_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/celery-scan-worker",
>   "path": "docker-compose.yml",
>   "sha": "6ca74c580277107a03f32dc5db94a2b4ac82831c",
>   "message": "Add Redis and Celery worker services",
>   "content": "services:\n  api:\n    build: .\n    environment:\n      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@db:5432/northaccessbfsg\n      ENVIRONMENT: development\n      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\n      CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND: redis://redis:6379/1\n    ports:\n      - \"8000:8000\"\n    depends_on:\n      db:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      redis:\n        condition: service_healthy\n\n  worker:\n    build: .\n    command: celery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info\n    environment:\n      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@db:5432/northaccessbfsg\n      ENVIRONMENT: development\n      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\n      CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND: redis://redis:6379/1\n    depends_on:\n      db:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      redis:\n        condition: service_healthy\n\n  db:\n    image: postgres:16-alpine\n    environment:\n      POSTGRES_USER: northaccess\n      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: northaccess\n      POSTGRES_DB: northaccessbfsg\n    ports:\n      - \"5432:5432\"\n    volumes:\n      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD-SHELL\", \"pg_isready -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg\"]\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 10\n\n  redis:\n    image: redis:7-alpine\n    ports:\n      - \"6379:6379\"\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD\", \"redis-cli\", \"ping\"]\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 10\n\nvolumes:\n  postgres_data:\n"
> }
> ```

> codex_apps.github_update_file
>
> ````json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/celery-scan-worker",
>   "path": "README.md",
>   "sha": "35a77bc70fff6752721657ee46f11b4fe7692416",
>   "message": "Document async scan worker flow",
>   "content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nMinimal FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    health.py\n    scans.py\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    celery.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  models/\n    __init__.py\n    finding.py\n    lead.py\n    scan.py\n  services/\n    __init__.py\n    scan_service.py\n  workers/\n    __init__.py\n    scan_worker.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```text\nhttp://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the `leads`, `scans`, and `findings` tables on startup.\n\n## Async Scan Flow\n\nRun a scan for an existing lead:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/{lead_id}\n```\n\nExpected immediate response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker then updates the scan lifecycle:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> done\n```\n\nIf task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed`.\n\nThe worker process can also be started independently:\n\n```bash\ncelery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info\n```\n"
> }
> ````
>

> ```text
> {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey, String, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass ScanStatus(str, enum.Enum):\n    pending = \"pending\"\n    running = \"running\"\n    done = \"done\"\n    failed = \"failed\"\n\n\nclass Scan(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"scans\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    lead_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"leads.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n    )\n    status: Mapped[ScanStatus] = mapped_column(\n        Enum(ScanStatus, name=\"scan_status\"), default=ScanStatus.pending, nullable=False\n    )\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n    started_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True))\n    completed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True))\n    failed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True))\n\n    lead: Mapped[\"Lead\"] = relationship(\"Lead\", back_populates=\"scans\")\n    findings: Mapped[list[\"Finding\"]] = relationship(\n        \"Finding\", back_populates=\"scan\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "66445cf3004063f05bf6d451350ffac80c048e3a", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/celery-scan-worker/app/models/scan.py", "display_title": "scan.py"}
> ```

> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/celery-scan-worker",
>   "path": "app/models/scan.py",
>   "sha": "66445cf3004063f05bf6d451350ffac80c048e3a",
>   "message": "Make scan lifecycle timestamps explicitly nullable",
>   "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey, String, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass ScanStatus(str, enum.Enum):\n    pending = \"pending\"\n    running = \"running\"\n    done = \"done\"\n    failed = \"failed\"\n\n\nclass Scan(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"scans\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    lead_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"leads.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n    )\n    status: Mapped[ScanStatus] = mapped_column(\n        Enum(ScanStatus, name=\"scan_status\"), default=ScanStatus.pending, nullable=False\n    )\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n    started_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    completed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    failed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n\n    lead: Mapped[\"Lead\"] = relationship(\"Lead\", back_populates=\"scans\")\n    findings: Mapped[list[\"Finding\"]] = relationship(\n        \"Finding\", back_populates=\"scan\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n"
> }

> ````json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "base_branch": "main",
>   "head_branch": "codex/celery-scan-worker",
>   "title": "Add Celery Redis scan execution worker",
>   "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add Redis-backed Celery configuration in `app/core/celery.py`\n- Add asynchronous scan worker in `app/workers/scan_worker.py`\n- Add scan orchestration service and `POST /scans/run/{lead_id}` endpoint\n- Extend `Scan` status lifecycle with `failed`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, and `failed_at`\n- Update Docker Compose to run API, PostgreSQL, Redis, and an independent Celery worker\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/{lead_id}\n```\n\nImmediate API response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe worker transitions the scan:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> done\n```\n\nFailures are persisted as `failed` with `failed_at`.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Intentionally keeps scan execution as placeholder logic only.\n- Does not add Playwright, compliance engine, reporting, or CLI.\n- I could not run Docker from this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.",
>   "draft": true
> }
> ````

>   "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add Redis-backed Celery configuration in `app/core/celery.py`\n- Add asynchronous scan worker in `app/workers/scan_worker.py`\n- Add scan orchestration service and `POST /scans/run/{lead_id}` endpoint\n- Extend `Scan` status lifecycle with `failed`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, and `failed_at`\n- Update Docker Compose to run API, PostgreSQL, Redis, and an independent Celery worker\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/{lead_id}\n```\n\nImmediate API response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe worker transitions the scan:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> done\n```\n\nFailures are persisted as `failed` with `failed_at`.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Intentionally keeps scan execution as placeholder logic only.\n- Does not add Playwright, compliance engine, reporting, or CLI.\n- I could not run Docker from this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.",

> {"url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/2", "number": 2, "state": "open", "merged": false, "mergeable": true, "draft": true, "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add Redis-backed Celery configuration in `app/core/celery.py`\n- Add asynchronous scan worker in `app/workers/scan_worker.py`\n- Add scan orchestration service and `POST /scans/run/{lead_id}` endpoint\n- Extend `Scan` status lifecycle with `failed`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, and `failed_at`\n- Update Docker Compose to run API, PostgreSQL, Redis, and an independent Celery worker\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/{lead_id}\n```\n\nImmediate API response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe worker transitions the scan:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> done\n```\n\nFailures are persisted as `failed` with `failed_at`.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Intentionally keeps scan execution as placeholder logic only.\n- Does not add Playwright, compliance engine, reporting, or CLI.\n- I could not run Docker from this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.", "title": "Add Celery Redis scan execution worker", "base": "main", "base_sha": "f5a92ded1f8143faf190eab9159c05b5c03c3bd8", "head": "codex/celery-scan-worker", "head_sha": "c677973c900ebab45a812cc257d2794749931a4d", "merge_commit_sha": "a8e0e6de83f50ee3d7db509b5d4a5fea2fadad9d", "user": {"login": "DopeChicka", "name": "DopeChicka", "email": null, "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25302183?v=4", "id": 25302183}, "requested_reviewers": null, "requested_team_reviewers": null, "diff": null, "comments": null, "created_at": "2026-05-08T11:22:23Z", "updated_at": "2026-05-08T11:22:23Z", "closed_at": null, "merged_at": null, "commits": 15, "changed_files": 14, "additions": 231, "deletions": 2, "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/2", "display_title": "Add Celery Redis scan execution worker"}
> ````

> ````json
> {
>   "url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/2",
>   "number": 2,
>   "state": "open",
>   "merged": false,
>   "mergeable": true,
>   "draft": true,
>   "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add Redis-backed Celery configuration in `app/core/celery.py`\n- Add asynchronous scan worker in `app/workers/scan_worker.py`\n- Add scan orchestration service and `POST /scans/run/{lead_id}` endpoint\n- Extend `Scan` status lifecycle with `failed`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, and `failed_at`\n- Update Docker Compose to run API, PostgreSQL, Redis, and an independent Celery worker\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/{lead_id}\n```\n\nImmediate API response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe worker transitions the scan:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> done\n```\n\nFailures are persisted as `failed` with `failed_at`.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Intentionally keeps scan execution as placeholder logic only.\n- Does not add Playwright, compliance engine, reporting, or CLI.\n- I could not run Docker from this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.",
>   "title": "Add Celery Redis scan execution worker",
>   "base": "main",
>   "base_sha": "f5a92ded1f8143faf190eab9159c05b5c03c3bd8",

>   "updated_at": "2026-05-08T11:22:23Z",

>   "merged_at": null,
>   "commits": 15,
>   "changed_files": 14,

> ````text
> {"diff": "diff --git a/.env.example b/.env.example\nindex 5465735..3593663 100644\n--- a/.env.example\n+++ b/.env.example\n@@ -1,2 +1,4 @@\n DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@localhost:5432/northaccessbfsg\n ENVIRONMENT=development\n+CELERY_BROKER_URL=redis://localhost:6379/0\n+CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND=redis://localhost:6379/1\ndiff --git a/README.md b/README.md\nindex 35a77bc..2cbe941 100644\n--- a/README.md\n+++ b/README.md\n@@ -11,8 +11,10 @@ app/\n   api/\n     __init__.py\n     health.py\n+    scans.py\n   core/\n     __init__.py\n+    celery.py\n     config.py\n   db/\n     __init__.py\n@@ -24,6 +26,12 @@ app/\n     finding.py\n     lead.py\n     scan.py\n+  services/\n+    __init__.py\n+    scan_service.py\n+  workers/\n+    __init__.py\n+    scan_worker.py\n Dockerfile\n docker-compose.yml\n requirements.txt\n@@ -48,4 +56,32 @@ Expected response:\n {\"status\":\"ok\"}\n ```\n \n-The API waits for PostgreSQL to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the `leads`, `scans`, and `findings` tables on startup.\n+The API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the `leads`, `scans`, and `findings` tables on startup.\n+\n+## Async Scan Flow\n+\n+Run a scan for an existing lead:\n+\n+```bash\n+curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/{lead_id}\n+```\n+\n+Expected immediate response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n+```\n+\n+The API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker then updates the scan lifecycle:\n+\n+```text\n+pending -> running -> done\n+```\n+\n+If task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed`.\n+\n+The worker process can also be started independently:\n+\n+```bash\n+celery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info\n+```\ndiff --git a/app/api/scans.py b/app/api/scans.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..6a117f6\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/app/api/scans.py\n@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@\n+from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, status\n+from sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n+\n+from app.db.session import get_db\n+from app.services.scan_service import LeadNotFoundError, ScanQueueError, create_scan_job\n+\n+router = APIRouter(prefix=\"/scans\", tags=[\"scans\"])\n+\n+\n+@router.post(\"/run/{lead_id}\", status_code=status.HTTP_202_ACCEPTED)\n+def run_scan(lead_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, str]:\n+    try:\n+        scan = create_scan_job(db, lead_id)\n+    except LeadNotFoundError as exc:\n+        raise HTTPException(\n+            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=\"Lead not found\"\n+        ) from exc\n+    except ScanQueueError as exc:\n+        raise HTTPException(\n+            status_code=status.HTTP_503_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,\n+            detail=\"Scan queue is unavailable\",\n+        ) from exc\n+\n+    return {\"scan_id\": scan.id}\ndiff --git a/app/core/celery.py b/app/core/celery.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..683e011\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/app/core/celery.py\n@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@\n+from celery import Celery\n+\n+from app.core.config import settings\n+\n+celery_app = Celery(\n+    \"northaccessbfsg\",\n+    broker=settings.celery_broker_url,\n+    backend=settings.celery_result_backend,\n+    include=[\"app.workers.scan_worker\"],\n+)\n+\n+celery_app.conf.update(\n+    accept_content=[\"json\"],\n+    enable_utc=True,\n+    result_serializer=\"json\",\n+    task_serializer=\"json\",\n+    task_track_started=True,\n+    timezone=\"UTC\",\n+)\ndiff --git a/app/core/config.py b/app/core/config.py\nindex a1ab96f..4830fc4 100644\n--- a/app/core/config.py\n+++ b/app/core/config.py\n@@ -9,6 +9,8 @@ class Settings(BaseSettings):\n     database_url: str = (\n         \"postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@localhost:5432/northaccessbfsg\"\n     )\n+    celery_broker_url: str = \"redis://localhost:6379/0\"\n+    celery_result_backend: str = \"redis://localhost:6379/1\"\n \n     model_config = SettingsConfigDict(\n         env_file=\".env\",\ndiff --git a/app/db/init_db.py b/app/db/init_db.py\nindex 0420133..e159a5b 100644\n--- a/app/db/init_db.py\n+++ b/app/db/init_db.py\n@@ -1,7 +1,27 @@\n+from sqlalchemy import text\n+\n from app.db.base import Base\n from app.db.session import engine\n from app.models import Finding, Lead, Scan  # noqa: F401\n \n \n+_SCAN_SCHEMA_UPDATES = (\n+    \"ALTER TYPE scan_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'failed'\",\n+    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS started_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n+    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n+    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS failed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n+)\n+\n+\n def init_db() -> None:\n     Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n+    _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema()\n+\n+\n+def _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema() -> None:\n+    if engine.dialect.name != \"postgresql\":\n+        return\n+\n+    with engine.begin() as connection:\n+        for statement in _SCAN_SCHEMA_UPDATES:\n+            connection.execute(text(statement))\ndiff --git a/app/main.py b/app/main.py\nindex b854988..85d1c08 100644\n--- a/app/main.py\n+++ b/app/main.py\n@@ -1,9 +1,10 @@\n-from contextlib import asynccontextmanager\n from collections.abc import AsyncIterator\n+from contextlib import asynccontextmanager\n \n from fastapi import FastAPI\n \n from app.api.health import router as health_router\n+from app.api.scans import router as scans_router\n from app.core.config import settings\n from app.db.init_db import init_db\n \n@@ -21,3 +22,4 @@ async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:\n )\n \n app.include_router(health_router)\n+app.include_router(scans_router)\ndiff --git a/app/models/scan.py b/app/models/scan.py\nindex d978deb..4a0739f 100644\n--- a/app/models/scan.py\n+++ b/app/models/scan.py\n@@ -14,6 +14,7 @@ class ScanStatus(str, enum.Enum):\n     pending = \"pending\"\n     running = \"running\"\n     done = \"done\"\n+    failed = \"failed\"\n \n \n class Scan(Base):\n@@ -31,6 +32,15 @@ class Scan(Base):\n     created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n         DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n     )\n+    started_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n+        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n+    )\n+    completed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n+        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n+    )\n+    failed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n+        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n+    )\n \n     lead: Mapped[\"Lead\"] = relationship(\"Lead\", back_populates=\"scans\")\n     findings: Mapped[list[\"Finding\"]] = relationship(\ndiff --git a/app/services/__init__.py b/app/services/__init__.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..e69de29\ndiff --git a/app/services/scan_service.py b/app/services/scan_service.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..6f01580\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/app/services/scan_service.py\n@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@\n+from datetime import UTC, datetime\n+\n+from sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n+\n+from app.models import Lead, Scan, ScanStatus\n+from app.workers.scan_worker import run_scan_task\n+\n+\n+class LeadNotFoundError(Exception):\n+    pass\n+\n+\n+class ScanQueueError(Exception):\n+    pass\n+\n+\n+def create_scan_job(db: Session, lead_id: str) -> Scan:\n+    lead = db.get(Lead, lead_id)\n+    if lead is None:\n+        raise LeadNotFoundError(f\"Lead {lead_id} was not found\")\n+\n+    scan = Scan(lead_id=lead.id, status=ScanStatus.pending)\n+    db.add(scan)\n+    db.commit()\n+    db.refresh(scan)\n+\n+    try:\n+        run_scan_task.delay(scan.id)\n+    except Exception as exc:\n+        scan.status = ScanStatus.failed\n+        scan.failed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n+        db.commit()\n+        raise ScanQueueError(\"Could not enqueue scan task\") from exc\n+\n+    return scan\ndiff --git a/app/workers/__init__.py b/app/workers/__init__.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..e69de29\ndiff --git a/app/workers/scan_worker.py b/app/workers/scan_worker.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..871f9f9\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/app/workers/scan_worker.py\n@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@\n+import logging\n+import time\n+from datetime import UTC, datetime\n+\n+from app.core.celery import celery_app\n+from app.db.session import SessionLocal\n+from app.models import Scan, ScanStatus\n+\n+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n+\n+\n+@celery_app.task(name=\"app.workers.scan_worker.run_scan\")\n+def run_scan_task(scan_id: str) -> str:\n+    db = SessionLocal()\n+    try:\n+        scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n+        if scan is None:\n+            logger.warning(\"Scan %s was not found; skipping task\", scan_id)\n+            return scan_id\n+\n+        scan.status = ScanStatus.running\n+        scan.started_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n+        db.commit()\n+\n+        logger.info(\"Started placeholder accessibility scan %s\", scan_id)\n+        time.sleep(2)\n+\n+        scan.status = ScanStatus.done\n+        scan.completed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n+        db.commit()\n+\n+        logger.info(\"Completed placeholder accessibility scan %s\", scan_id)\n+        return scan_id\n+    except Exception:\n+        db.rollback()\n+        _mark_scan_failed(db, scan_id)\n+        logger.exception(\"Scan task %s failed\", scan_id)\n+        raise\n+    finally:\n+        db.close()\n+\n+\n+def _mark_scan_failed(db, scan_id: str) -> None:\n+    scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n+    if scan is None:\n+        return\n+\n+    scan.status = ScanStatus.failed\n+    scan.failed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n+    db.commit()\ndiff --git a/docker-compose.yml b/docker-compose.yml\nindex 6ca74c5..cfe7606 100644\n--- a/docker-compose.yml\n+++ b/docker-compose.yml\n@@ -4,11 +4,29 @@ services:\n     environment:\n       DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@db:5432/northaccessbfsg\n       ENVIRONMENT: development\n+      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\n+      CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND: redis://redis:6379/1\n     ports:\n       - \"8000:8000\"\n     depends_on:\n       db:\n         condition: service_healthy\n+      redis:\n+        condition: service_healthy\n+\n+  worker:\n+    build: .\n+    command: celery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info\n+    environment:\n+      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@db:5432/northaccessbfsg\n+      ENVIRONMENT: development\n+      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\n+      CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND: redis://redis:6379/1\n+    depends_on:\n+      db:\n+        condition: service_healthy\n+      redis:\n+        condition: service_healthy\n \n   db:\n     image: postgres:16-alpine\n@@ -26,5 +44,15 @@ services:\n       timeout: 5s\n       retries: 10\n \n+  redis:\n+    image: redis:7-alpine\n+    ports:\n+      - \"6379:6379\"\n+    healthcheck:\n+      test: [\"CMD\", \"redis-cli\", \"ping\"]\n+      interval: 5s\n+      timeout: 5s\n+      retries: 10\n+\n volumes:\n   postgres_data:\ndiff --git a/requirements.txt b/requirements.txt\nindex ef13747..f0d6732 100644\n--- a/requirements.txt\n+++ b/requirements.txt\n@@ -3,3 +3,4 @@ uvicorn[standard]==0.34.0\n SQLAlchemy==2.0.36\n psycopg2-binary==2.9.10\n pydantic-settings==2.7.1\n+celery[redis]==5.4.0\n"}
> ````

import Lead, Scan, ScanStatus\n+from app.workers.scan_worker import run_scan_task\n+\n+\n+class LeadNotFoundError(Exception):\n+    pass\n+\n+\n+class ScanQueueError(Exception):\n+    pass\n+\n+\n+def create_scan_job(db: Session, lead_id: str) -> Scan:\n+    lead = db.get(Lead, lead_id)\n+    if lead is None:\n+        raise LeadNotFoundError(f\"Lead {lead_id} was not found\")\n+\n+    scan = Scan(lead_id=lead.id, status=ScanStatus.pending)\n+    db.add(scan)\n+    db.commit()\n+    db.refresh(scan)\n+\n+    try:\n+        run_scan_task.delay(scan.id)\n+    except Exception as exc:\n+ logging.getLogger(__name__)\n+\n+\n+@celery_app.task(name=\"app.workers.scan_worker.run_scan\")\n+def run_scan_task(scan_id: str) -> str:\n+    db = SessionLocal()\n+    try:\n+        scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n+        if scan is None:\n+            logger.warning(\"Scan %s was not found; skipping task\", scan_id)\n+            return scan_id\n+\n+        scan.status = ScanStatus.running\n+        scan.started_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n+        db.commit()\n+\n+        logger.info(\"Started placeholder accessibility scan %s\", scan_id)\n+        time.sleep(2)\n+\n+ return scan_id\n+    except Exception:\n+        db.rollback()\n+        _mark_scan_failed(db, scan_id)\n+        logger.exception(\"Scan task %s failed\", scan_id)\n+        raise\n+    finally:\n+ Implemented in GitHub on branch `codex/celery-scan-worker` and opened Draft PR:

[PR #2: Add Celery Redis scan execution worker](https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/2)

**Changed Structure**
```text
app/
  api/scans.py
  core/celery.py
  services/scan_service.py
  workers/scan_worker.py
```

**Core Changes**
- Added Redis-backed Celery config in `app/core/celery.py`
- Added `POST /scans/run/{lead_id}` returning `{"scan_id": "..."}`
- Added scan orchestration service that creates a `pending` scan and queues Celery
- Added worker task that transitions `pending -> running -> done`
- Added `failed` status plus `started_at`, `completed_at`, `failed_at`
- Updated `docker-compose.yml` with `redis` and `worker` services

**Run**
```bash
docker-compose up --build
```

**Example Flow**
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/{lead_id}
```

Response:
```json
{"scan_id":"<scan-id>"}
```

Worker command works independently:
```bash
celery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info
```

I could not run Docker from this scratch environment, so the PR is left as draft with the implementation ready for review/testing.

> # Role
> You are a senior backend + automation engineer extending the NorthAccessBFSG system.
>
> # Goal
> Implement the Playwright-based accessibility audit engine that executes real browser scans for BFSG compliance analysis.
>
> This is the core scanning system that will later feed compliance, evidence, and reporting modules.
>
> # Context
> Existing system now includes:
> - FastAPI backend
> - PostgreSQL models (Lead, Scan, Finding)
> - Celery + Redis async scan execution (Task 2 completed)
>
> You MUST integrate into existing scan worker architecture.
>
> # Scope (STRICT)
>
> ## 1. Playwright integration
> Install and configure Playwright inside worker environment.
>
> Create:
> - app/workers/playwright_engine.py
>
> Must support:
> - Chromium (primary)
> - Firefox (secondary)
> - WebKit (optional)
>
> ## 2. Scan execution flow (REAL IMPLEMENTATION)
>
> Extend scan_worker.py:
>
> When scan runs:
>
> ### Step A: Browser session
> - launch browser
> - create new page
> - set viewport (desktop default)
>
> ### Step B: Navigation
> - page.goto(url from Lead domain)
>
> ### Step C: Accessibility scanning
> Integrate axe-core (or equivalent):
> - inject axe-core script
> - run accessibility scan
> - collect violations
>
> ### Step D: Evidence capture
> For each scan:
> - screenshot full page
> - extract DOM snapshot
> - extract accessibility tree
> - capture current URL
> - store timestamp
>
> ### Step E: Findings generation
> Convert axe violations into Finding objects:
> - rule_id
> - severity mapping
> - wcag references (if available)
> - confidence score (default heuristic allowed)
>
> ## 3. Data persistence
> Save:
> - Findings into DB
> - link to Scan ID
> - store evidence metadata (NOT raw binary yet, only placeholders allowed if storage not implemented)
>
> ## 4. Worker integration
> Modify Celery worker flow:
>
> Scan lifecycle must be:
> - pending → running → processing → done
>
> If error:
> - failed state with error message
>
> ## 5. Configuration
> Add Playwright config module:
> - app/core/browser_config.py
>
> Must define:
> - headless mode
> - timeout settings
> - retry strategy
>
> ## 6. Docker update requirement
> Ensure worker container installs:
> - playwright browsers
> - system dependencies
>
> Example:
> - playwright install chromium
>
> ## 7. Safety constraints
> - DO NOT implement full compliance engine yet
> - DO NOT implement reporting yet
> - DO NOT implement PDF generation yet
> - DO NOT implement CI/CD gates yet
>
> Only scanning + evidence extraction + raw findings
>
> # Success Criteria
>
> System is correct if:
>
> - Celery worker triggers Playwright scan
> - Browser launches successfully in Docker
> - Page loads target domain
> - axe-core returns violations
> - Findings are stored in PostgreSQL
> - Scan status transitions correctly
> - No blocking API behavior
>
> # Output Required
>
> 1. New/modified files
> 2. Playwright worker implementation
> 3. Axe-core integration logic
> 4. Docker adjustments (if needed)
> 5. Example scan execution flow
> 6. Debug notes for local testing
>
> # Success Definition
> A developer can run:
>
> docker-compose up
>
> then trigger:
>
> POST /scans/run/{lead_id}
>
> and observe:
> - browser opens (headless)
> - scan executes
> - findings stored
> - scan completes successfully

>
> Ich lese den aktuellen Worker-PR samt Modell-/Docker-Dateien, damit die Browser-Engine wirklich in die vorhandene Queue-Schicht greift und nicht parallel daran vorbei gebaut wird.

> {"content": "import logging\nimport time\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\n\nfrom app.core.celery import celery_app\nfrom app.db.session import SessionLocal\nfrom app.models import Scan, ScanStatus\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n@celery_app.task(name=\"app.workers.scan_worker.run_scan\")\ndef run_scan_task(scan_id: str) -> str:\n    db = SessionLocal()\n    try:\n        scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n        if scan is None:\n            logger.warning(\"Scan %s was not found; skipping task\", scan_id)\n            return scan_id\n\n        scan.status = ScanStatus.running\n        scan.started_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n        db.commit()\n\n        logger.info(\"Started placeholder accessibility scan %s\", scan_id)\n        time.sleep(2)\n\n        scan.status = ScanStatus.done\n        scan.completed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n        db.commit()\n\n        logger.info(\"Completed placeholder accessibility scan %s\", scan_id)\n        return scan_id\n    except Exception:\n        db.rollback()\n        _mark_scan_failed(db, scan_id)\n        logger.exception(\"Scan task %s failed\", scan_id)\n        raise\n    finally:\n        db.close()\n\n\ndef _mark_scan_failed(db, scan_id: str) -> None:\n    scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n    if scan is None:\n        return\n\n    scan.status = ScanStatus.failed\n    scan.failed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n    db.commit()\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "871f9f990a6882e0471783d42df91569c67362cb", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/celery-scan-worker/app/workers/scan_worker.py", "display_title": "scan_worker.py"}

> {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey, String, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass ScanStatus(str, enum.Enum):\n    pending = \"pending\"\n    running = \"running\"\n    done = \"done\"\n    failed = \"failed\"\n\n\nclass Scan(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"scans\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    lead_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"leads.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n    )\n    status: Mapped[ScanStatus] = mapped_column(\n        Enum(ScanStatus, name=\"scan_status\"), default=ScanStatus.pending, nullable=False\n    )\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n    started_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    completed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    failed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n\n    lead: Mapped[\"Lead\"] = relationship(\"Lead\", back_populates=\"scans\")\n    findings: Mapped[list[\"Finding\"]] = relationship(\n        \"Finding\", back_populates=\"scan\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "4a0739ffd837619578349fce5f4d5bed1565b59a", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/celery-scan-worker/app/models/scan.py", "display_title": "scan.py"}

> {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, String, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass Lead(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"leads\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    domain: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False, index=True)\n    company_name: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n\n    scans: Mapped[list[\"Scan\"]] = relationship(\n        \"Scan\", back_populates=\"lead\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "98fcb5437d3be5e415a7e72bd42ae752da2a1a04", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/celery-scan-worker/app/models/lead.py", "display_title": "lead.py"}
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "from sqlalchemy import text\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.db.session import engine\nfrom app.models import Finding, Lead, Scan  # noqa: F401\n\n\n_SCAN_SCHEMA_UPDATES = (\n    \"ALTER TYPE scan_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'failed'\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS started_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS failed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n)\n\n\ndef init_db() -> None:\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n    _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema()\n\n\ndef _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema() -> None:\n    if engine.dialect.name != \"postgresql\":\n        return\n\n    with engine.begin() as connection:\n        for statement in _SCAN_SCHEMA_UPDATES:\n            connection.execute(text(statement))\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "e159a5b903c642c49b910589d3714ac8d5a1c506", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/celery-scan-worker/app/db/init_db.py", "display_title": "init_db.py"}
> ```

> <details><summary>Searched web 2 times</summary>
>
> - Searched web for `PyPI axe-core python package playwright inject axe.min.js`
> - Searched web for `axe-playwright-python documentation import Axe Python Playwright analyze`
>
> </details>
>
> </details>
>
> Ich nutze `axe-playwright-python` als Python-Integration für axe-core statt eine eigene JS-Bundle-Kopie ins Repo zu werfen. Die offiziellen/package docs zeigen genau diesen Sync-Playwright-Pfad, passend für Celery-Tasks.
>
> <details><summary>Searched web 1 time</summary>
>
> <details><summary>Searched web 1 time</summary>
>
> - Searched web for `PyPI playwright latest version Python May 2026`
>
> </details>
>
> </details>
>
> Ich passe noch den Queue-Aufruf an: Die API wird Tasks per Celery-Taskname senden, statt das Worker-Modul direkt zu importieren. Dadurch bleibt der API-Start leichtgewichtig, auch wenn der Worker später Browser-Binaries und Playwright lädt.
>

> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/playwright-audit-engine",
>   "path": "app/core/browser_config.py",
>   "message": "Add browser scan configuration",
>   "content": "from dataclasses import dataclass\n\nfrom app.core.config import settings\n\nSUPPORTED_BROWSERS = {\"chromium\", \"firefox\", \"webkit\"}\nSUPPORTED_WAIT_UNTIL = {\"commit\", \"domcontentloaded\", \"load\", \"networkidle\"}\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass BrowserConfig:\n    browser_name: str\n    headless: bool\n    navigation_timeout_ms: int\n    action_timeout_ms: int\n    viewport_width: int\n    viewport_height: int\n    retries: int\n    wait_until: str\n\n\ndef get_browser_config() -> BrowserConfig:\n    browser_name = settings.browser_name.lower().strip()\n    if browser_name not in SUPPORTED_BROWSERS:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Unsupported browser '{settings.browser_name}'\")\n\n    wait_until = settings.browser_wait_until.lower().strip()\n    if wait_until not in SUPPORTED_WAIT_UNTIL:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Unsupported wait strategy '{settings.browser_wait_until}'\")\n\n    return BrowserConfig(\n        browser_name=browser_name,\n        headless=settings.browser_headless,\n        navigation_timeout_ms=settings.browser_navigation_timeout_ms,\n        action_timeout_ms=settings.browser_action_timeout_ms,\n        viewport_width=settings.browser_viewport_width,\n        viewport_height=settings.browser_viewport_height,\n        retries=max(settings.browser_retries, 0),\n        wait_until=wait_until,\n    )\n"
> }
> ```

>
> Die Engine selbst sammelt echte Browser-Evidence, speichert aber nur Metadaten: Hashes, Größen, Counts, URL und Zeitstempel. Roh-Screenshots/DOM werden bewusst noch nicht persistiert, weil Storage/Reporting laut Scope noch nicht dran sind.
>
> <details><summary>Called 12 tools</summary>
>
> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_create_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/playwright-audit-engine",
>   "path": "app/workers/playwright_engine.py",
>   "message": "Add Playwright axe scan engine",
>   "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport json\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\nfrom typing import Any\nfrom urllib.parse import urlparse\n\nfrom axe_playwright_python.sync_playwright import Axe\nfrom playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright\n\nfrom app.core.browser_config import BrowserConfig, get_browser_config\n\n_SEVERITY_BY_AXE_IMPACT = {\n    \"critical\": \"critical\",\n    \"serious\": \"high\",\n    \"moderate\": \"medium\",\n    \"minor\": \"low\",\n}\n_CONFIDENCE_BY_AXE_IMPACT = {\n    \"critical\": 0.95,\n    \"serious\": 0.9,\n    \"moderate\": 0.8,\n    \"minor\": 0.65,\n}\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass EngineFinding:\n    rule_id: str\n    severity: str\n    wcag_refs: list[str]\n    confidence_score: float\n    description: str | None\n    help_url: str | None\n    evidence_metadata: dict[str, Any]\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass PlaywrightScanResult:\n    target_url: str\n    current_url: str\n    captured_at: str\n    evidence_metadata: dict[str, Any]\n    findings: list[EngineFinding]\n\n\ndef run_accessibility_scan(domain: str) -> PlaywrightScanResult:\n    config = get_browser_config()\n    target_url = normalize_target_url(domain)\n    last_error: Exception | None = None\n\n    for attempt in range(config.retries + 1):\n        try:\n            return _run_once(target_url=target_url, config=config, attempt=attempt)\n        except Exception as exc:\n            last_error = exc\n            if attempt < config.retries:\n                time.sleep(min(2**attempt, 5))\n\n    raise RuntimeError(f\"Playwright scan failed for {target_url}\") from last_error\n\n\ndef normalize_target_url(domain: str) -> str:\n    candidate = domain.strip()\n    parsed = urlparse(candidate)\n    if parsed.scheme:\n        return candidate\n    return f\"https://{candidate}\"\n\n\ndef _run_once(\n    target_url: str, config: BrowserConfig, attempt: int\n) -> PlaywrightScanResult:\n    captured_at = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()\n\n    with sync_playwright() as playwright:\n        browser_type = getattr(playwright, config.browser_name)\n        launch_args = [\"--no-sandbox\"] if config.browser_name == \"chromium\" else []\n        browser = browser_type.launch(headless=config.headless, args=launch_args)\n        try:\n            context = browser.new_context(\n                viewport={\n                    \"width\": config.viewport_width,\n                    \"height\": config.viewport_height,\n                }\n            )\n            context.set_default_timeout(config.action_timeout_ms)\n            context.set_default_navigation_timeout(config.navigation_timeout_ms)\n\n            page = context.new_page()\n            page.goto(\n                target_url,\n                wait_until=config.wait_until,\n                timeout=config.navigation_timeout_ms,\n            )\n\n            axe_response = Axe().run(page).response\n            dom_snapshot = page.content()\n            screenshot_bytes = page.screenshot(full_page=True)\n            accessibility_tree = _capture_accessibility_tree(page)\n            current_url = page.url\n            evidence_metadata = _build_scan_evidence_metadata(\n                accessibility_tree=accessibility_tree,\n                axe_response=axe_response,\n                captured_at=captured_at,\n                config=config,\n                current_url=current_url,\n                dom_snapshot=dom_snapshot,\n                screenshot_bytes=screenshot_bytes,\n                target_url=target_url,\n                attempt=attempt,\n            )\n            findings = _findings_from_axe_response(axe_response, evidence_metadata)\n\n            return PlaywrightScanResult(\n                target_url=target_url,\n                current_url=current_url,\n                captured_at=captured_at,\n                evidence_metadata=evidence_metadata,\n                findings=findings,\n            )\n        finally:\n            browser.close()\n\n\ndef _capture_accessibility_tree(page) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n    try:\n        return page.accessibility.snapshot(interesting_only=False)\n    except Exception:\n        return None\n\n\ndef _build_scan_evidence_metadata(\n    *,\n    accessibility_tree: dict[str, Any] | None,\n    axe_response: dict[str, Any],\n    captured_at: str,\n    config: BrowserConfig,\n    current_url: str,\n    dom_snapshot: str,\n    screenshot_bytes: bytes,\n    target_url: str,\n    attempt: int,\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    accessibility_tree_json = _json_dump(accessibility_tree) if accessibility_tree else None\n\n    return {\n        \"target_url\": target_url,\n        \"current_url\": current_url,\n        \"captured_at\": captured_at,\n        \"browser\": {\n            \"name\": config.browser_name,\n            \"headless\": config.headless,\n            \"viewport\": {\n                \"width\": config.viewport_width,\n                \"height\": config.viewport_height,\n            },\n            \"navigation_timeout_ms\": config.navigation_timeout_ms,\n            \"attempt\": attempt + 1,\n        },\n        \"axe\": {\n            \"violations_count\": len(axe_response.get(\"violations\", [])),\n            \"passes_count\": len(axe_response.get(\"passes\", [])),\n            \"incomplete_count\": len(axe_response.get(\"incomplete\", [])),\n            \"inapplicable_count\": len(axe_response.get(\"inapplicable\", [])),\n        },\n        \"screenshot\": {\n            \"storage\": \"pending\",\n            \"full_page\": True,\n            \"byte_count\": len(screenshot_bytes),\n            \"sha256\": hashlib.sha256(screenshot_bytes).hexdigest(),\n        },\n        \"dom_snapshot\": {\n            \"storage\": \"pending\",\n            \"char_count\": len(dom_snapshot),\n            \"sha256\": _sha256_text(dom_snapshot),\n        },\n        \"accessibility_tree\": {\n            \"storage\": \"pending\",\n            \"available\": accessibility_tree is not None,\n            \"node_count\": _count_accessibility_nodes(accessibility_tree),\n            \"sha256\": _sha256_text(accessibility_tree_json)\n            if accessibility_tree_json\n            else None,\n        },\n    }\n\n\ndef _findings_from_axe_response(\n    axe_response: dict[str, Any], scan_evidence: dict[str, Any]\n) -> list[EngineFinding]:\n    findings: list[EngineFinding] = []\n    for violation in axe_response.get(\"violations\", []):\n        impact = violation.get(\"impact\") or \"unknown\"\n        nodes = violation.get(\"nodes\") or []\n        wcag_refs = _extract_wcag_refs(violation.get(\"tags\") or [])\n        confidence_score = _confidence_for_violation(impact=impact, node_count=len(nodes))\n\n        findings.append(\n            EngineFinding(\n                rule_id=violation.get(\"id\", \"unknown\"),\n                severity=_SEVERITY_BY_AXE_IMPACT.get(impact, \"info\"),\n                wcag_refs=wcag_refs,\n                confidence_score=confidence_score,\n                description=violation.get(\"description\") or violation.get(\"help\"),\n                help_url=violation.get(\"helpUrl\"),\n                evidence_metadata={\n                    **scan_evidence,\n                    \"axe_violation\": {\n                        \"impact\": impact,\n                        \"help\": violation.get(\"help\"),\n                        \"help_url\": violation.get(\"helpUrl\"),\n                        \"node_count\": len(nodes),\n                        \"sample_targets\": _sample_node_targets(nodes),\n                    },\n                },\n            )\n        )\n\n    return findings\n\n\ndef _extract_wcag_refs(tags: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    return sorted({tag for tag in tags if tag.lower().startswith(\"wcag\")})\n\n\ndef _confidence_for_violation(*, impact: str, node_count: int) -> float:\n    base = _CONFIDENCE_BY_AXE_IMPACT.get(impact, 0.5)\n    if node_count >= 3:\n        base += 0.03\n    return min(base, 0.99)\n\n\ndef _sample_node_targets(nodes: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[list[str]]:\n    targets: list[list[str]] = []\n    for node in nodes[:5]:\n        target = node.get(\"target\")\n        if isinstance(target, list):\n            targets.append([str(item) for item in target])\n    return targets\n\n\ndef _count_accessibility_nodes(node: dict[str, Any] | None) -> int:\n    if not node:\n        return 0\n    children = node.get(\"children\") or []\n    return 1 + sum(_count_accessibility_nodes(child) for child in children)\n\n\ndef _json_dump(value: Any) -> str:\n    return json.dumps(value, default=str, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)\n\n\ndef _sha256_text(value: str) -> str:\n    return hashlib.sha256(value.encode(\"utf-8\")).hexdigest()\n"
> }
> ```

> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/playwright-audit-engine",
>   "path": "app/models/scan.py",
>   "sha": "4a0739ffd837619578349fce5f4d5bed1565b59a",
>   "message": "Add processing status and scan error details",
>   "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey, String, Text, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass ScanStatus(str, enum.Enum):\n    pending = \"pending\"\n    running = \"running\"\n    processing = \"processing\"\n    done = \"done\"\n    failed = \"failed\"\n\n\nclass Scan(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"scans\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    lead_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"leads.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n    )\n    status: Mapped[ScanStatus] = mapped_column(\n        Enum(ScanStatus, name=\"scan_status\"), default=ScanStatus.pending, nullable=False\n    )\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n    started_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    completed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    failed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    error_message: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\n\n    lead: Mapped[\"Lead\"] = relationship(\"Lead\", back_populates=\"scans\")\n    findings: Mapped[list[\"Finding\"]] = relationship(\n        \"Finding\", back_populates=\"scan\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n"
> }
> ```
