Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n+\n+Inspect enriched compliance findings:\n+\n+```bash\n+docker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, normalized_severity, wcag_refs, en_refs, bfsg_refs, compliance_confidence_score FROM compliance_findings;\"\n+```\n+\n The worker process can also be started independently:\n \n ```bash\ndiff --git a/app/api/compliance.py b/app/api/compliance.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..98d279e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/app/api/compliance.py\n@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@\n+from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, status\n+from sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n+\n+from app.db.session import get_db\n+from app.services.compliance_service import ScanNotFoundError, run_compliance_mapping\n+\n+router = APIRouter(prefix=\"/compliance\", tags=[\"compliance\"])\n+\n+\n+@router.post(\"/run/{scan_id}\")\n+def run_compliance(scan_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, int | float | str]:\n+    try:\n+        summary = run_compliance_mapping(db, scan_id)\n+    except ScanNotFoundError as exc:\n+        raise HTTPException(\n+            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=\"Scan not found\"\n+        ) from exc\n+\n+    return summary.as_dict()\ndiff --git a/app/compliance/__init__.py b/app/compliance/__init__.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..e69de29\ndiff --git a/app/compliance/rule_engine.py b/app/compliance/rule_engine.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..0017e4f\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/app/compliance/rule_engine.py\n@@ -0,0 +1,167 @@\n+from __future__ import annotations\n+\n+import hashlib\n+import json\n+import re\n+from dataclasses import dataclass\n+from functools import lru_cache\n+from pathlib import Path\n+from typing import Any\n+\n+from app.models import Finding\n+\n+_RULES_PATH = Path(__file__).parent / \"rules\" / \"wcag_en_bfsg_map.json\"\n+_WCAG_DOTTED_RE = re.compile(r\"\\b([1-4])\\.(\\d+)\\.(\\d+)\\b\")\n+_WCAG_TAG_RE = re.compile(r\"^wcag([1-4])(\\d)(\\d+)$\", re.IGNORECASE)\n+_DEFAULT_SEVERITY_MAPPING = {\n+    \"critical\": \"critical\",\n+    \"serious\": \"high\",\n+    \"high\": \"high\",\n+    \"moderate\": \"medium\",\n+    \"medium\": \"medium\",\n+    \"minor\": \"low\",\n+    \"low\": \"low\",\n+    \"info\": \"info\",\n+    \"unknown\": \"info\",\n+}\n+_BFSG_CATEGORY_BY_WCAG_PRINCIPLE = {\n+    \"1\": \"perceivable_content_and_information\",\n+    \"2\": \"operable_interface_and_navigation\",\n+    \"3\": \"understandable_information_and_inputs\",\n+    \"4\": \"robust_assistive_technology_compatibility\",\n+}\n+\n+\n+@dataclass(frozen=True)\n+class ComplianceMapping:\n+    rule_id: str\n+    wcag_refs: list[str]\n+    en_refs: list[str]\n+    bfsg_refs: list[str]\n+    bfsg_category: str\n+    normalized_severity: str\n+    compliance_confidence_score: float\n+    mapping_version: str\n+    mapping_metadata: dict[str, Any]\n+\n+\n+class ComplianceRuleEngine:\n+    def __init__(self, rules_path: Path = _RULES_PATH) -> None:\n+        self.rules_path = rules_path\n+        self._raw_rules_text = rules_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n+        self._rules_hash = hashlib.sha256(self._raw_rules_text.encode(\"utf-8\")).hexdigest()\n+        self.dataset = json.loads(self._raw_rules_text)\n+        self.rules_by_id = {\n+            rule[\"rule_id\"]: rule for rule in self.dataset.get(\"rules\", [])\n+        }\n+        self.severity_rules = self.dataset.get(\"severity_normalization_rules\", {})\n+        self.mapping_version = f\"{self.dataset['version']}:{self._rules_hash[:12]}\"\n+\n+    def enrich_finding(self, finding: Finding) -> ComplianceMapping:\n+        rule = self.rules_by_id.get(finding.rule_id)\n+        raw_wcag_refs = _normalize_wcag_refs(finding.wcag_refs or [])\n+\n+        if rule is not None:\n+            wcag_refs = _normalize_wcag_refs(rule.get(\"wcag_references\", [])) or raw_wcag_refs\n+            en_refs = sorted(set(rule.get(\"en_301_549_references\", [])))\n+            bfsg_category = rule.get(\"bfsg_category\") or _derive_bfsg_category(wcag_refs)\n+            normalized_severity = self._normalize_severity(\n+                finding.severity, rule.get(\"severity_mapping\", {})\n+            )\n+            source = \"rule_dataset\"\n+            base_confidence = 0.95\n+        else:\n+            wcag_refs = raw_wcag_refs\n+            en_refs = [_wcag_to_en_301_549_ref(ref) for ref in wcag_refs]\n+            bfsg_category = _derive_bfsg_category(wcag_refs)\n+            normalized_severity = self._normalize_severity(finding.severity, {})\n+            source = \"wcag_fallback\"\n+            base_confidence = 0.7 if wcag_refs else 0.4\n+\n+        en_refs = sorted({ref for ref in en_refs if ref})\n+        bfsg_refs = [f\"BFSG:{bfsg_category}\"] if bfsg_category else []\n+        raw_confidence = finding.confidence_score if finding.confidence_score is not None else 0.75\n+        compliance_confidence_score = round(\n+            min(0.99, (raw_confidence * 0.7) + (base_confidence * 0.3)), 4\n+        )\n+\n+        return ComplianceMapping(\n+            rule_id=finding.rule_id,\n+            wcag_refs=wcag_refs,\n+            en_refs=en_refs,\n+            bfsg_refs=bfsg_refs,\n+            bfsg_category=bfsg_category,\n+            normalized_severity=normalized_severity,\n+            compliance_confidence_score=compliance_confidence_score,\n+            mapping_version=self.mapping_version,\n+            mapping_metadata={\n+                \"source\": source,\n+                \"rules_file\": str(self.rules_path.name),\n+                \"rules_version\": self.dataset[\"version\"],\n+                \"rules_sha256\": self._rules_hash,\n+                \"raw_rule_id\": finding.rule_id,\n+                \"raw_severity\": finding.severity,\n+                \"raw_wcag_refs\": raw_wcag_refs,\n+                \"standards\": self.dataset.get(\"standard_versions\", {}),\n+            },\n+        )\n+\n+    def _normalize_severity(\n+        self, raw_severity: str | None, severity_mapping: dict[str, Any]\n+    ) -> str:\n+        value = (raw_severity or \"unknown\").lower().strip()\n+        strategy = severity_mapping.get(\"strategy\")\n+        if strategy:\n+            strategy_mapping = self.severity_rules.get(strategy, {})\n+            normalized = strategy_mapping.get(value)\n+            if normalized:\n+                return normalized\n+\n+        normalized = _DEFAULT_SEVERITY_MAPPING.get(value)\n+        if normalized:\n+            return normalized\n+\n+        return severity_mapping.get(\"default\", \"info\")\n+\n+\n+def _normalize_wcag_refs(values: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n+    refs: set[str] = set()\n+    for value in values:\n+        item = str(value).strip()\n+        dotted_match = _WCAG_DOTTED_RE.search(item)\n+        if dotted_match:\n+            refs.add(\".\".join(dotted_match.groups()))\n+            continue\n+\n+        tag_match = _WCAG_TAG_RE.match(item)\n+        if tag_match:\n+            refs.add(\".\".join(tag_match.groups()))\n+\n+    return sorted(refs, key=_wcag_sort_key)\n+\n+\n+def _wcag_to_en_301_549_ref(wcag_ref: str) -> str:\n+    parts = wcag_ref.split(\".\")\n+    if len(parts) != 3 or parts[0] not in _BFSG_CATEGORY_BY_WCAG_PRINCIPLE:\n+        return \"\"\n+    return f\"9.{parts[0]}.{parts[1]}.{parts[2]}\"\n+\n+\n+def _derive_bfsg_category(wcag_refs: list[str]) -> str:\n+    if not wcag_refs:\n+        return \"unmapped_accessibility_risk\"\n+\n+    principle = wcag_refs[0].split(\".\", 1)[0]\n+    return _BFSG_CATEGORY_BY_WCAG_PRINCIPLE.get(\n+        principle, \"unmapped_accessibility_risk\"\n+    )\n+\n+\n+def _wcag_sort_key(wcag_ref: str) -> tuple[int, int, int]:\n+    parts = wcag_ref.split(\".\")\n+    return tuple(int(part) for part in parts)  # type: ignore[return-value]\n+\n+\n+@lru_cache\n+def get_rule_engine() -> ComplianceRuleEngine:\n+    return ComplianceRuleEngine()\ndiff --git a/app/compliance/rules/__init__.py b/app/compliance/rules/__init__.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..e69de29\ndiff --git b/app/models/compliance_finding.py\n@@ -0,0 +1,54 @@\n+from __future__ import annotations\n+\n+import uuid\n+from datetime import datetime\n+from typing import Any\n+\n+from sqlalchemy import JSON, DateTime, Float, ForeignKey, String, UniqueConstraint, func\n+from sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n+\n+from app.db.base import Base\n+\n+\n+class ComplianceFinding(Base):\n+    __tablename__ = \"compliance_findings\"\n+    __table_args__ = (\n+        UniqueConstraint(\n+            \"finding_id\",\n+            \"mapping_version\",\n+            name=\"uq_compliance_findings_finding_mapping_version\",\n+        ),\n+    )\n+\n+    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n+        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n+    )\n+    scan_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n+        String(36), ForeignKey(\"scans.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n+    )\n+    finding_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n+        String(36), ForeignKey(\"findings.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n+    )\n+    rule_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100), nullable=False, index=True)\n+    mapping_version: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(80), nullable=False, index=True)\n+    wcag_refs: Mapped[list[str]] = mapped_column(JSON, default=list, nullable=False)\n+    en_refs: Mapped[list[str]] = mapped_column(JSON, default=list, nullable=False)\n+    bfsg_refs: Mapped[list[str]] = mapped_column(JSON, default=list, nullable=False)\n+    bfsg_category: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(120), nullable=False, index=True)\n+    normalized_severity: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(50), nullable=False, index=True)\n+    compliance_confidence_score: Mapped[float] = mapped_column(Float, nullable=False)\n+    mapping_metadata: Mapped[dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(\n+        JSON, default=dict, nullable=False\n+    )\n+    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n+        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n+    )\n+    updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n+        DateTime(timezone=True),\n+        server_default=func.now(),\n+        onupdate=func.now(),\n+        nullable=False,\n+    )\n+\n+    scan: Mapped[\"Scan\"] = relationship(\"Scan\")\n+    finding: Mapped[\"Finding\"] = relationship(\"Finding\")\ndiff --git a/app/services/compliance_service.py b/app/services/compliance_service.py\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..5f80c7e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/app/services/compliance_service.py\n@@ -0,0 +1,136 @@\n+from __future__ import annotations\n+\n+from dataclasses import dataclass\n+\n+from sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n+\n+from app.compliance.rule_engine import ComplianceMapping, get_rule_engine\n+from app.models import ComplianceFinding, Finding, Scan\n+\n+\n+class ScanNotFoundError(Exception):\n+    pass\n+\n+\n+@dataclass(frozen=True)\n+class ComplianceRunSummary:\n+    scan_id: str\n+    mapping_version: str\n+    total_findings: int\n+    mapped_findings: int\n+    critical_count: int\n+    high_count: int\n+    compliance_coverage_score: float\n+\n+    def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, int | float | str]:\n+        return {\n+            \"scan_id\": self.scan_id,\n+            \"mapping_version\": self.mapping_version,\n+            \"total_findings\": self.total_findings,\n+            \"mapped_findings\": self.mapped_findings,\n+            \"critical_count\": self.critical_count,\n+            \"high_count\": self.high_count,\n+            \"compliance_coverage_score\": self.compliance_coverage_score,\n+        }\n+\n+\n+def run_compliance_mapping(db: Session, scan_id: str) -> ComplianceRunSummary:\n+    scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n+    if scan is None:\n+        raise ScanNotFoundError(f\"Scan {scan_id} was not found\")\n+\n+    raw_findings = (\n+        db.query(Finding)\n+        .filter(Finding.scan_id == scan_id)\n+        .order_by(Finding.id.asc())\n+        .all()\n+    )\n+    rule_engine = get_rule_engine()\n+    mapping_version = rule_engine.mapping_version\n+    enriched_findings: list[ComplianceFinding] = []\n+    raw_finding_ids = [finding.id for finding in raw_findings]\n+\n+    for raw_finding in raw_findings:\n+        mapping = rule_engine.enrich_finding(raw_finding)\n+        enriched_findings.append(\n+            _upsert_compliance_finding(\n+                db,\n+                raw_finding=raw_finding,\n+                mapping=mapping,\n+            )\n+        )\n+\n+    stale_query = db.query(ComplianceFinding).filter(\n+        ComplianceFinding.scan_id == scan_id,\n+        ComplianceFinding.mapping_version == mapping_version,\n+    )\n+    if raw_finding_ids:\n+        stale_query.filter(~ComplianceFinding.finding_id.in_(raw_finding_ids)).delete(\n+            synchronize_session=False\n+        )\n+    else:\n+        stale_query.delete(synchronize_session=False)\n+\n+    db.commit()\n+\n+    critical_count = sum(\n+        1 for finding in enriched_findings if finding.normalized_severity == \"critical\"\n+    )\n+    high_count = sum(\n+        1 for finding in enriched_findings if finding.normalized_severity == \"high\"\n+    )\n+    mapped_findings = sum(\n+        1\n+        for finding in enriched_findings\n+        if finding.wcag_refs and finding.en_refs and finding.bfsg_refs\n+    )\n+    total_findings = len(raw_findings)\n+    compliance_coverage_score = (\n+        1.0 if total_findings == 0 else round(mapped_findings / total_findings, 4)\n+    )\n+\n+    return ComplianceRunSummary(\n+        scan_id=scan_id,\n+        mapping_version=mapping_version,\n+        total_findings=total_findings,\n+        mapped_findings=mapped_findings,\n+        critical_count=critical_count,\n+        high_count=high_count,\n+        compliance_coverage_score=compliance_coverage_score,\n+    )\n+\n+\n+def _upsert_compliance_finding(\n+    db: Session, *, raw_finding: Finding, mapping: ComplianceMapping\n+) -> ComplianceFinding:\n+    compliance_finding = (\n+        db.query(ComplianceFinding)\n+        .filter(\n+            ComplianceFinding.finding_id == raw_finding.id,\n+            ComplianceFinding.mapping_version == mapping.mapping_version,\n+        )\n+        .one_or_none()\n+    )\n+\n+    if compliance_finding is None:\n+        compliance_finding = ComplianceFinding(\n+            scan_id=raw_finding.scan_id,\n+            finding_id=raw_finding.id,\n+            rule_id=raw_finding.rule_id,\n+            mapping_version=mapping.mapping_version,\n+        )\n+        db.add(compliance_finding)\n+\n+    compliance_finding.scan_id = raw_finding.scan_id\n+    compliance_finding.rule_id = raw_finding.rule_id\n+    compliance_finding.wcag_refs = mapping.wcag_refs\n+    compliance_finding.en_refs = mapping.en_refs\n+    compliance_finding.bfsg_refs = mapping.bfsg_refs\n+    compliance_finding.bfsg_category = mapping.bfsg_category\n+    compliance_finding.normalized_severity = mapping.normalized_severity\n+    compliance_finding.compliance_confidence_score = (\n+        mapping.compliance_confidence_score\n+    )\n+    compliance_finding.mapping_metadata = mapping.mapping_metadata\n+\n+    return compliance_finding\n"
> }

Implemented as a new dependent Draft PR:

[PR #4: Add deterministic compliance mapping layer](https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/4)

Merge order is now: PR #2 -> PR #3 -> PR #4.

Added:
```text
app/api/compliance.py
app/compliance/rule_engine.py
app/compliance/rules/wcag_en_bfsg_map.json
app/models/compliance_finding.py
app/services/compliance_service.py
```

What it does:
- Maps raw axe `rule_id` and WCAG tags into WCAG, EN 301 549 clause refs, and BFSG relevance categories.
- Stores enriched results separately in `compliance_findings`.
- Keeps raw `findings` untouched.
- Uses deterministic `mapping_version = rules_version + rules_file_sha256`.
- Supports idempotent reruns via `finding_id + mapping_version`.
- Adds `POST /compliance/run/{scan_id}`.

Example:
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}
```

Returns:
```json
{
  "scan_id": "<scan-id>",
  "mapping_version": "2026.05.08-001:<rules-hash>",
  "total_findings": 3,
  "mapped_findings": 3,
  "critical_count": 0,
  "high_count": 2,
  "compliance_coverage_score": 1.0
}
```

I did not modify Playwright or Celery execution. I could not run Docker locally in this scratch environment, so validation is diff/code-review based. The EN 301 549 web clause mapping follows the ETSI clause 9/WCAG structure: [ETSI EN 301 549 V3.2.1](https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_en/301500_301599/301549/03.02.01_60/en_301549v030201p.pdf).

> push live everything

configuration in `app/core/browser_config.py`\n- Add `app/workers/playwright_engine.py` using headless Chromium/Firefox-capable Playwright and axe-core via `axe-playwright-python`\n- Replace placeholder worker sleep with real browser navigation, axe scan execution, evidence metadata capture, and finding persistence\n- Extend scan lifecycle to `pending -> running -> processing -> done`, with `failed` and `error_message` on errors\n- Add scan/finding evidence metadata fields, WCAG refs, confidence score, description, and help URL persistence\n- Update Docker image to install Playwright Chromium + Firefox browsers and configure worker shared memory\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nWorker result:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nInspect evidence and findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT id, status, evidence_metadata->>'current_url' AS current_url FROM scans;\"\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, severity, wcag_refs, confidence_score FROM findings;\"\n```\n\n## Notes\n\n- Stores metadata placeholders only for screenshot/DOM/accessibility tree evidence: hash, size/count, URL, timestamp, browser info.\n- Does not implement compliance mapping, reporting, PDF generation, Playwright-driven crawling, or CI/CD gates.\n- I could not run Docker in this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.\n- Depends on PR #2 (`codex/celery-scan-worker`).", "title": "Add Playwright axe accessibility scan engine", "base": "codex/celery-scan-worker", "base_sha": "c677973c900ebab45a812cc257d2794749931a4d", "head": "codex/playwright-audit-engine", "head_sha": "a36915ea9a94c9671fd77feb1031bdbbe38279f2", "merge_commit_sha": "cc129a0f6eab1a1fef23c489639e0c2bcdbf6d8b", "user": {"login": "DopeChicka", "name": "DopeChicka", "email": null, "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25302183?v=4", "id": 25302183}, "requested_reviewers": null, "requested_team_reviewers": null, "diff": null, "comments": null, "created_at": "2026-05-08T11:36:14Z", "updated_at": "2026-05-08T11:36:14Z", "closed_at": null, "merged_at": null, "commits": 18, "changed_files": 14, "additions": 483, "deletions": 23, "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/3", "display_title": "Add Playwright axe accessibility scan engine"}
> ````

> ````json
> {
>   "url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/3",
>   "number": 3,
>   "state": "open",
>   "merged": false,
>   "mergeable": true,
>   "draft": true,
>   "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add Playwright browser scan configuration in `app/core/browser_config.py`\n- Add `app/workers/playwright_engine.py` using headless Chromium/Firefox-capable Playwright and axe-core via `axe-playwright-python`\n- Replace placeholder worker sleep with real browser navigation, axe scan execution, evidence metadata capture, and finding persistence\n- Extend scan lifecycle to `pending -> running -> processing -> done`, with `failed` and `error_message` on errors\n- Add scan/finding evidence metadata fields, WCAG refs, confidence score, description, and help URL persistence\n- Update Docker image to install Playwright Chromium + Firefox browsers and configure worker shared memory\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nWorker result:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nInspect evidence and findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT id, status, evidence_metadata->>'current_url' AS current_url FROM scans;\"\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, severity, wcag_refs, confidence_score FROM findings;\"\n```\n\n## Notes\n\n- Stores metadata placeholders only for screenshot/DOM/accessibility tree evidence: hash, size/count, URL, timestamp, browser info.\n- Does not implement compliance mapping, reporting, PDF generation, Playwright-driven crawling, or CI/CD gates.\n- I could not run Docker in this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.\n- Depends on PR #2 (`codex/celery-scan-worker`).",
>   "title": "Add Playwright axe accessibility scan engine",
>   "base": "codex/celery-scan-worker",

> ````text
> {"url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/4", "number": 4, "state": "open", "merged": false, "mergeable": true, "draft": true, "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add versioned compliance rule dataset at `app/compliance/rules/wcag_en_bfsg_map.json`\n- Add deterministic rule engine that maps raw axe rule IDs and WCAG tags to WCAG, EN 301 549, and BFSG relevance categories\n- Add `ComplianceFinding` model so raw `findings` are never overwritten\n- Add compliance service with idempotent upsert by `finding_id` + `mapping_version`\n- Add `POST /compliance/run/{scan_id}` endpoint returning total findings, critical/high counts, and compliance coverage score\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"scan_id\": \"<scan-id>\",\n  \"mapping_version\": \"2026.05.08-001:<rules-hash>\",\n  \"total_findings\": 3,\n  \"mapped_findings\": 3,\n  \"critical_count\": 0,\n  \"high_count\": 2,\n  \"compliance_coverage_score\": 1.0\n}\n```\n\nInspect persisted enriched findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, normalized_severity, wcag_refs, en_refs, bfsg_refs, compliance_confidence_score FROM compliance_findings;\"\n```\n\n## Integrity and determinism\n\n- Raw `findings` rows are not modified.\n- Enriched compliance rows are stored in `compliance_findings`.\n- Mapping version is derived from the rules file version plus rules file SHA-256 hash.\n- Re-running the endpoint updates existing compliance rows for the same `finding_id` and `mapping_version` and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Does not implement PDF/JSON/EARL reporting.\n- Does not modify Playwright engine or Celery worker execution.\n- I could not run Docker in this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.\n- Depends on PR #3 (`codex/playwright-audit-engine`).", "title": "Add deterministic compliance mapping layer", "base": "codex/playwright-audit-engine", "base_sha": "a36915ea9a94c9671fd77feb1031bdbbe38279f2", "head": "codex/compliance-mapping", "head_sha": "8a71c44120b138b9714a94e7241da9669480b604", "merge_commit_sha": "7259991834589fe93ab6f66f06e0a0bec3583ba4", "user": {"login": "DopeChicka", "name": "DopeChicka", "email": null, "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25302183?v=4", "id": 25302183}, "requested_reviewers": null, "requested_team_reviewers": null, "diff": null, "comments": null, "created_at": "2026-05-08T11:45:30Z", "updated_at": "2026-05-08T11:45:30Z", "closed_at": null, "merged_at": null, "commits": 11, "changed_files": 11, "additions": 717, "deletions": 3, "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/4", "display_title": "Add deterministic compliance mapping layer"}
> ````

> ````json
> {
>   "url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/4",
>   "number": 4,
>   "state": "open",
>   "merged": false,
>   "mergeable": true,
>   "draft": true,
>   "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add versioned compliance rule dataset at `app/compliance/rules/wcag_en_bfsg_map.json`\n- Add deterministic rule engine that maps raw axe rule IDs and WCAG tags to WCAG, EN 301 549, and BFSG relevance categories\n- Add `ComplianceFinding` model so raw `findings` are never overwritten\n- Add compliance service with idempotent upsert by `finding_id` + `mapping_version`\n- Add `POST /compliance/run/{scan_id}` endpoint returning total findings, critical/high counts, and compliance coverage score\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"scan_id\": \"<scan-id>\",\n  \"mapping_version\": \"2026.05.08-001:<rules-hash>\",\n  \"total_findings\": 3,\n  \"mapped_findings\": 3,\n  \"critical_count\": 0,\n  \"high_count\": 2,\n  \"compliance_coverage_score\": 1.0\n}\n```\n\nInspect persisted enriched findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, normalized_severity, wcag_refs, en_refs, bfsg_refs, compliance_confidence_score FROM compliance_findings;\"\n```\n\n## Integrity and determinism\n\n- Raw `findings` rows are not modified.\n- Enriched compliance rows are stored in `compliance_findings`.\n- Mapping version is derived from the rules file version plus rules file SHA-256 hash.\n- Re-running the endpoint updates existing compliance rows for the same `finding_id` and `mapping_version` and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Does not implement PDF/JSON/EARL reporting.\n- Does not modify Playwright engine or Celery worker execution.\n- I could not run Docker in this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.\n- Depends on PR #3 (`codex/playwright-audit-engine`).",
>   "title": "Add deterministic compliance mapping layer",
>   "base": "codex/playwright-audit-engine",
>   "base_sha": "a36915ea9a94c9671fd77feb1031bdbbe38279f2",

> {"url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/2", "number": 2, "state": "closed", "merged": true, "mergeable": false, "draft": true, "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add Redis-backed Celery configuration in `app/core/celery.py`\n- Add asynchronous scan worker in `app/workers/scan_worker.py`\n- Add scan orchestration service and `POST /scans/run/{lead_id}` endpoint\n- Extend `Scan` status lifecycle with `failed`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, and `failed_at`\n- Update Docker Compose to run API, PostgreSQL, Redis, and an independent Celery worker\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/{lead_id}\n```\n\nImmediate API response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe worker transitions the scan:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> done\n```\n\nFailures are persisted as `failed` with `failed_at`.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Intentionally keeps scan execution as placeholder logic >   "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add Redis-backed Celery configuration in `app/core/celery.py`\n- Add asynchronous scan worker in `app/workers/scan_worker.py`\n- Add scan orchestration service and `POST /scans/run/{lead_id}` endpoint\n- Extend `Scan` status lifecycle with `failed`, `started_at`, `completed_at`, and `failed_at`\n- Update Docker Compose to run API, PostgreSQL, Redis, and an independent Celery worker\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/{lead_id}\n```\n\nImmediate API response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe worker transitions the scan:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> done\n```\n\nFailures are persisted as `failed` with `failed_at`.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Intentionally keeps scan execution as placeholder logic only.\n- Does not add Playwright, compliance engine, reporting, or CLI.\n- I could not run Docker from this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.",

> {"url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/3", "number": 3, "state": "open", "merged": false, "mergeable": true, "draft": true, "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add Playwright browser scan configuration in `app/core/browser_config.py`\n- Add `app/workers/playwright_engine.py` using headless Chromium/Firefox-capable Playwright and axe-core via `axe-playwright-python`\n- Replace placeholder worker sleep with real browser navigation, axe scan execution, evidence metadata capture, and finding persistence\n- Extend scan lifecycle to `pending -> running -> processing -> done`, with `failed` and `error_message` on errors\n- Add scan/finding evidence metadata fields, WCAG refs, confidence score, description, and help URL persistence\n- Update Docker image to install Playwright Chromium + Firefox browsers and configure worker shared memory\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nWorker result:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nInspect evidence and findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT id, status, evidence_metadata->>'current_url' AS current_url FROM scans;\"\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, severity, wcag_refs, confidence_score FROM findings;\"\n```\n\n## Notes\n\n- Stores metadata placeholders only for screenshot/DOM/accessibility tree evidence: hash, size/count, URL, timestamp, browser info.\n- Does not implement compliance mapping, reporting, PDF generation, Playwright-driven crawling, or CI/CD gates.\n- I could not run Docker in this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.\n- Depends on PR #2 (`codex/celery-scan-worker`).", "title": "Add Playwright axe accessibility scan engine", "base": "codex/celery-scan-worker", "base_sha": "c677973c900ebab45a812cc257d2794749931a4d", "head": "codex/playwright-audit-engine", "head_sha": >   "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add Playwright browser scan configuration in `app/core/browser_config.py`\n- Add `app/workers/playwright_engine.py` using headless Chromium/Firefox-capable Playwright and axe-core via `axe-playwright-python`\n- Replace placeholder worker sleep with real browser navigation, axe scan execution, evidence metadata capture, and finding persistence\n- Extend scan lifecycle to `pending -> running -> processing -> done`, with `failed` and `error_message` on errors\n- Add scan/finding evidence metadata fields, WCAG refs, confidence score, description, and help URL persistence\n- Update Docker image to install Playwright Chromium + Firefox browsers and configure worker shared memory\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nWorker result:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nInspect evidence and findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT id, status, evidence_metadata->>'current_url' AS current_url FROM scans;\"\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, severity, wcag_refs, confidence_score FROM findings;\"\n```\n\n## Notes\n\n- Stores metadata placeholders only for screenshot/DOM/accessibility tree evidence: hash, size/count, URL, timestamp, browser info.\n- Does not implement compliance mapping, reporting, PDF generation, Playwright-driven crawling, or CI/CD gates.\n- I could not run Docker in this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.\n- Depends on PR #2 (`codex/celery-scan-worker`).",

> {"url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/4", "number": 4, "state": "open", "merged": false, "mergeable": true, "draft": true, "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add versioned compliance rule dataset at `app/compliance/rules/wcag_en_bfsg_map.json`\n- Add deterministic rule engine that maps raw axe rule IDs and WCAG tags to WCAG, EN 301 549, and BFSG relevance categories\n- Add `ComplianceFinding` model so raw `findings` are never overwritten\n- Add compliance service with idempotent upsert by `finding_id` + `mapping_version`\n- Add `POST /compliance/run/{scan_id}` endpoint returning total findings, critical/high counts, and compliance coverage score\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"scan_id\": \"<scan-id>\",\n  \"mapping_version\": \"2026.05.08-001:<rules-hash>\",\n  \"total_findings\": 3,\n  \"mapped_findings\": 3,\n  \"critical_count\": 0,\n  \"high_count\": 2,\n  \"compliance_coverage_score\": 1.0\n}\n```\n\nInspect persisted enriched findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, normalized_severity, wcag_refs, en_refs, bfsg_refs, compliance_confidence_score FROM compliance_findings;\"\n```\n\n## Integrity and determinism\n\n- Raw `findings` rows are not modified.\n- Enriched compliance rows are stored in `compliance_findings`.\n- Mapping version is derived from the rules file version plus rules file SHA-256 hash.\n- Re-running the endpoint updates existing compliance rows for the same `finding_id` and `mapping_version` and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Does not implement PDF/JSON/EARL reporting.\n- Does not modify Playwright engine or Celery worker execution.\n- I could not run Docker in this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.\n- Depends on PR #3 (`codex/playwright-audit-engine`).", "title": "Add deterministic compliance mapping layer", "base": "codex/playwright-audit-engine", "base_sha": "a36915ea9a94c9671fd77feb1031bdbbe38279f2", "head": "codex/compliance-mapping", "head_sha": "8a71c44120b138b9714a94e7241da9669480b604", "merge_commit_sha": "7259991834589fe93ab6f66f06e0a0bec3583ba4", "user": {"login": "DopeChicka", "name": "DopeChicka", "email": null, "avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25302183?v=4", "id": 25302183}, "requested_reviewers": null, "requested_team_reviewers": null, "diff": null, "comments": null, "created_at": "2026-05-08T11:45:30Z", "updated_at": "2026-05-08T11:45:30Z", "closed_at": null, "merged_at": null, "commits": 11, "changed_files": 11, "additions": 717, "deletions": 3, "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/4", "display_title": "Add deterministic compliance mapping layer"}

> ````json
> {
>   "url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/4",
>   "number": 4,
>   "state": "open",
>   "merged": false,
>   "mergeable": true,
>   "draft": true,
>   "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add versioned compliance rule dataset at `app/compliance/rules/wcag_en_bfsg_map.json`\n- Add deterministic rule engine that maps raw axe rule IDs and WCAG tags to WCAG, EN 301 549, and BFSG relevance categories\n- Add `ComplianceFinding` model so raw `findings` are never overwritten\n- Add compliance service with idempotent upsert by `finding_id` + `mapping_version`\n- Add `POST /compliance/run/{scan_id}` endpoint returning total findings, critical/high counts, and compliance coverage score\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"scan_id\": \"<scan-id>\",\n  \"mapping_version\": \"2026.05.08-001:<rules-hash>\",\n  \"total_findings\": 3,\n  \"mapped_findings\": 3,\n  \"critical_count\": 0,\n  \"high_count\": 2,\n  \"compliance_coverage_score\": 1.0\n}\n```\n\nInspect persisted enriched findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, normalized_severity, wcag_refs, en_refs, bfsg_refs, compliance_confidence_score FROM compliance_findings;\"\n```\n\n## Integrity and determinism\n\n- Raw `findings` rows are not modified.\n- Enriched compliance rows are stored in `compliance_findings`.\n- Mapping version is derived from the rules file version plus rules file SHA-256 hash.\n- Re-running the endpoint updates existing compliance rows for the same `finding_id` and `mapping_version` and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Does not implement PDF/JSON/EARL reporting.\n- Does not modify Playwright engine or Celery worker execution.\n- I could not run Docker in this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.\n- Depends on PR #3 (`codex/playwright-audit-engine`).",
>   "title": "Add deterministic compliance mapping layer",
>   "base": "codex/playwright-audit-engine",

> </details>
>
> GitHub hat #2 automatisch als merged erkannt. Weil #3 und #4 gestapelte Draft-PRs gegen Zwischenbranches sind, bleiben sie in der PR-Ansicht formal offen, obwohl `main` bereits exakt auf dem finalen #4-Commit steht. Ich schließe die beiden sauber mit einem Hinweis, damit die Repo-Ansicht nicht verwirrend bleibt.

>   "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add Playwright browser scan configuration in `app/core/browser_config.py`\n- Add `app/workers/playwright_engine.py` using headless Chromium/Firefox-capable Playwright and axe-core via `axe-playwright-python`\n- Replace placeholder worker sleep with real browser navigation, axe scan execution, evidence metadata capture, and finding persistence\n- Extend scan lifecycle to `pending -> running -> processing -> done`, with `failed` and `error_message` on errors\n- Add scan/finding evidence metadata fields, WCAG refs, confidence score, description, and help URL persistence\n- Update Docker image to install Playwright Chromium + Firefox browsers and configure worker shared memory\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nWorker result:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nInspect evidence and findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT id, status, evidence_metadata->>'current_url' AS current_url FROM scans;\"\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, severity, wcag_refs, confidence_score FROM findings;\"\n```\n\n## Notes\n\n- Stores metadata placeholders only for screenshot/DOM/accessibility tree evidence: hash, size/count, URL, timestamp, browser info.\n- Does not implement compliance mapping, reporting, PDF generation, Playwright-driven crawling, or CI/CD gates.\n- I could not run Docker in this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.\n- Depends on PR #2 (`codex/celery-scan-worker`).",

> {"url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/4", "number": 4, "state": "closed", "merged": false, "mergeable": true, "draft": true, "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add versioned compliance rule dataset at `app/compliance/rules/wcag_en_bfsg_map.json`\n- Add deterministic rule engine that maps raw axe rule IDs and WCAG tags to WCAG, EN 301 549, and BFSG relevance categories\n- Add `ComplianceFinding` model so raw `findings` are never overwritten\n- Add compliance service with idempotent upsert by `finding_id` + `mapping_version`\n- Add `POST /compliance/run/{scan_id}` endpoint returning total findings, critical/high counts, and compliance coverage score\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"scan_id\": \"<scan-id>\",\n  \"mapping_version\": \"2026.05.08-001:<rules-hash>\",\n  \"total_findings\": 3,\n  \"mapped_findings\": 3,\n  \"critical_count\": 0,\n  \"high_count\": 2,\n  \"compliance_coverage_score\": 1.0\n}\n```\n\nInspect persisted enriched findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, normalized_severity, wcag_refs, en_refs, bfsg_refs, compliance_confidence_score FROM compliance_findings;\"\n```\n\n## Integrity and determinism\n\n- Raw `findings` rows are not modified.\n- Enriched compliance rows are stored in `compliance_findings`.\n- Mapping version is derived from the rules file version plus rules file SHA-256 hash.\n- Re-running the endpoint updates existing compliance rows for the same `finding_id` and `mapping_version` and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Does not implement PDF/JSON/EARL reporting.\n- Does not modify Playwright engine or >   "body": "## Summary\n\n- Add versioned compliance rule dataset at `app/compliance/rules/wcag_en_bfsg_map.json`\n- Add deterministic rule engine that maps raw axe rule IDs and WCAG tags to WCAG, EN 301 549, and BFSG relevance categories\n- Add `ComplianceFinding` model so raw `findings` are never overwritten\n- Add compliance service with idempotent upsert by `finding_id` + `mapping_version`\n- Add `POST /compliance/run/{scan_id}` endpoint returning total findings, critical/high counts, and compliance coverage score\n\n## Example flow\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"scan_id\": \"<scan-id>\",\n  \"mapping_version\": \"2026.05.08-001:<rules-hash>\",\n  \"total_findings\": 3,\n  \"mapped_findings\": 3,\n  \"critical_count\": 0,\n  \"high_count\": 2,\n  \"compliance_coverage_score\": 1.0\n}\n```\n\nInspect persisted enriched findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, normalized_severity, wcag_refs, en_refs, bfsg_refs, compliance_confidence_score FROM compliance_findings;\"\n```\n\n## Integrity and determinism\n\n- Raw `findings` rows are not modified.\n- Enriched compliance rows are stored in `compliance_findings`.\n- Mapping version is derived from the rules file version plus rules file SHA-256 hash.\n- Re-running the endpoint updates existing compliance rows for the same `finding_id` and `mapping_version` and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Notes\n\n- Does not implement PDF/JSON/EARL reporting.\n- Does not modify Playwright engine or Celery worker execution.\n- I could not run Docker in this scratch environment, so validation was limited to connector-backed diff and code review.\n- Depends on PR #3 (`codex/playwright-audit-engine`).",

Done. Everything is now live on `main` at commit `8a71c44120b138b9714a94e7241da9669480b604`.

This includes Celery + Redis, Playwright + axe scanning, raw finding persistence, and the BFSG/WCAG/EN 301 549 compliance mapping endpoint. PR #2 is marked merged; PR #3 and #4 were closed with comments because they were stacked draft PRs, but their commits are included on `main`.

To run the live version locally after pulling:
```bash
docker-compose up --build
```

Small note: this is live in GitHub `main`; your `localhost:8000` instance will only reflect it after the local project is updated and rebuilt.

> # Role
> You are a senior backend engineer extending NorthAccessBFSG into an audit-grade compliance system.
>
> # Goal
> Implement a persistent, tamper-evident Evidence System that stores all artifacts from accessibility scans in a reproducible and verifiable way.
>
> This system ensures audit traceability and legal defensibility of scan results.
>
> # Context
> Existing system:
> - FastAPI backend
> - PostgreSQL database
> - Celery + Redis async scan execution
> - Playwright scanning engine (axe-core integration)
> - Compliance mapping engine (WCAG → EN → BFSG)
>
> Current gap:
> Evidence (screenshots, DOM snapshots, accessibility trees) is NOT persistently stored or cryptographically verifiable.
>
> # Scope (STRICT)
>
> ## 1. Evidence Storage Layer
> Create module:
> - app/evidence/storage.py
>
> Must support:
> - storing screenshots
> - storing DOM snapshots
> - storing accessibility snapshots
> - retrieving evidence by scan_id and finding_id
>
> Use file-based or object storage abstraction (S3-compatible interface preferred).
>
> ## 2. Evidence Model
> Extend database with:
>
> EvidenceBundle:
> - id
> - scan_id
> - finding_id (nullable for global evidence)
> - type (screenshot | dom | a11y_tree)
> - storage_path
> - created_at
> - hash (SHA-256)
>
> ## 3. Hashing & Integrity Layer
> Create module:
> - app/evidence/hashing.py
>
> Must:
> - compute SHA-256 hash of all evidence artifacts
> - generate deterministic evidence fingerprint per finding
> - support chain hashing (optional but preferred)
>
> Rule:
> Any modification to evidence must invalidate hash chain.
>
> ## 4. Integration into Playwright Worker
> Extend scan execution flow:
>
> For each page scanned:
> - capture screenshot
> - capture DOM snapshot
> - capture accessibility tree snapshot
> - send to evidence storage
> - generate hashes
> - link EvidenceBundle to Finding
>
> ## 5. API Layer
> Add endpoints:
>
> GET /evidence/scan/{scan_id}
> GET /evidence/finding/{finding_id}
>
> Must return:
> - metadata
> - storage references
> - hashes
> - timestamps
>
> No raw binary required in API response (only references).
>
> ## 6. Storage Abstraction
> Create:
> - app/evidence/storage_backend.py
>
> Must allow:
> - local filesystem (dev)
> - S3-compatible backend (production-ready abstraction)
>
> ## 7. Immutability Requirement
> Evidence must be:
> - append-only
> - never overwritten
> - versioned if re-captured
>
> ## 8. Docker requirement
> Ensure:
> - evidence storage directory mounted in container OR S3 config supported
>
> # Constraints
> - DO NOT implement reporting (PDF/JSON/EARL yet)
> - DO NOT modify compliance engine logic
> - DO NOT change Playwright core behavior except evidence hooks
> - MUST ensure backward compatibility with existing scan system
>
> # Success Criteria
>
> System is correct if:
>
> - every scan generates evidence bundles
> - each finding is linked to at least one evidence entry
> - hashes are generated and stored
> - evidence can be retrieved via API
> - evidence integrity is verifiable (same input → same hash)
> - storage works in docker-compose environment
>
> # Output Required
>
> 1. Evidence system architecture
> 2. Database schema additions
> 3. Python implementation (storage + hashing)
> 4. Playwright integration changes
> 5. API endpoints
> 6. Docker/storage setup changes
> 7. Example evidence lifecycle flow

> ```text
> {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport json\nimport time\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\nfrom typing import Any\nfrom urllib.parse import urlparse\n\nfrom axe_playwright_python.sync_playwright import Axe\nfrom playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright\n\nfrom app.core.browser_config import BrowserConfig, get_browser_config\n\n_SEVERITY_BY_AXE_IMPACT = {\n    \"critical\": \"critical\",\n    \"serious\": \"high\",\n    \"moderate\": \"medium\",\n    \"minor\": \"low\",\n}\n_CONFIDENCE_BY_AXE_IMPACT = {\n    \"critical\": 0.95,\n    \"serious\": 0.9,\n    \"moderate\": 0.8,\n    \"minor\": 0.65,\n}\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass EngineFinding:\n    rule_id: str\n    severity: str\n    wcag_refs: list[str]\n    confidence_score: float\n    description: str | None\n    help_url: str | None\n    evidence_metadata: dict[str, Any]\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass PlaywrightScanResult:\n    target_url: str\n    current_url: str\n    captured_at: str\n    evidence_metadata: dict[str, Any]\n    findings: list[EngineFinding]\n\n\ndef run_accessibility_scan(domain: str) -> PlaywrightScanResult:\n    config = get_browser_config()\n    target_url = normalize_target_url(domain)\n    last_error: Exception | None = None\n\n    for attempt in range(config.retries + 1):\n        try:\n            return _run_once(target_url=target_url, config=config, attempt=attempt)\n        except Exception as exc:\n            last_error = exc\n            if attempt < config.retries:\n                time.sleep(min(2**attempt, 5))\n\n    raise RuntimeError(f\"Playwright scan failed for {target_url}\") from last_error\n\n\ndef normalize_target_url(domain: str) -> str:\n    candidate = domain.strip()\n    parsed = urlparse(candidate)\n    if parsed.scheme:\n        return candidate\n    return f\"https://{candidate}\"\n\n\ndef _run_once(\n    target_url: str, config: BrowserConfig, attempt: int\n) -> PlaywrightScanResult:\n    captured_at = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()\n\n    with sync_playwright() as playwright:\n        browser_type = getattr(playwright, config.browser_name)\n        launch_args = [\"--no-sandbox\"] if config.browser_name == \"chromium\" else []\n        browser = browser_type.launch(headless=config.headless, args=launch_args)\n        try:\n            context = browser.new_context(\n                viewport={\n                    \"width\": config.viewport_width,\n                    \"height\": config.viewport_height,\n                }\n            )\n            context.set_default_timeout(config.action_timeout_ms)\n            context.set_default_navigation_timeout(config.navigation_timeout_ms)\n\n            page = context.new_page()\n            page.goto(\n                target_url,\n                wait_until=config.wait_until,\n                timeout=config.navigation_timeout_ms,\n            )\n\n            axe_response = Axe().run(page).response\n            dom_snapshot = page.content()\n            screenshot_bytes = page.screenshot(full_page=True)\n            accessibility_tree = _capture_accessibility_tree(page)\n            current_url = page.url\n            evidence_metadata = _build_scan_evidence_metadata(\n                accessibility_tree=accessibility_tree,\n                axe_response=axe_response,\n                captured_at=captured_at,\n                config=config,\n                current_url=current_url,\n                dom_snapshot=dom_snapshot,\n                screenshot_bytes=screenshot_bytes,\n                target_url=target_url,\n                attempt=attempt,\n            )\n            findings = _findings_from_axe_response(axe_response, evidence_metadata)\n\n            return PlaywrightScanResult(\n                target_url=target_url,\n                current_url=current_url,\n                captured_at=captured_at,\n                evidence_metadata=evidence_metadata,\n                findings=findings,\n            )\n        finally:\n            browser.close()\n\n\ndef _capture_accessibility_tree(page) -> dict[str, Any] | None:\n    try:\n        return page.accessibility.snapshot(interesting_only=False)\n    except Exception:\n        return None\n\n\ndef _build_scan_evidence_metadata(\n    *,\n    accessibility_tree: dict[str, Any] | None,\n    axe_response: dict[str, Any],\n    captured_at: str,\n    config: BrowserConfig,\n    current_url: str,\n    dom_snapshot: str,\n    screenshot_bytes: bytes,\n    target_url: str,\n    attempt: int,\n) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    accessibility_tree_json = _json_dump(accessibility_tree) if accessibility_tree else None\n\n    return {\n        \"target_url\": target_url,\n        \"current_url\": current_url,\n        \"captured_at\": captured_at,\n        \"browser\": {\n            \"name\": config.browser_name,\n            \"headless\": config.headless,\n            \"viewport\": {\n                \"width\": config.viewport_width,\n                \"height\": config.viewport_height,\n            },\n            \"navigation_timeout_ms\": config.navigation_timeout_ms,\n            \"attempt\": attempt + 1,\n        },\n        \"axe\": {\n            \"violations_count\": len(axe_response.get(\"violations\", [])),\n            \"passes_count\": len(axe_response.get(\"passes\", [])),\n            \"incomplete_count\": len(axe_response.get(\"incomplete\", [])),\n            \"inapplicable_count\": len(axe_response.get(\"inapplicable\", [])),\n        },\n        \"screenshot\": {\n            \"storage\": \"pending\",\n            \"full_page\": True,\n            \"byte_count\": len(screenshot_bytes),\n            \"sha256\": hashlib.sha256(screenshot_bytes).hexdigest(),\n        },\n        \"dom_snapshot\": {\n            \"storage\": \"pending\",\n            \"char_count\": len(dom_snapshot),\n            \"sha256\": _sha256_text(dom_snapshot),\n        },\n        \"accessibility_tree\": {\n            \"storage\": \"pending\",\n            \"available\": accessibility_tree is not None,\n            \"node_count\": _count_accessibility_nodes(accessibility_tree),\n            \"sha256\": _sha256_text(accessibility_tree_json)\n            if accessibility_tree_json\n            else None,\n        },\n    }\n\n\ndef _findings_from_axe_response(\n    axe_response: dict[str, Any], scan_evidence: dict[str, Any]\n) -> list[EngineFinding]:\n    findings: list[EngineFinding] = []\n    for violation in axe_response.get(\"violations\", []):\n        impact = violation.get(\"impact\") or \"unknown\"\n        nodes = violation.get(\"nodes\") or []\n        wcag_refs = _extract_wcag_refs(violation.get(\"tags\") or [])\n        confidence_score = _confidence_for_violation(impact=impact, node_count=len(nodes))\n\n        findings.append(\n            EngineFinding(\n                rule_id=violation.get(\"id\", \"unknown\"),\n                severity=_SEVERITY_BY_AXE_IMPACT.get(impact, \"info\"),\n                wcag_refs=wcag_refs,\n                confidence_score=confidence_score,\n                description=violation.get(\"description\") or violation.get(\"help\"),\n                help_url=violation.get(\"helpUrl\"),\n                evidence_metadata={\n                    **scan_evidence,\n                    \"axe_violation\": {\n                        \"impact\": impact,\n                        \"help\": violation.get(\"help\"),\n                        \"help_url\": violation.get(\"helpUrl\"),\n                        \"node_count\": len(nodes),\n                        \"sample_targets\": _sample_node_targets(nodes),\n                    },\n                },\n            )\n        )\n\n    return findings\n\n\ndef _extract_wcag_refs(tags: list[str]) -> list[str]:\n    return sorted({tag for tag in tags if tag.lower().startswith(\"wcag\")})\n\n\ndef _confidence_for_violation(*, impact: str, node_count: int) -> float:\n    base = _CONFIDENCE_BY_AXE_IMPACT.get(impact, 0.5)\n    if node_count >= 3:\n        base += 0.03\n    return min(base, 0.99)\n\n\ndef _sample_node_targets(nodes: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[list[str]]:\n    targets: list[list[str]] = []\n    for node in nodes[:5]:\n        target = node.get(\"target\")\n        if isinstance(target, list):\n            targets.append([str(item) for item in target])\n    return targets\n\n\ndef _count_accessibility_nodes(node: dict[str, Any] | None) -> int:\n    if not node:\n        return 0\n    children = node.get(\"children\") or []\n    return 1 + sum(_count_accessibility_nodes(child) for child in children)\n\n\ndef _json_dump(value: Any) -> str:\n    return json.dumps(value, default=str, ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True)\n\n\ndef _sha256_text(value: str) -> str:\n    return hashlib.sha256(value.encode(\"utf-8\")).hexdigest()\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "eb98ee3584cd92979599986eb9c0aafd83403dc3", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/workers/playwright_engine.py", "display_title": "playwright_engine.py"}
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "import logging\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n\nfrom app.core.celery import celery_app\nfrom app.db.session import SessionLocal\nfrom app.models import Finding, Scan, ScanStatus\nfrom app.workers.playwright_engine import PlaywrightScanResult, run_accessibility_scan\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n_MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH = 4000\n\n\n@celery_app.task(name=\"app.workers.scan_worker.run_scan\")\ndef run_scan_task(scan_id: str) -> str:\n    db = SessionLocal()\n    try:\n        scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n        if scan is None:\n            logger.warning(\"Scan %s was not found; skipping task\", scan_id)\n            return scan_id\n\n        domain = scan.lead.domain\n        scan.status = ScanStatus.running\n        scan.started_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n        scan.completed_at = None\n        scan.failed_at = None\n        scan.error_message = None\n        scan.evidence_metadata = {}\n        db.commit()\n\n        logger.info(\"Started Playwright accessibility scan %s for %s\", scan_id, domain)\n        scan_result = run_accessibility_scan(domain)\n\n        scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n        if scan is None:\n            logger.warning(\"Scan %s disappeared before persistence\", scan_id)\n            return scan_id\n\n        scan.status = ScanStatus.processing\n        scan.evidence_metadata = scan_result.evidence_metadata\n        db.commit()\n\n        _persist_findings(db, scan_id=scan_id, scan_result=scan_result)\n\n        scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n        if scan is not None:\n            scan.status = ScanStatus.done\n            scan.completed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n            scan.error_message = None\n            db.commit()\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"Completed Playwright accessibility scan %s with %s findings\",\n            scan_id,\n            len(scan_result.findings),\n        )\n        return scan_id\n    except Exception as exc:\n        db.rollback()\n        _mark_scan_failed(db, scan_id, exc)\n        logger.exception(\"Scan task %s failed\", scan_id)\n        raise\n    finally:\n        db.close()\n\n\ndef _persist_findings(\n    db: Session, *, scan_id: str, scan_result: PlaywrightScanResult\n) -> None:\n    db.query(Finding).filter(Finding.scan_id == scan_id).delete(synchronize_session=False)\n\n    for engine_finding in scan_result.findings:\n        db.add(\n            Finding(\n                scan_id=scan_id,\n                rule_id=engine_finding.rule_id,\n                severity=engine_finding.severity,\n                description=engine_finding.description,\n                help_url=engine_finding.help_url,\n                wcag_refs=engine_finding.wcag_refs,\n                confidence_score=engine_finding.confidence_score,\n                evidence_metadata=engine_finding.evidence_metadata,\n            )\n        )\n\n    db.commit()\n\n\ndef _mark_scan_failed(db: Session, scan_id: str, error: Exception) -> None:\n    scan = db.get(Scan, scan_id)\n    if scan is None:\n        return\n\n    scan.status = ScanStatus.failed\n    scan.failed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n    scan.error_message = str(error)[:_MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_LENGTH]\n    db.commit()\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "7c43dfcfd65d3d4ff377bbe625db8e23c7366a3b", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/workers/scan_worker.py", "display_title": "scan_worker.py"}
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "from sqlalchemy import text\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.db.session import engine\nfrom app.models import ComplianceFinding, Finding, Lead, Scan  # noqa: F401\n\n_SCAN_ENUM_UPDATES = (\n    \"ALTER TYPE scan_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'processing'\",\n    \"ALTER TYPE scan_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'failed'\",\n)\n\n_SCAN_COLUMN_UPDATES = (\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS started_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS failed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS error_message TEXT\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS evidence_metadata JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb\",\n)\n\n_FINDING_COLUMN_UPDATES = (\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS description TEXT\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS help_url VARCHAR(500)\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS wcag_refs JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS confidence_score DOUBLE PRECISION\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS evidence_metadata JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb\",\n)\n\n\ndef init_db() -> None:\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n    _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema()\n    _ensure_finding_evidence_schema()\n\n\ndef _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema() -> None:\n    if engine.dialect.name != \"postgresql\":\n        return\n\n    with engine.connect() as connection:\n        autocommit_connection = connection.execution_options(isolation_level=\"AUTOCOMMIT\")\n        for statement in _SCAN_ENUM_UPDATES:\n            autocommit_connection.execute(text(statement))\n\n    with engine.begin() as connection:\n        for statement in _SCAN_COLUMN_UPDATES:\n            connection.execute(text(statement))\n\n\ndef _ensure_finding_evidence_schema() -> None:\n    if engine.dialect.name != \"postgresql\":\n        return\n\n    with engine.begin() as connection:\n        for statement in _FINDING_COLUMN_UPDATES:\n            connection.execute(text(statement))\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "b7be0d443669c65e1e9ebe6bc881739a8f10bfbe", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/db/init_db.py", "display_title": "init_db.py"}
> ```
>
> </details>
>
> Die aktuelle Engine hält Screenshot/DOM/A11y-Tree nur als Hash/Metadaten im Scanresult. Ich ändere das so, dass die Engine Artefakt-Payloads zurückgibt und der Worker diese append-only im Evidence Storage persistiert.

> {"content": "from functools import lru_cache\n\nfrom pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict\n\n\nclass Settings(BaseSettings):\n    app_name: str = \"NorthAccessBFSG API\"\n    environment: str = \"development\"\n    database_url: str = (\n        \"postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@localhost:5432/northaccessbfsg\"\n    )\n    celery_broker_url: str = \"redis://localhost:6379/0\"\n    celery_result_backend: str = \"redis://localhost:6379/1\"\n    browser_name: str = \"chromium\"\n    browser_headless: bool = True\n    browser_navigation_timeout_ms: int = 30_000\n    browser_action_timeout_ms: int = 10_000\n    browser_viewport_width: int = 1440\n    browser_viewport_height: int = 900\n    browser_retries: int = 1\n    browser_wait_until: str = \"networkidle\"\n\n    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(\n        env_file=\".env\",\n        env_file_encoding=\"utf-8\",\n        extra=\"ignore\",\n    )\n\n\n@lru_cache\ndef get_settings() -> Settings:\n    return Settings()\n\n\nsettings = get_settings()\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "96a1ed0d18b2196aa5c034a17db5d2585f1afeef", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/core/config.py", "display_title": "config.py"}

> {"content": "from collections.abc import AsyncIterator\nfrom contextlib import asynccontextmanager\n\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\n\nfrom app.api.compliance import router as compliance_router\nfrom app.api.health import router as health_router\nfrom app.api.scans import router as scans_router\nfrom app.core.config import settings\nfrom app.db.init_db import init_db\n\n\n@asynccontextmanager\nasync def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:\n    init_db()\n    yield\n\n\napp = FastAPI(\n    title=settings.app_name,\n    version=\"0.1.0\",\n    lifespan=lifespan,\n)\n\napp.include_router(health_router)\napp.include_router(scans_router)\napp.include_router(compliance_router)\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "4c27566e19b41834b2fe917328b60161fb97a45d", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/main.py", "display_title": "main.py"}

> {"content": "services:\n  api:\n    build: .\n    environment:\n      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@db:5432/northaccessbfsg\n      ENVIRONMENT: development\n      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\n      CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND: redis://redis:6379/1\n    ports:\n      - \"8000:8000\"\n    depends_on:\n      db:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      redis:\n        condition: service_healthy\n\n  worker:\n    build: .\n    command: celery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info\n    shm_size: \"1gb\"\n    environment:\n      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@db:5432/northaccessbfsg\n      ENVIRONMENT: development\n      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\n      CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND: redis://redis:6379/1\n      BROWSER_NAME: chromium\n      BROWSER_HEADLESS: \"true\"\n      BROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS: 30000\n      BROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS: 10000\n      BROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH: 1440\n      BROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT: 900\n      BROWSER_RETRIES: 1\n      BROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL: networkidle\n    depends_on:\n      db:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      redis:\n        condition: service_healthy\n\n  db:\n    image: postgres:16-alpine\n    environment:\n      POSTGRES_USER: northaccess\n      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: northaccess\n      POSTGRES_DB: northaccessbfsg\n    ports:\n      - \"5432:5432\"\n    volumes:\n      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD-SHELL\", \"pg_isready -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg\"]\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 10\n\n  redis:\n    image: redis:7-alpine\n    ports:\n      - \"6379:6379\"\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD\", \"redis-cli\", \"ping\"]\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 10\n\nvolumes:\n  postgres_data:\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "95bb94d98dfc02bc57df51ef5fb624b9cede5f2c", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/docker-compose.yml", "display_title": "docker-compose.yml"}

> ```json

> ```
>
> ````text
> {"content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nMinimal FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance.py\n    health.py\n    scans.py\n  compliance/\n    __init__.py\n    rule_engine.py\n    rules/\n      __init__.py\n      wcag_en_bfsg_map.json\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    browser_config.py\n    celery.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  models/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance_finding.py\n    finding.py\n    lead.py\n    scan.py\n  services/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance_service.py\n    scan_service.py\n  workers/\n    __init__.py\n    playwright_engine.py\n    scan_worker.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```text\nhttp://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the `leads`, `scans`, `findings`, and `compliance_findings` tables on startup.\n\n## Async Scan Flow\n\nCreate a lead directly in the database while a public lead API does not exist yet:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n```\n\nRun a scan for the lead:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nExpected immediate response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker launches Playwright headless Chromium, loads the lead domain, runs axe-core, captures evidence metadata, and persists raw findings.\n\nLifecycle:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nIf task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n\nInspect scan status and evidence metadata:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT id, status, error_message, evidence_metadata->>'current_url' AS current_url FROM scans;\"\n```\n\nInspect stored findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, severity, wcag_refs, confidence_score FROM findings;\"\n```\n\n## Compliance Mapping Flow\n\nRun deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"scan_id\": \"<scan-id>\",\n  \"mapping_version\": \"2026.05.08-001:<rules-hash>\",\n  \"total_findings\": 3,\n  \"mapped_findings\": 3,\n  \"critical_count\": 0,\n  \"high_count\": 2,\n  \"compliance_coverage_score\": 1.0\n}\n```\n\nCompliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\nInspect enriched compliance findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, normalized_severity, wcag_refs, en_refs, bfsg_refs, compliance_confidence_score FROM compliance_findings;\"\n```\n\nThe worker process can also be started independently:\n\n```bash\ncelery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info\n```\n\n## Browser Configuration\n\nThe worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n\n```text\nBROWSER_NAME=chromium\nBROWSER_HEADLESS=true\nBROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS=30000\nBROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS=10000\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH=1440\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=900\nBROWSER_RETRIES=1\nBROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL=networkidle\n```\n\nWebKit is supported by configuration but is not installed in the default image.\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "3372df2735fb0cf38cd46d7f137bd4481f690b7f", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/README.md", "display_title": "README.md"}
> ````

> </details>
>
> Ich füge jetzt das Evidence-Modell plus Storage-Abstraktion hinzu: lokal als Docker-Volume für Dev, S3-kompatibel über Konfiguration für Produktion. Die DB speichert nur Referenzen, Hashes, Versionen und Chain-Hashes, keine Binärdaten.
>

> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/evidence-system",
>   "path": "app/evidence/hashing.py",
>   "message": "Add evidence hashing helpers",
>   "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport hashlib\nimport json\nfrom typing import Any\n\n\ndef sha256_bytes(content: bytes) -> str:\n    return hashlib.sha256(content).hexdigest()\n\n\ndef sha256_text(content: str) -> str:\n    return sha256_bytes(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n\n\ndef canonical_json(value: Any) -> str:\n    return json.dumps(value, default=str, ensure_ascii=False, separators=(\",\", \":\"), sort_keys=True)\n\n\ndef sha256_json(value: Any) -> str:\n    return sha256_text(canonical_json(value))\n\n\ndef evidence_fingerprint(*, finding_id: str, artifact_hashes: list[str]) -> str:\n    payload = {\n        \"artifact_hashes\": sorted(artifact_hashes),\n        \"finding_id\": finding_id,\n    }\n    return sha256_json(payload)\n\n\ndef chain_hash(\n    *,\n    previous_chain_hash: str | None,\n    artifact_hash: str,\n    scan_id: str,\n    finding_id: str | None,\n    evidence_type: str,\n    storage_path: str,\n    version: int,\n) -> str:\n    payload = {\n        \"artifact_hash\": artifact_hash,\n        \"evidence_type\": evidence_type,\n        \"finding_id\": finding_id,\n        \"previous_chain_hash\": previous_chain_hash,\n        \"scan_id\": scan_id,\n        \"storage_path\": storage_path,\n        \"version\": version,\n    }\n    return sha256_json(payload)\n"
> }
> ```

>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/evidence-system",
>   "path": "app/evidence/storage_backend.py",
>   "message": "Add evidence storage backends",
>   "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom abc import ABC, abstractmethod\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass StoredObject:\n    backend: str\n    storage_path: str\n    size_bytes: int\n\n\nclass EvidenceStorageBackend(ABC):\n    name: str\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    def put_bytes(self, *, storage_path: str, content: bytes, content_type: str) -> StoredObject:\n        pass\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    def get_bytes(self, *, storage_path: str) -> bytes:\n        pass\n\n\nclass LocalFileStorageBackend(EvidenceStorageBackend):\n    name = \"local\"\n\n    def __init__(self, root_path: str) -> None:\n        self.root_path = Path(root_path)\n\n    def put_bytes(self, *, storage_path: str, content: bytes, content_type: str) -> StoredObject:\n        destination = self._resolve(storage_path)\n        destination.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        if destination.exists():\n            raise FileExistsError(f\"Evidence object already exists: {storage_path}\")\n\n        destination.write_bytes(content)\n        return StoredObject(\n            backend=self.name,\n            storage_path=storage_path,\n            size_bytes=len(content),\n        )\n\n    def get_bytes(self, *, storage_path: str) -> bytes:\n        return self._resolve(storage_path).read_bytes()\n\n    def _resolve(self, storage_path: str) -> Path:\n        relative_path = Path(storage_path)\n        if relative_path.is_absolute() or \"..\" in relative_path.parts:\n            raise ValueError(\"Evidence storage path must be relative\")\n        return self.root_path / relative_path\n\n\nclass S3StorageBackend(EvidenceStorageBackend):\n    name = \"s3\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        bucket: str,\n        prefix: str = \"\",\n        endpoint_url: str | None = None,\n        region_name: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        if not bucket:\n            raise ValueError(\"S3 evidence storage requires a bucket\")\n\n        import boto3\n\n        self.bucket = bucket\n        self.prefix = prefix.strip(\"/\")\n        self.client = boto3.client(\n            \"s3\", endpoint_url=endpoint_url or None, region_name=region_name or None\n        )\n\n    def put_bytes(self, *, storage_path: str, content: bytes, content_type: str) -> StoredObject:\n        key = self._key(storage_path)\n        try:\n            self.client.head_object(Bucket=self.bucket, Key=key)\n        except self.client.exceptions.ClientError as exc:\n            status_code = exc.response.get(\"ResponseMetadata\", {}).get(\"HTTPStatusCode\")\n            if status_code != 404:\n                raise\n        else:\n            raise FileExistsError(f\"Evidence object already exists: {storage_path}\")\n\n        self.client.put_object(\n            Bucket=self.bucket,\n            Key=key,\n            Body=content,\n            ContentType=content_type,\n            Metadata={\"immutable\": \"true\"},\n        )\n        return StoredObject(\n            backend=self.name,\n            storage_path=f\"s3://{self.bucket}/{key}\",\n            size_bytes=len(content),\n        )\n\n    def get_bytes(self, *, storage_path: str) -> bytes:\n        key = storage_path\n        prefix = f\"s3://{self.bucket}/\"\n        if storage_path.startswith(prefix):\n            key = storage_path[len(prefix) :]\n\n        response = self.client.get_object(Bucket=self.bucket, Key=key)\n        return response[\"Body\"].read()\n\n    def _key(self, storage_path: str) -> str:\n        clean_path = storage_path.strip(\"/\")\n        if self.prefix:\n            return f\"{self.prefix}/{clean_path}\"\n        return clean_path\n"
> }
> ```
