> {"content": "services:\n  api:\n    build: .\n    environment:\n      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@db:5432/northaccessbfsg\n      ENVIRONMENT: development\n      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\n      CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND: redis://redis:6379/1\n      EVIDENCE_STORAGE_BACKEND: local\n      EVIDENCE_LOCAL_ROOT: /app/evidence-data\n    ports:\n      - \"8000:8000\"\n    volumes:\n      - evidence_data:/app/evidence-data\n    depends_on:\n      db:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      redis:\n        condition: service_healthy\n\n  worker:\n    build: .\n    command: celery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info\n    shm_size: \"1gb\"\n    environment:\n      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@db:5432/northaccessbfsg\n      ENVIRONMENT: development\n      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\n      CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND: redis://redis:6379/1\n      BROWSER_NAME: chromium\n      BROWSER_HEADLESS: \"true\"\n      BROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS: 30000\n      BROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS: 10000\n      BROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH: 1440\n      BROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT: 900\n      BROWSER_RETRIES: 1\n      BROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL: networkidle\n      EVIDENCE_STORAGE_BACKEND: local\n      EVIDENCE_LOCAL_ROOT: /app/evidence-data\n    volumes:\n      - evidence_data:/app/evidence-data\n    depends_on:\n      db:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      redis:\n        condition: service_healthy\n\n  db:\n    image: postgres:16-alpine\n    environment:\n      POSTGRES_USER: northaccess\n      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: northaccess\n      POSTGRES_DB: northaccessbfsg\n    ports:\n      - \"5432:5432\"\n    volumes:\n      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD-SHELL\", \"pg_isready -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg\"]\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 10\n\n  redis:\n    image: redis:7-alpine\n    ports:\n      - \"6379:6379\"\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD\", \"redis-cli\", \"ping\"]\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 10\n\nvolumes:\n  postgres_data:\n  evidence_data:\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "d47029c22f28ee20febbb311cdd969aad4aa9cb4", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/docker-compose.yml", "display_title": "docker-compose.yml"}

Done, pushed live to `main`.

Latest live commit:
`0d3e5a0c117655bf2352860d959c08f80b593fe6`

Evidence System is now on GitHub with:
- `EvidenceBundle` model
- append-only evidence storage
- SHA-256 hashing/fingerprints
- local/S3-ready storage abstraction
- Playwright worker evidence hooks
- `GET /evidence/scan/{scan_id}`
- `GET /evidence/finding/{finding_id}`
- Docker volume `evidence_data` mounted at `/app/evidence-data`

To run the live version locally:

```bash
docker-compose up --build
```

I did not run Docker locally in this final push step, but I verified `main` now matches the evidence branch exactly.

`main` auf GitHub zeigt aktuell Commit `0d3e5a0c117655bf2352860d959c08f80b593fe6`.

Der lokale Thread-Ordner ist kein nutzbarer Checkout: `git` und `python` sind in dieser Shell nicht im PATH, daher konnte ich `git status -sb` und `python -m py_compile ...` lokal nicht erfolgreich ausführen. Ich habe den Live-Repo-Tree direkt über GitHub geprüft.

**`find . -maxdepth 2 -type f | sort` entspricht auf GitHub aktuell:**
```text
./.dockerignore
./.env.example
./.gitignore
./Dockerfile
./README.md
./app/__init__.py
./app/main.py
./docker-compose.yml
./requirements.txt
```

**Fehlende Dateien**
```text
MISSING: 13_city_guard.py
MISSING: 14_evidence_gate.py
MISSING: 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py
MISSING: 05_run_pipeline.py
MISSING: 04_filter_quality.py
MISSING: orte_deutschland.csv
```

Damit ist der guarded Runner aktuell nicht lauffähig, weil bereits `15_run_pipeline_guarded.py` selbst im Repo fehlt. Deshalb kann ich auch nicht prüfen, welche internen Referenzen dieser Runner enthält.

**Empfehlung**
Restore ist sinnvoller als Stub oder Rewrite. Die Dateinamen wirken wie eine bestehende Pipeline-Kette; Stubs würden nur scheinbare Lauffähigkeit erzeugen, und Rewrite wäre riskant ohne Originalverhalten. Besonders `orte_deutschland.csv` sollte als echte Runtime-Datenquelle wiederhergestellt werden, nicht erfunden.

> merge to live. if you need to fix something to do that. do it!

<details><summary>12 previous messages</summary>

>
> </details>
>
> Ich ergänze jetzt bewusst keine Fachlogik, sondern nur ein kleines, explizites Pipeline-Gerüst: die Guard-Dateien, die beiden referenzierten Pipeline-Skripte und die CSV als leere Datenquelle mit Header. Damit ist der Runner ehrlich lauffähig und bricht später kontrolliert ab, statt auf fehlende Dateien zu zeigen.
>

>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "main",
>   "path": "13_city_guard.py",
>   "message": "Restore guarded pipeline runtime files",
>   "content": "\"\"\"Validate that the city input file exists and is readable.\n\nThis guard intentionally performs only structural checks. It does not invent or\nmodify city data.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport csv\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nCITY_FILE = Path(\"orte_deutschland.csv\")\n\n\ndef validate_city_file(path: Path = CITY_FILE) -> dict[str, int | str]:\n    if not path.is_file():\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing city source file: {path}\")\n\n    with path.open(\"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\", newline=\"\") as handle:\n        reader = csv.DictReader(handle)\n        if not reader.fieldnames:\n            raise ValueError(f\"City source file has no header: {path}\")\n        row_count = sum(1 for _ in reader)\n\n    return {\"file\": str(path), \"rows\": row_count}\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    result = validate_city_file()\n    print(f\"CITY_GUARD_OK file={result['file']} rows={result['rows']}\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n"
> }
> ```

> {

>   "path": "14_evidence_gate.py",
>   "message": "Restore guarded pipeline runtime files",
>   "content": "\"\"\"Validate that the evidence output directory can be used by the pipeline.\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nEVIDENCE_DIR = Path(\"evidence-data\")\n\n\ndef ensure_evidence_dir(path: Path = EVIDENCE_DIR) -> dict[str, str]:\n    path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    if not path.is_dir():\n        raise NotADirectoryError(f\"Evidence path is not a directory: {path}\")\n\n    probe = path / \".write_check\"\n    probe.write_text(\"ok\", encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    probe.unlink()\n    return {\"directory\": str(path)}\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    result = ensure_evidence_dir()\n    print(f\"EVIDENCE_GATE_OK directory={result['directory']}\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n"
> }
> ```

> codex_apps.github_create_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "main",
>   "path": "04_filter_quality.py",
>   "message": "Restore guarded pipeline runtime files",
>   "content": "\"\"\"Minimal quality filter entrypoint for the guarded pipeline.\n\nThe real data source is expected in ``orte_deutschland.csv``. This step only\nchecks that the file is readable and reports how many rows are available.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport csv\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nINPUT_FILE = Path(\"orte_deutschland.csv\")\n\n\ndef inspect_input(path: Path = INPUT_FILE) -> dict[str, int | str]:\n    if not path.is_file():\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing input file: {path}\")\n\n    with path.open(\"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\", newline=\"\") as handle:\n        reader = csv.DictReader(handle)\n        if not reader.fieldnames:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Input file has no CSV header: {path}\")\n        row_count = sum(1 for _ in reader)\n\n    return {\"input\": str(path), \"rows\": row_count}\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    result = inspect_input()\n    print(f\"FILTER_QUALITY_OK input={result['input']} rows={result['rows']}\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n"
> }
> ```

> }

>
> codex_apps.github_create_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "main",
>   "path": "15_run_pipeline_guarded.py",
>   "message": "Restore guarded pipeline runtime files",
>   "content": "\"\"\"Guarded pipeline runner.\n\nThe runner fails before execution if any runtime file it depends on is absent.\nIt references only files that are part of this repository.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nREQUIRED_RUNTIME_FILES = (\n    \"04_filter_quality.py\",\n    \"05_run_pipeline.py\",\n    \"13_city_guard.py\",\n    \"14_evidence_gate.py\",\n    \"orte_deutschland.csv\",\n)\n\nGUARD_SCRIPTS = (\n    \"13_city_guard.py\",\n    \"14_evidence_gate.py\",\n)\n\nPIPELINE_SCRIPT = \"05_run_pipeline.py\"\n\n\ndef assert_runtime_files_exist(root: Path = Path(\".\")) -> None:\n    missing = [name for name in REQUIRED_RUNTIME_FILES if not (root / name).is_file()]\n    if missing:\n        missing_list = \", \".join(missing)\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing runtime dependency files: {missing_list}\")\n\n\ndef run_python_script(script_name: str) -> None:\n    completed = subprocess.run(\n        [sys.executable, script_name],\n        check=False,\n        cwd=Path(\".\"),\n    )\n    if completed.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"Pipeline step failed: {script_name} exit={completed.returncode}\")\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    assert_runtime_files_exist()\n    for script_name in GUARD_SCRIPTS:\n        run_python_script(script_name)\n    run_python_script(PIPELINE_SCRIPT)\n    print(\"PIPELINE_GUARDED_OK\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n"
> }
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "\"\"\"Minimal quality filter entrypoint for the guarded pipeline.\n\nThe real data source is expected in ``orte_deutschland.csv``. This step only\nchecks that the file is readable and reports how many rows are available.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport csv\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nINPUT_FILE = Path(\"orte_deutschland.csv\")\n\n\ndef inspect_input(path: Path = INPUT_FILE) -> dict[str, int | str]:\n    if not path.is_file():\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing input file: {path}\")\n\n    with path.open(\"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\", newline=\"\") as handle:\n        reader = csv.DictReader(handle)\n        if not reader.fieldnames:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Input file has no CSV header: {path}\")\n        row_count = sum(1 for _ in reader)\n\n    return {\"input\": str(path), \"rows\": row_count}\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    result = inspect_input()\n    print(f\"FILTER_QUALITY_OK input={result['input']} rows={result['rows']}\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "87d64b2d701810c52f94a05c77846cf3cf990142", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/04_filter_quality.py", "display_title": "04_filter_quality.py"}
> ```

Merged/fixed live on GitHub `main`.

What I did:
- No open PR existed to merge, and all `codex/*` branches were already behind or identical to `main`.
- Added the missing guarded pipeline runtime files directly to `main`:
  - `13_city_guard.py`
  - `14_evidence_gate.py`
  - `15_run_pipeline_guarded.py`
  - `05_run_pipeline.py`
  - `04_filter_quality.py`
  - `orte_deutschland.csv`

Latest live commit:
`1736a46e09b27add7725af1e28f154ec62d0847e`

Verified via GitHub tree that the requested files now exist at repo root. The guarded runner now references only files that are present in the repo.

One caveat: I could not run `python -m py_compile ...` locally because this shell has no `python`, `python3`, `py`, or `uv` in PATH. Also, `orte_deutschland.csv` is currently a header-only runtime placeholder, so real city data should still be restored for meaningful pipeline output.

> # Role
> You are a senior backend/platform engineer working on the repository:
>
> DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG
>
> # Important Naming Rule
> The project is called NorthAccessBFSG.
> Do NOT rename it.
> Do NOT introduce bfsg-auditor-pro anywhere.
> If old text mentions bfsg-auditor-pro, treat it as outdated wording and keep the repository/project name NorthAccessBFSG.
>
> # Current Goal
> Move the project from placeholder pipeline files toward a real Lead Discovery foundation.
>
> The current repo already has:
> - FastAPI
> - PostgreSQL
> - Redis/Celery
> - Playwright + axe scan worker
> - compliance mapping basics
> - evidence bundle storage
> - numbered guarded pipeline files:
>   - 04_filter_quality.py
>   - 05_run_pipeline.py
>   - 13_city_guard.py
>   - 14_evidence_gate.py
>   - 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py
>   - orte_deutschland.csv
>
> Problem:
> orte_deutschland.csv currently appears to be a weak placeholder or raw postal-code file and is not yet safely usable as a real city resolver source.
>
> # Data File Rule
> Use the provided orte_deutschland.csv as raw input.
>
> Preferred repo path:
>
> data/orte_deutschland.csv
>
> Important:
> The file uses semicolon delimiter and columns:
> - plz
> - stadt
>
> But the `stadt` column may contain both real city names and organization/special-recipient names.
> Do NOT assume every row is a valid city.
>
> Implement a resolver that:
> - reads semicolon-separated CSV
> - filters usable city rows
> - resolves Lübeck, Luebeck, and lubeck
> - ignores obvious organization/special-recipient rows when city name does not exactly match the requested city
> - fails if no usable city rows exist
> - fails if the CSV has only headers/no usable rows
> - prefers exact normalized city-name matches, not substring matches
> - keeps this file as raw data under data/orte_deutschland.csv
>
> Do NOT search by "contains Lübeck", because that would incorrectly return organizations or special recipients such as:
> - Stadtwerke Lübeck Gruppe GmbH
> - Finanzamt Lübeck
> - Agentur für Arbeit Lübeck
> - Deutsche Telekom AG Niederlassung Lübeck
>
> Only exact normalized city matches should be returned.
>
> # Task
> Implement a minimal but real Lead Discovery foundation for city/PLZ resolution.
>
> Do NOT build Google Maps API integration yet.
> Do NOT scrape websites.
> Do NOT build Playwright changes.
> Do NOT build reporting.
> Do NOT add legal claims.
>
> Build only the clean foundation that later allows:
>
> City input -> postal codes / region metadata -> discovery keywords -> later external provider call.
>
> # Required Work
>
> ## 1. Move/standardize city data
> Use the provided raw file as:
>
> data/orte_deutschland.csv
>
> Keep a backward-compatible root-level orte_deutschland.csv only if needed by the guarded pipeline, but prefer the canonical path:
>
> data/orte_deutschland.csv
>
> The guarded pipeline must not silently accept:
> - missing file
> - header-only file
> - zero usable city rows
> - unreadable CSV
>
> ## 2. Add city resolver module
> Create:
>
> app/discovery/place_resolver.py
>
> It should:
> - load data/orte_deutschland.csv
> - read semicolon-separated CSV
> - require columns plz and stadt
> - normalize city names case-insensitively
> - normalize German umlauts:
>   - ü -> ue
>   - ä -> ae
>   - ö -> oe
>   - ß -> ss
> - resolve "Lübeck", "Luebeck", and "lubeck" to the same city
> - return matching postal codes and metadata
> - return only exact normalized city matches
> - raise a clear error if no places are found
> - raise a clear error if the CSV has only headers/no usable rows
>
> Since the raw CSV only has plz and stadt, return at minimum:
> - city
> - postal_code
> - country = "DE"
>
> If state/latitude/longitude are not present, do NOT invent them.
>
> Example internal result object:
> {
>   "city": "Lübeck",
>   "postal_code": "23552",
>   "country": "DE"
> }
>
> ## 3. Add discovery keyword foundation
> Create:
>
> app/discovery/keywords.py
>
> It should define BFSG-relevant vertical keyword groups for later provider searches.
>
> Use structured groups, for example:
> - ecommerce
> - banking
> - transport
> - booking
> - healthcare_services
> - telecom
> - public_services_candidate
>
> Each group should contain:
> - group_id
> - label
> - keywords
> - bfsg_relevance_reason
> - enabled
>
> No external API calls yet.
>
> ## 4. Add API endpoints
> Create:
>
> app/api/discovery.py
>
> Add:
>
> GET /discovery/places/{city}
>
> Example response:
> {
>   "city": "Lübeck",
>   "matches": [
>     {
>       "postal_code": "23552",
>       "city": "Lübeck",
>       "country": "DE"
>     }
>   ]
> }
>
> Add:
>
> GET /discovery/keywords
>
> Returns the structured keyword groups.
>
> Wire the discovery router into app/main.py.
>
> ## 5. Add tests
> Add minimal pytest setup if missing.
>
> Tests must cover:
> - Lübeck resolves
> - Luebeck resolves
> - lubeck resolves
> - unknown city fails clearly
> - header-only CSV fails clearly
> - substring/special recipient behavior does not pollute exact city match results
> - keyword endpoint returns structured groups
> - discovery places endpoint returns matches for Lübeck
>
> Use TestClient for API endpoint tests if appropriate.
>
> ## 6. Update README
> Update README to document:
> - project name NorthAccessBFSG
> - canonical city data path: data/orte_deutschland.csv
> - how to run the discovery endpoints
> - that this is only resolver/keyword foundation
> - that this is NOT live Google Maps API integration yet
> - that this does NOT perform scraping
>
> Example curl commands:
>
> curl http://localhost:8000/health
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Lübeck
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords
>
> ## 7. Keep pipeline safe
> Update if needed:
> - 13_city_guard.py
> - 04_filter_quality.py
> - 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py
>
> The guarded pipeline must:
> - fail if required files are missing
> - fail if city CSV has no usable data rows
> - use data/orte_deutschland.csv as canonical source
> - not pretend an empty placeholder is valid
> - not rely on substring matching for city resolution
>
> ## 8. Do not overbuild
> Do NOT implement:
> - Google Maps API
> - external provider calls
> - Playwright changes
> - report generation
> - EARL export
> - PDF export
> - legal scoring
> - human review UI
> - aggressive scraping
> - broad refactors
>
> Keep this step small and reviewable.
>
> # Validation Commands
> Run or prepare for the developer to run:
>
> git status -sb
>
> find . -maxdepth 4 -type f | sort
>
> python -m py_compile \

> # Expected Output
> Return:
>
> 1. Changed files
> 2. What was added
> 3. Exact validation results
> 4. Any remaining risks or TODOs
> 5. Whether this is safe to merge into main
>
> # Merge Safety
> Only say "safe to merge" if:
> - tests pass
> - py_compile passes
> - guarded pipeline passes
> - discovery endpoints are wired into FastAPI
> - the city resolver fails clearly on missing/empty/bad data
> - no external API calls were added
> - project naming remains NorthAccessBFSG

> ```text
> {"content": "from collections.abc import AsyncIterator\nfrom contextlib import asynccontextmanager\n\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\n\nfrom app.api.compliance import router as compliance_router\nfrom app.api.evidence import router as evidence_router\nfrom app.api.health import router as health_router\nfrom app.api.scans import router as scans_router\nfrom app.core.config import settings\nfrom app.db.init_db import init_db\n\n\n@asynccontextmanager\nasync def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:\n    init_db()\n    yield\n\n\napp = FastAPI(\n    title=settings.app_name,\n    version=\"0.1.0\",\n    lifespan=lifespan,\n)\n\napp.include_router(health_router)\napp.include_router(scans_router)\napp.include_router(compliance_router)\napp.include_router(evidence_router)\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "20d90b9beb430efccd59e639e6a136cde7d3db93", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/main.py", "display_title": "main.py"}
> ```

> ```
>
> ````text
> {"content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nMinimal FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance.py\n    health.py\n    scans.py\n  compliance/\n    __init__.py\n    rule_engine.py\n    rules/\n      __init__.py\n      wcag_en_bfsg_map.json\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    browser_config.py\n    celery.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  models/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance_finding.py\n    finding.py\n    lead.py\n    scan.py\n  services/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance_service.py\n    scan_service.py\n  workers/\n    __init__.py\n    playwright_engine.py\n    scan_worker.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```text\nhttp://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the `leads`, `scans`, `findings`, and `compliance_findings` tables on startup.\n\n## Async Scan Flow\n\nCreate a lead directly in the database while a public lead API does not exist yet:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n```\n\nRun a scan for the lead:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nExpected immediate response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker launches Playwright headless Chromium, loads the lead domain, runs axe-core, captures evidence metadata, and persists raw findings.\n\nLifecycle:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nIf task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n\nInspect scan status and evidence metadata:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT id, status, error_message, evidence_metadata->>'current_url' AS current_url FROM scans;\"\n```\n\nInspect stored findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, severity, wcag_refs, confidence_score FROM findings;\"\n```\n\n## Compliance Mapping Flow\n\nRun deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"scan_id\": \"<scan-id>\",\n  \"mapping_version\": \"2026.05.08-001:<rules-hash>\",\n  \"total_findings\": 3,\n  \"mapped_findings\": 3,\n  \"critical_count\": 0,\n  \"high_count\": 2,\n  \"compliance_coverage_score\": 1.0\n}\n```\n\nCompliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\nInspect enriched compliance findings:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, normalized_severity, wcag_refs, en_refs, bfsg_refs, compliance_confidence_score FROM compliance_findings;\"\n```\n\nThe worker process can also be started independently:\n\n```bash\ncelery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info\n```\n\n## Browser Configuration\n\nThe worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n\n```text\nBROWSER_NAME=chromium\nBROWSER_HEADLESS=true\nBROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS=30000\nBROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS=10000\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH=1440\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=900\nBROWSER_RETRIES=1\nBROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL=networkidle\n```\n\nWebKit is supported by configuration but is not installed in the default image.\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "3372df2735fb0cf38cd46d7f137bd4481f690b7f", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/README.md", "display_title": "README.md"}
> ````

> ```text
> {"content": "\"\"\"Validate that the city input file exists and is readable.\n\nThis guard intentionally performs only structural checks. It does not invent or\nmodify city data.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport csv\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nCITY_FILE = Path(\"orte_deutschland.csv\")\n\n\ndef validate_city_file(path: Path = CITY_FILE) -> dict[str, int | str]:\n    if not path.is_file():\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing city source file: {path}\")\n\n    with path.open(\"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\", newline=\"\") as handle:\n        reader = csv.DictReader(handle)\n        if not reader.fieldnames:\n            raise ValueError(f\"City source file has no header: {path}\")\n        row_count = sum(1 for _ in reader)\n\n    return {\"file\": str(path), \"rows\": row_count}\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    result = validate_city_file()\n    print(f\"CITY_GUARD_OK file={result['file']} rows={result['rows']}\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "e08eef12a6b14e30c350533e0de9789573c77427", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/13_city_guard.py", "display_title": "13_city_guard.py"}
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "\"\"\"Minimal quality filter entrypoint for the guarded pipeline.\n\nThe real data source is expected in ``orte_deutschland.csv``. This step only\nchecks that the file is readable and reports how many rows are available.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport csv\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nINPUT_FILE = Path(\"orte_deutschland.csv\")\n\n\ndef inspect_input(path: Path = INPUT_FILE) -> dict[str, int | str]:\n    if not path.is_file():\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing input file: {path}\")\n\n    with path.open(\"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\", newline=\"\") as handle:\n        reader = csv.DictReader(handle)\n        if not reader.fieldnames:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Input file has no CSV header: {path}\")\n        row_count = sum(1 for _ in reader)\n\n    return {\"input\": str(path), \"rows\": row_count}\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    result = inspect_input()\n    print(f\"FILTER_QUALITY_OK input={result['input']} rows={result['rows']}\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "87d64b2d701810c52f94a05c77846cf3cf990142", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/04_filter_quality.py", "display_title": "04_filter_quality.py"}
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "\"\"\"Guarded pipeline runner.\n\nThe runner fails before execution if any runtime file it depends on is absent.\nIt references only files that are part of this repository.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nREQUIRED_RUNTIME_FILES = (\n    \"04_filter_quality.py\",\n    \"05_run_pipeline.py\",\n    \"13_city_guard.py\",\n    \"14_evidence_gate.py\",\n    \"orte_deutschland.csv\",\n)\n\nGUARD_SCRIPTS = (\n    \"13_city_guard.py\",\n    \"14_evidence_gate.py\",\n)\n\nPIPELINE_SCRIPT = \"05_run_pipeline.py\"\n\n\ndef assert_runtime_files_exist(root: Path = Path(\".\")) -> None:\n    missing = [name for name in REQUIRED_RUNTIME_FILES if not (root / name).is_file()]\n    if missing:\n        missing_list = \", \".join(missing)\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing runtime dependency files: {missing_list}\")\n\n\ndef run_python_script(script_name: str) -> None:\n    completed = subprocess.run(\n        [sys.executable, script_name],\n        check=False,\n        cwd=Path(\".\"),\n    )\n    if completed.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"Pipeline step failed: {script_name} exit={completed.returncode}\")\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    assert_runtime_files_exist()\n    for script_name in GUARD_SCRIPTS:\n        run_python_script(script_name)\n    run_python_script(PIPELINE_SCRIPT)\n    print(\"PIPELINE_GUARDED_OK\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "1798b893140bbff4fed9de34e6caf5c3cb43ae3b", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/15_run_pipeline_guarded.py", "display_title": "15_run_pipeline_guarded.py"}
> ```

>
> Die CSV ist tatsächlich eine gemischte Rohdatei: zuerst viele Sonderempfänger/Organisationen, später echte Orte. Deshalb baue ich den Resolver bewusst exact-match-basiert und lasse Guard/Tests gegen header-only und substring pollution prüfen.
>
> <details><summary>Called 3 tools</summary>
>
> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_fetch_file
>
> ```json

>   "path": "app/api/scans.py"
> }
> ```
>
> ```text
> {"content": "from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, status\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n\nfrom app.db.session import get_db\nfrom app.services.scan_service import LeadNotFoundError, ScanQueueError, create_scan_job\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/scans\", tags=[\"scans\"])\n\n\n@router.post(\"/run/{lead_id}\", status_code=status.HTTP_202_ACCEPTED)\ndef run_scan(lead_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, str]:\n    try:\n        scan = create_scan_job(db, lead_id)\n    except LeadNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=\"Lead not found\"\n        ) from exc\n    except ScanQueueError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=status.HTTP_503_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE,\n            detail=\"Scan queue is unavailable\",\n        ) from exc\n\n    return {\"scan_id\": scan.id}\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "6a117f6c4650106b984c95430ffd54a00e27c939", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/lead-discovery-foundation/app/api/scans.py", "display_title": "scans.py"}
> ```
>
> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_fetch_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "ref": "codex/lead-discovery-foundation",
>   "path": "app/core/config.py"
> }
> ```
>
> ```text
> {"content": "from functools import lru_cache\n\nfrom pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict\n\n\nclass Settings(BaseSettings):\n    app_name: str = \"NorthAccessBFSG API\"\n    environment: str = \"development\"\n    database_url: str = (\n        \"postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@localhost:5432/northaccessbfsg\"\n    )\n    celery_broker_url: str = \"redis://localhost:6379/0\"\n    celery_result_backend: str = \"redis://localhost:6379/1\"\n    browser_name: str = \"chromium\"\n    browser_headless: bool = True\n    browser_navigation_timeout_ms: int = 30_000\n    browser_action_timeout_ms: int = 10_000\n    browser_viewport_width: int = 1440\n    browser_viewport_height: int = 900\n    browser_retries: int = 1\n    browser_wait_until: str = \"networkidle\"\n    evidence_storage_backend: str = \"local\"\n    evidence_local_root: str = \"/app/evidence-data\"\n    evidence_s3_bucket: str = \"\"\n    evidence_s3_prefix: str = \"northaccessbfsg/evidence\"\n    evidence_s3_endpoint_url: str = \"\"\n    evidence_s3_region: str = \"eu-central-1\"\n\n    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(\n        env_file=\".env\",\n        env_file_encoding=\"utf-8\",\n        extra=\"ignore\",\n    )\n\n\n@lru_cache\ndef get_settings() -> Settings:\n    return Settings()\n\n\nsettings = get_settings()\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "90e24fc501c17f776a4b7d763fab6d23a30b1852", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/lead-discovery-foundation/app/core/config.py", "display_title": "config.py"}
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "services:\n  api:\n    build: .\n    environment:\n      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@db:5432/northaccessbfsg\n      ENVIRONMENT: development\n      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\n      CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND: redis://redis:6379/1\n      EVIDENCE_STORAGE_BACKEND: local\n      EVIDENCE_LOCAL_ROOT: /app/evidence-data\n    ports:\n      - \"8000:8000\"\n    volumes:\n      - evidence_data:/app/evidence-data\n    depends_on:\n      db:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      redis:\n        condition: service_healthy\n\n  worker:\n    build: .\n    command: celery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info\n    shm_size: \"1gb\"\n    environment:\n      DATABASE_URL: postgresql+psycopg2://northaccess:northaccess@db:5432/northaccessbfsg\n      ENVIRONMENT: development\n      CELERY_BROKER_URL: redis://redis:6379/0\n      CELERY_RESULT_BACKEND: redis://redis:6379/1\n      BROWSER_NAME: chromium\n      BROWSER_HEADLESS: \"true\"\n      BROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS: 30000\n      BROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS: 10000\n      BROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH: 1440\n      BROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT: 900\n      BROWSER_RETRIES: 1\n      BROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL: networkidle\n      EVIDENCE_STORAGE_BACKEND: local\n      EVIDENCE_LOCAL_ROOT: /app/evidence-data\n    volumes:\n      - evidence_data:/app/evidence-data\n    depends_on:\n      db:\n        condition: service_healthy\n      redis:\n        condition: service_healthy\n\n  db:\n    image: postgres:16-alpine\n    environment:\n      POSTGRES_USER: northaccess\n      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: northaccess\n      POSTGRES_DB: northaccessbfsg\n    ports:\n      - \"5432:5432\"\n    volumes:\n      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD-SHELL\", \"pg_isready -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg\"]\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 10\n\n  redis:\n    image: redis:7-alpine\n    ports:\n      - \"6379:6379\"\n    healthcheck:\n      test: [\"CMD\", \"redis-cli\", \"ping\"]\n      interval: 5s\n      timeout: 5s\n      retries: 10\n\nvolumes:\n  postgres_data:\n  evidence_data:\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "d47029c22f28ee20febbb311cdd969aad4aa9cb4", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/lead-discovery-foundation/docker-compose.yml", "display_title": "docker-compose.yml"}

>
> Ich habe jetzt genug Kontext. Die Änderungen bleiben eng: `app/discovery/*`, ein API-Router, Tests, README und die drei Guard-Dateien auf kanonische `data/orte_deutschland.csv`; keine Docker-/Worker-/Playwright-Änderungen.

> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/lead-discovery-foundation",
>   "path": "app/discovery/place_resolver.py",
>   "message": "Add lead discovery foundation",
>   "content": "\"\"\"City and postal-code resolution for lead discovery.\n\nThe resolver reads the raw Deutsche Post-style city data from\n``data/orte_deutschland.csv``. The source is treated as mixed raw input: rows may\ncontain real places, organizations, or special postal recipients. Resolution is\ntherefore exact after normalization and never substring-based.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport csv\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import asdict, dataclass\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nPROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\nDEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH = PROJECT_ROOT / \"data\" / \"orte_deutschland.csv\"\nREQUIRED_COLUMNS = {\"plz\", \"stadt\"}\nPOSTAL_CODE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"^\\d{5}$\")\nWHITESPACE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"\\s+\")\nUMLAUT_TRANSLATION = str.maketrans(\n    {\n        \"ä\": \"ae\",\n        \"ö\": \"oe\",\n        \"ü\": \"ue\",\n        \"ß\": \"ss\",\n    }\n)\n\n\nclass PlaceResolverError(ValueError):\n    \"\"\"Base error for place resolver failures.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass PlaceDataError(PlaceResolverError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when the configured city data file is missing or invalid.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass PlaceNotFoundError(PlaceResolverError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when a city cannot be resolved from usable city rows.\"\"\"\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass PlaceMatch:\n    city: str\n    postal_code: str\n    country: str = \"DE\"\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n        return asdict(self)\n\n\ndef normalize_city_name(value: str) -> str:\n    normalized = value.strip().casefold().translate(UMLAUT_TRANSLATION)\n    return WHITESPACE_PATTERN.sub(\" \", normalized)\n\n\ndef _loose_umlaut_key(value: str) -> str:\n    return value.replace(\"ae\", \"a\").replace(\"oe\", \"o\").replace(\"ue\", \"u\")\n\n\ndef _match_keys(value: str) -> set[str]:\n    normalized = normalize_city_name(value)\n    if not normalized:\n        return set()\n    return {normalized, _loose_umlaut_key(normalized)}\n\n\ndef _normalized_fieldnames(fieldnames: list[str] | None) -> dict[str, str]:\n    if not fieldnames:\n        return {}\n    return {name.strip().casefold(): name for name in fieldnames if name}\n\n\ndef _is_usable_row(postal_code: str, city: str) -> bool:\n    return bool(POSTAL_CODE_PATTERN.match(postal_code.strip()) and city.strip())\n\n\ndef load_places(data_path: Path | str = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH) -> list[PlaceMatch]:\n    path = Path(data_path)\n    if not path.is_file():\n        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is missing: {path}\")\n\n    try:\n        with path.open(\"r\", encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as handle:\n            reader = csv.DictReader(handle, delimiter=\";\")\n            fields = _normalized_fieldnames(reader.fieldnames)\n            missing_columns = REQUIRED_COLUMNS - set(fields)\n            if missing_columns:\n                missing = \", \".join(sorted(missing_columns))\n                raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is missing required columns: {missing}\")\n\n            postal_field = fields[\"plz\"]\n            city_field = fields[\"stadt\"]\n            places = []\n            seen = set()\n            for row in reader:\n                postal_code = (row.get(postal_field) or \"\").strip()\n                city = (row.get(city_field) or \"\").strip()\n                if not _is_usable_row(postal_code, city):\n                    continue\n\n                key = (postal_code, city)\n                if key in seen:\n                    continue\n                seen.add(key)\n                places.append(PlaceMatch(city=city, postal_code=postal_code))\n    except UnicodeDecodeError as exc:\n        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is not readable as UTF-8: {path}\") from exc\n    except csv.Error as exc:\n        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is not readable CSV: {path}\") from exc\n\n    if not places:\n        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file has no usable city rows: {path}\")\n\n    return places\n\n\ndef resolve_city(city: str, data_path: Path | str = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH) -> list[PlaceMatch]:\n    requested_keys = _match_keys(city)\n    if not requested_keys:\n        raise PlaceNotFoundError(\"No city name provided\")\n\n    matches = [\n        place\n        for place in load_places(data_path)\n        if _match_keys(place.city) & requested_keys\n    ]\n    matches.sort(key=lambda place: (place.postal_code, place.city))\n\n    if not matches:\n        raise PlaceNotFoundError(f\"No places found for city: {city}\")\n\n    return matches\n"
> }
> ```

>   "message": "Add lead discovery foundation",
>   "content": "\"\"\"BFSG-relevant keyword groups for future lead discovery provider searches.\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom copy import deepcopy\n\nKEYWORD_GROUPS = [\n    {\n        \"group_id\": \"ecommerce\",\n        \"label\": \"E-Commerce\",\n        \"keywords\": [\"online shop\", \"webshop\", \"e-commerce\", \"versandhandel\"],\n        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Online retail services can fall within BFSG-relevant consumer-facing digital services.\",\n        \"enabled\": True,\n    },\n    {\n        \"group_id\": \"banking\",\n        \"label\": \"Banking\",\n        \"keywords\": [\"bank\", \"sparkasse\", \"kredit\", \"online banking\"],\n        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Banking and financial self-service channels are a core BFSG-relevant service area.\",\n        \"enabled\": True,\n    },\n    {\n        \"group_id\": \"transport\",\n        \"label\": \"Transport\",\n        \"keywords\": [\"verkehrsbetriebe\", \"bahn\", \"bus\", \"ticket\"],\n        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Passenger transport information and ticketing services are relevant for accessibility discovery.\",\n        \"enabled\": True,\n    },\n    {\n        \"group_id\": \"booking\",\n        \"label\": \"Booking\",\n        \"keywords\": [\"buchung\", \"reservierung\", \"hotel\", \"reise\"],\n        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Booking and reservation services can be consumer-facing digital transaction services.\",\n        \"enabled\": True,\n    },\n    {\n        \"group_id\": \"healthcare_services\",\n        \"label\": \"Healthcare Services\",\n        \"keywords\": [\"terminbuchung\", \"arzt\", \"klinik\", \"apotheke\"],\n        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Healthcare service portals are useful candidates for later accessibility audit discovery.\",\n        \"enabled\": True,\n    },\n    {\n        \"group_id\": \"telecom\",\n        \"label\": \"Telecom\",\n        \"keywords\": [\"telekommunikation\", \"internet\", \"mobilfunk\", \"dsl\"],\n        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Telecom providers commonly operate BFSG-relevant consumer portals and ordering flows.\",\n        \"enabled\": True,\n    },\n    {\n        \"group_id\": \"public_services_candidate\",\n        \"label\": \"Public Services Candidate\",\n        \"keywords\": [\"stadtportal\", \"buergerdienst\", \"terminvereinbarung\", \"verwaltung\"],\n        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Public service portals are discovery candidates, while legal applicability must be assessed later.\",\n        \"enabled\": True,\n    },\n]\n\n\ndef get_keyword_groups() -> list[dict[str, object]]:\n    return deepcopy(KEYWORD_GROUPS)\n"
> }

> codex_apps.github_create_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/lead-discovery-foundation",
>   "path": "app/api/discovery.py",
>   "message": "Add lead discovery foundation",
>   "content": "from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException, status\n\nfrom app.discovery.keywords import get_keyword_groups\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import PlaceDataError, PlaceNotFoundError, resolve_city\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/discovery\", tags=[\"discovery\"])\n\n\n@router.get(\"/places/{city}\")\ndef get_places(city: str) -> dict[str, object]:\n    try:\n        matches = resolve_city(city)\n    except PlaceNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n    except PlaceDataError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            detail=str(exc),\n        ) from exc\n\n    return {\"city\": city, \"matches\": [match.to_dict() for match in matches]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/keywords\")\ndef get_keywords() -> dict[str, object]:\n    return {\"groups\": get_keyword_groups()}\n"
> }
> ```

> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/lead-discovery-foundation",
>   "path": "app/main.py",
>   "sha": "20d90b9beb430efccd59e639e6a136cde7d3db93",
>   "message": "Wire discovery API router",
>   "content": "from collections.abc import AsyncIterator\nfrom contextlib import asynccontextmanager\n\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\n\nfrom app.api.compliance import router as compliance_router\nfrom app.api.discovery import router as discovery_router\nfrom app.api.evidence import router as evidence_router\nfrom app.api.health import router as health_router\nfrom app.api.scans import router as scans_router\nfrom app.core.config import settings\nfrom app.db.init_db import init_db\n\n\n@asynccontextmanager\nasync def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:\n    init_db()\n    yield\n\n\napp = FastAPI(\n    title=settings.app_name,\n    version=\"0.1.0\",\n    lifespan=lifespan,\n)\n\napp.include_router(health_router)\napp.include_router(scans_router)\napp.include_router(compliance_router)\napp.include_router(evidence_router)\napp.include_router(discovery_router)\n"
> }
> ```

>   "message": "Use canonical city data in guarded pipeline",
>   "content": "\"\"\"Validate that the canonical city input file exists and has usable rows.\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH, PlaceDataError, load_places\n\n\nCITY_FILE = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH\n\n\ndef validate_city_file() -> dict[str, int | str]:\n    try:\n        places = load_places(CITY_FILE)\n    except PlaceDataError:\n        raise\n\n    return {\"file\": str(CITY_FILE), \"rows\": len(places)}\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    result = validate_city_file()\n    print(f\"CITY_GUARD_OK file={result['file']} usable_rows={result['rows']}\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n"
> }

> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/lead-discovery-foundation",
>   "path": "04_filter_quality.py",
>   "sha": "87d64b2d701810c52f94a05c77846cf3cf990142",
>   "message": "Use canonical city data in guarded pipeline",
>   "content": "\"\"\"Minimal quality filter entrypoint for the guarded pipeline.\n\nThe real data source is expected at ``data/orte_deutschland.csv``. This step\nchecks that the raw semicolon-separated CSV is readable and contains usable\nrows. It does not perform substring-based city matching.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH, PlaceDataError, load_places\n\n\nINPUT_FILE = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH\n\n\ndef inspect_input() -> dict[str, int | str]:\n    try:\n        places = load_places(INPUT_FILE)\n    except PlaceDataError:\n        raise\n\n    return {\"input\": str(INPUT_FILE), \"usable_rows\": len(places)}\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    result = inspect_input()\n    print(f\"FILTER_QUALITY_OK input={result['input']} usable_rows={result['usable_rows']}\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n"
> }
> ```

>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/lead-discovery-foundation",
>   "path": "15_run_pipeline_guarded.py",
>   "sha": "1798b893140bbff4fed9de34e6caf5c3cb43ae3b",
>   "message": "Use canonical city data in guarded pipeline",
>   "content": "\"\"\"Guarded pipeline runner.\n\nThe runner fails before execution if any runtime file it depends on is absent.\nIt references only files that are part of this repository and validates the\ncanonical raw city data before running the pipeline step.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nREQUIRED_RUNTIME_FILES = (\n    \"04_filter_quality.py\",\n    \"05_run_pipeline.py\",\n    \"13_city_guard.py\",\n    \"14_evidence_gate.py\",\n    \"data/orte_deutschland.csv\",\n)\n\nGUARD_SCRIPTS = (\n    \"13_city_guard.py\",\n    \"14_evidence_gate.py\",\n)\n\nPIPELINE_SCRIPT = \"05_run_pipeline.py\"\n\n\ndef assert_runtime_files_exist(root: Path = Path(\".\")) -> None:\n    missing = [name for name in REQUIRED_RUNTIME_FILES if not (root / name).is_file()]\n    if missing:\n        missing_list = \", \".join(missing)\n        raise FileNotFoundError(f\"Missing runtime dependency files: {missing_list}\")\n\n\ndef run_python_script(script_name: str) -> None:\n    completed = subprocess.run(\n        [sys.executable, script_name],\n        check=False,\n        cwd=Path(\".\"),\n    )\n    if completed.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(f\"Pipeline step failed: {script_name} exit={completed.returncode}\")\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    assert_runtime_files_exist()\n    for script_name in GUARD_SCRIPTS:\n        run_python_script(script_name)\n    run_python_script(PIPELINE_SCRIPT)\n    print(\"PIPELINE_GUARDED_OK\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n"
> }
> ```

> Die Implementierung ist im Branch. Jetzt lege ich die Tests an, mit kleinen Fixture-CSV-Dateien für die Randfälle; der API-Test nutzt einen isolierten FastAPI-Router, damit keine Postgres-Startup-Abhängigkeit die Discovery-Tests verfälscht.

> <details><summary>Called 3 tools</summary>

> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/lead-discovery-foundation",
>   "path": "tests/test_place_resolver.py",
>   "message": "Add discovery tests",
>   "content": "from pathlib import Path\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import (\n    PlaceDataError,\n    PlaceNotFoundError,\n    load_places,\n    resolve_city,\n)\n\n\ndef write_city_csv(path: Path, content: str) -> Path:\n    path.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    return path\n\n\ndef test_luebeck_umlaut_resolves_from_canonical_data() -> None:\n    matches = resolve_city(\"Lübeck\")\n\n    assert matches\n    assert {match.city for match in matches} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n    assert any(match.postal_code == \"23552\" for match in matches)\n    assert all(match.country == \"DE\" for match in matches)\n\n\ndef test_luebeck_transliteration_resolves_from_canonical_data() -> None:\n    matches = resolve_city(\"Luebeck\")\n\n    assert matches\n    assert {match.city for match in matches} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n\n\ndef test_lubeck_plain_ascii_resolves_from_canonical_data() -> None:\n    matches = resolve_city(\"lubeck\")\n\n    assert matches\n    assert {match.city for match in matches} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n\n\ndef test_unknown_city_fails_clearly(tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n    data_file = write_city_csv(tmp_path / \"places.csv\", \"plz;stadt\\n23552;Lübeck\\n\")\n\n    with pytest.raises(PlaceNotFoundError, match=\"No places found for city: Atlantis\"):\n        resolve_city(\"Atlantis\", data_file)\n\n\ndef test_header_only_csv_fails_clearly(tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n    data_file = write_city_csv(tmp_path / \"places.csv\", \"plz;stadt\\n\")\n\n    with pytest.raises(PlaceDataError, match=\"no usable city rows\"):\n        load_places(data_file)\n\n\ndef test_substring_special_recipients_do_not_pollute_exact_city_match(tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n    data_file = write_city_csv(\n        tmp_path / \"places.csv\",\n        \"plz;stadt\\n\"\n        \"23552;Lübeck\\n\"\n        \"23560;Stadtwerke Lübeck Gruppe GmbH\\n\"\n        \"23558;Finanzamt Lübeck\\n\"\n        \"23556;Agentur für Arbeit Lübeck\\n\",\n    )\n\n    matches = resolve_city(\"Luebeck\", data_file)\n\n    assert [match.city for match in matches] == [\"Lübeck\"]\n    assert [match.postal_code for match in matches] == [\"23552\"]\n"
> }
> ```

> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/lead-discovery-foundation",
>   "path": "tests/test_discovery_api.py",
>   "message": "Add discovery tests",
>   "content": "from fastapi import FastAPI\nfrom fastapi.testclient import TestClient\n\nfrom app.api.discovery import router as discovery_router\n\n\ndef make_client() -> TestClient:\n    app = FastAPI()\n    app.include_router(discovery_router)\n    return TestClient(app)\n\n\ndef test_keywords_endpoint_returns_structured_groups() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/keywords\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    groups = response.json()[\"groups\"]\n    assert groups\n    assert {\"group_id\", \"label\", \"keywords\", \"bfsg_relevance_reason\", \"enabled\"}.issubset(\n        groups[0]\n    )\n    assert \"ecommerce\" in {group[\"group_id\"] for group in groups}\n\n\ndef test_places_endpoint_returns_luebeck_matches() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/Lübeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    payload = response.json()\n    assert payload[\"city\"] == \"Lübeck\"\n    assert payload[\"matches\"]\n    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in payload[\"matches\"]} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n    assert any(match[\"postal_code\"] == \"23552\" for match in payload[\"matches\"])\n\n\ndef test_places_endpoint_accepts_luebeck_transliteration() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/Luebeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in response.json()[\"matches\"]} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n"
> }
> ```

> codex_apps.github_update_file
>
> ````json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/lead-discovery-foundation",
>   "path": "README.md",
>   "sha": "3372df2735fb0cf38cd46d7f137bd4481f690b7f",
>   "message": "Document lead discovery foundation",
>   "content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nNorthAccessBFSG is a FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\nThe current scope includes backend infrastructure, asynchronous scans, raw accessibility findings, compliance mapping basics, evidence bundle storage, and a minimal Lead Discovery foundation for city and keyword resolution.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance.py\n    discovery.py\n    evidence.py\n    health.py\n    scans.py\n  compliance/\n    __init__.py\n    rule_engine.py\n    rules/\n      __init__.py\n      wcag_en_bfsg_map.json\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    browser_config.py\n    celery.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  discovery/\n    __init__.py\n    keywords.py\n    place_resolver.py\n  evidence/\n    __init__.py\n    hashing.py\n    storage.py\n    storage_backend.py\n  models/\n  services/\n  workers/\ndata/\n  orte_deutschland.csv\n04_filter_quality.py\n05_run_pipeline.py\n13_city_guard.py\n14_evidence_gate.py\n15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the database tables on startup.\n\n## Lead Discovery Foundation\n\nThe canonical raw city data file is:\n\n```text\ndata/orte_deutschland.csv\n```\n\nThe file is semicolon-separated and must contain these columns:\n\n```text\nplz;stadt\n```\n\nThe resolver treats this file as raw input. The `stadt` column may contain real city names as well as organizations or special postal recipients, so city resolution uses exact normalized city-name matching only. It does not use substring matching.\n\nSupported normalization includes case-insensitive matching and German umlaut handling:\n\n```text\nLübeck -> Luebeck -> lubeck\n```\n\nDiscovery endpoints:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Lübeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/lubeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords\n```\n\nExample place response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n  \"matches\": [\n    {\n      \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n      \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n      \"country\": \"DE\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\nThis is only the resolver and keyword foundation for later lead discovery. It is not a live Google Maps API integration. It does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers.\n\n## Guarded Pipeline\n\nThe numbered guarded pipeline files validate that runtime dependencies are present and that the canonical city CSV is usable:\n\n```bash\npython -m py_compile \\\n  04_filter_quality.py \\\n  05_run_pipeline.py \\\n  13_city_guard.py \\\n  14_evidence_gate.py \\\n  15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n\npython 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n```\n\nThe guarded runner fails if `data/orte_deutschland.csv` is missing, unreadable, missing required columns, header-only, or contains zero usable rows.\n\n## Async Scan Flow\n\nCreate a lead directly in the database while a public lead API does not exist yet:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n```\n\nRun a scan for the lead:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nExpected immediate response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker launches Playwright headless Chromium, loads the lead domain, runs axe-core, captures evidence metadata, and persists raw findings.\n\nLifecycle:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nIf task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n\n## Compliance Mapping Flow\n\nRun deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nCompliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Browser Configuration\n\nThe worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n\n```text\nBROWSER_NAME=chromium\nBROWSER_HEADLESS=true\nBROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS=30000\nBROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS=10000\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH=1440\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=900\nBROWSER_RETRIES=1\nBROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL=networkidle\n```\n\nWebKit is supported by configuration but is not installed in the default image.\n"
> }
> ````

> {"content": "from collections.abc import AsyncIterator\nfrom contextlib import asynccontextmanager\n\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\n\nfrom app.api.compliance import router as compliance_router\nfrom app.api.discovery import router as discovery_router\nfrom app.api.evidence import router as evidence_router\nfrom app.api.health import router as health_router\nfrom app.api.scans import router as scans_router\nfrom app.core.config import settings\nfrom app.db.init_db import init_db\n\n\n@asynccontextmanager\nasync def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:\n    init_db()\n    yield\n\n\napp = FastAPI(\n    title=settings.app_name,\n    version=\"0.1.0\",\n    lifespan=lifespan,\n)\n\napp.include_router(health_router)\napp.include_router(scans_router)\napp.include_router(compliance_router)\napp.include_router(evidence_router)\napp.include_router(discovery_router)\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "15aa9c4d57c35ffd75a9e3b818811eb274a9c044", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/lead-discovery-foundation/app/main.py", "display_title": "main.py"}

> {"content": "\"\"\"Validate that the canonical city input file exists and has usable rows.\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH, PlaceDataError, load_places\n\n\nCITY_FILE = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH\n\n\ndef validate_city_file() -> dict[str, int | str]:\n    try:\n        places = load_places(CITY_FILE)\n    except PlaceDataError:\n        raise\n\n    return {\"file\": str(CITY_FILE), \"rows\": len(places)}\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    result = validate_city_file()\n    print(f\"CITY_GUARD_OK file={result['file']} usable_rows={result['rows']}\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "1634a4bedf48fc37ef32afde7aaa5d9e2e7c77ea", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/lead-discovery-foundation/13_city_guard.py", "display_title": "13_city_guard.py"}

> ```text
> {"content": "\"\"\"Minimal quality filter entrypoint for the guarded pipeline.\n\nThe real data source is expected at ``data/orte_deutschland.csv``. This step\nchecks that the raw semicolon-separated CSV is readable and contains usable\nrows. It does not perform substring-based city matching.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH, PlaceDataError, load_places\n\n\nINPUT_FILE = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH\n\n\ndef inspect_input() -> dict[str, int | str]:\n    try:\n        places = load_places(INPUT_FILE)\n    except PlaceDataError:\n        raise\n\n    return {\"input\": str(INPUT_FILE), \"usable_rows\": len(places)}\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    result = inspect_input()\n    print(f\"FILTER_QUALITY_OK input={result['input']} usable_rows={result['usable_rows']}\")\n    return 0\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    raise SystemExit(main())\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "773b2dcdb9177e2e6c0a97d056086d4c942093e6", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/lead-discovery-foundation/04_filter_quality.py", "display_title": "04_filter_quality.py"}

> Bug gefunden: /health funktioniert, /discovery/keywords funktioniert, aber /discovery/places/* schlägt im Container fehl:
>
> City data file is missing: /app/data/orte_deutschland.csv
>
> Ursache sehr wahrscheinlich:
> Dockerfile kopiert nur app/ nach /app, aber nicht data/.
>
> Bitte minimal fixen:
> - Dockerfile so anpassen, dass data/orte_deutschland.csv ins Image kopiert wird.
> - Wahrscheinlich reicht:
>   COPY data ./data
> - Keine neuen Features.
> - Keine Resolver-Logik ändern, außer zwingend nötig.
> - Kein Rename.
> - Projektname bleibt NorthAccessBFSG.
>
> Danach bitte validieren:
>
> docker compose up --build
>
> Dann:
>
> curl http://localhost:8000/health
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Lübeck
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/lubeck
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords
>
> Erwartung:
> - /health => {"status":"ok"}
> - alle drei places-Routen liefern Lübeck-Matches
> - keywords liefert Gruppen
> - keine "City data file is missing" Meldung mehr
>
> Bitte danach nur ausgeben:
> 1. geänderte Dateien
> 2. Dockerfile-Diff
> 3. Validierungsergebnisse
> 4. safe to merge: yes/no

> # Role
> You are a senior backend/platform engineer working on:
>
> DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG
>
> # Project Naming Rule
> The project is called NorthAccessBFSG.
> Do NOT introduce bfsg-auditor-pro.
> Do NOT rename the project.
>
> # Base
> Use the current main branch as the base.
> Create a new branch for this task.
>
> # Current State
> main is validated and contains:
> - FastAPI
> - PostgreSQL
> - Redis/Celery
> - Playwright + axe worker
> - compliance mapping basics
> - evidence bundle storage
> - discovery place resolver:
>   - data/orte_deutschland.csv
>   - app/discovery/place_resolver.py
>   - app/discovery/keywords.py
>   - app/api/discovery.py
> - validated endpoints:
>   - /discovery/places/Lübeck
>   - /discovery/places/Luebeck
>   - /discovery/places/lubeck
>   - /discovery/keywords
>
> Latest local validation on main:
> - pytest: 9 passed
> - guarded pipeline: passed
> - usable city rows: 10813
>
> # Goal
> Build the next minimal Lead Discovery foundation step:
>
> City + keyword groups -> deterministic search query plan -> persisted discovery run + lead candidates.
>
> Do NOT call Google Maps API yet.
> Do NOT scrape websites.
> Do NOT add Playwright changes.
> Do NOT add reporting.
> Do NOT add legal conclusions.
> Do NOT overbuild.
>
> This step is only the internal planner/storage foundation before adding a real external provider.
>
> # Required Work
>
> ## 1. Add database models
>
> Create models:
>
> ### DiscoveryRun
> Fields:
> - id
> - city
> - normalized_city
> - status: pending/running/done/failed
> - keyword_groups JSON
> - postal_codes JSON
> - query_plan JSON
> - created_at
> - completed_at nullable
> - error_message nullable
>
> ### LeadCandidate
> Fields:
> - id
> - discovery_run_id FK
> - source
> - source_reference nullable
> - company_name nullable
> - domain nullable
> - city nullable
> - postal_code nullable
> - address nullable
> - phone nullable
> - category nullable
> - raw_data JSON
> - confidence_score nullable
> - created_at
>
> Important:
> - This is candidate data, not confirmed legal applicability.
> - Do not overclaim BFSG violations.
>
> Wire models into:
> - app/models/__init__.py
> - app/db/init_db.py
>
> Use SQLAlchemy style consistent with the existing codebase.
>
> ## 2. Add query planner module
>
> Create:
>
> app/discovery/query_planner.py
>
> It should:
> - accept city input
> - resolve city via existing resolve_city()
> - get enabled keyword groups via get_keyword_groups()
> - build deterministic query plan entries
>
> Each query plan entry should include:
> - city
> - postal_code
> - keyword_group_id
> - keyword
> - query_text
>
> Example query_text:
> "Lübeck 23552 online shop"
> "Lübeck 23552 bank"
> "Lübeck 23552 terminbuchung"
>
> Important:
> - deterministic ordering
> - no external calls
> - no scraping
> - no randomness
>
> ## 3. Add discovery service
>
> Create:
>
> app/services/discovery_service.py
>
> It should:
> - create a DiscoveryRun
> - store resolved postal codes
> - store enabled keyword groups
> - store query_plan
> - create initial LeadCandidate rows only if explicitly provided by a provider later
>
> For this step:
> - no provider execution
> - no external API
> - just store the query plan
>
> Expose a function:
> create_discovery_run(db, city: str) -> DiscoveryRun
>
> ## 4. Add API endpoints
>
> Extend app/api/discovery.py or create clean endpoints under the same router:
>

> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords

> Then create discovery run:
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/Lübeck

> Use returned discovery_run_id:
>
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates
>

> - No reporting.

> - No broad refactor.

Foundation\n+\n+The canonical raw city data file is:\n+\n+```text\n+data/orte_deutschland.csv\n+```\n+\n+The file is semicolon-separated and must contain these columns:\n+\n+```text\n+plz;stadt\n+```\n+\n+The resolver treats this file as raw input. The `stadt` column may contain real city names as well as organizations or special postal recipients, so city resolution uses exact normalized city-name matching only. It does not use substring matching.\n+\n+Supported normalization includes case-insensitive matching and German umlaut handling:\n+\n+```text\n+L\u00fcbeck -> Luebeck -> lubeck\n+```\n+\n+Discovery endpoints:\n+\n+```bash\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/L\u00fcbeck\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/lubeck\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords\n+```\n+\n+Example place response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n+  \"matches\": [\n+    {\n+      \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n+      \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n+      \"country\": \"DE\"\n+    }\n+  ]\n+}\n+```\n+\n+This is only the resolver and keyword foundation for later lead discovery. It is not a live Google Maps API integration. It does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers.\n+\n+## Guarded Pipeline\n+\n+The numbered guarded pipeline files validate that runtime dependencies are present and that the canonical city CSV is usable:\n+\n+```bash\n+python -m py_compile \\\n+  04_filter_quality.py \\\n+  05_run_pipeline.py \\\n+  13_city_guard.py \\\n+  14_evidence_gate.py \\\n+  15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n+\n+python 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n+```\n+\n+The guarded runner fails if `data/orte_deutschland.csv` is missing, unreadable, missing required columns, header-only, or contains zero usable rows.\n \n ## Async Scan Flow\n \n@@ -99,18 +173,6 @@ pending -> running -> processing -> done\n \n If task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n \n-Inspect scan status and evidence metadata:\n-\n-```bash\n-docker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT id, status, error_message, evidence_metadata->>'current_url' AS current_url FROM scans;\"\n-```\n-\n-Inspect stored findings:\n-\n-```bash\n-docker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, severity, wcag_refs, confidence_score FROM findings;\"\n-```\n-\n ## Compliance Mapping Flow\n \n Run deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n@@ -119,34 +181,8 @@ Run deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n ```\n \n-Expected response:\n-\n-```json\n-{\n-  \"scan_id\": \"<scan-id>\",\n-  \"mapping_version\": \"2026.05.08-001:<rules-hash>\",\n-  \"total_findings\": 3,\n-  \"mapped_findings\": 3,\n-  \"critical_count\": 0,\n-  \"high_count\": 2,\n-  \"compliance_coverage_score\": 1.0\n-}\n-```\n-\n Compliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@\n+from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException, status\n+\n+from app.discovery.keywords import get_keyword_groups\n+from app.discovery.place_resolver import PlaceDataError, PlaceNotFoundError, resolve_city\n+\n+router = APIRouter(prefix=\"/discovery\", tags=[\"discovery\"])\n+\n+\n+@router.get(\"/places/{city}\")\n+def get_places(city: str) -> dict[str, object]:\n+    try:\n+        matches = resolve_city(city)\n+    except PlaceNotFoundError as exc:\n+        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n+    except PlaceDataError as exc:\n+        raise HTTPException(\n+            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n+            detail=str(exc),\n+        ) from exc\n+\n+    return {\"city\": city, \"matches\": [match.to_dict() for match in matches]}\n+\n+\n+@router.get(\"/keywords\")\n+def get_keywords() -> dict[str, object]:\n+    return {\"groups\": get_keyword_groups()}\n@@ -0,0 +1 @@\n+\"\"\"Lead discovery foundations for place resolution and search keywords.\"\"\"\n@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@\n+\"\"\"BFSG-relevant keyword groups for future lead discovery provider searches.\"\"\"\n+\n+from __future__ import annotations\n+\n+from copy import deepcopy\n+\n+KEYWORD_GROUPS = [\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"ecommerce\",\n+        \"label\": \"E-Commerce\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"online shop\", \"webshop\", \"e-commerce\", \"versandhandel\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Online retail services can fall within BFSG-relevant consumer-facing digital services.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"banking\",\n+        \"label\": \"Banking\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"bank\", \"sparkasse\", \"kredit\", \"online banking\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Banking and financial self-service channels are a core BFSG-relevant service area.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"transport\",\n+        \"label\": \"Transport\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"verkehrsbetriebe\", \"bahn\", \"bus\", \"ticket\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Passenger transport information and ticketing services are relevant for accessibility discovery.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"booking\",\n+        \"label\": \"Booking\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"buchung\", \"reservierung\", \"hotel\", \"reise\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Booking and reservation services can be consumer-facing digital transaction services.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"healthcare_services\",\n+        \"label\": \"Healthcare Services\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"terminbuchung\", \"arzt\", \"klinik\", \"apotheke\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Healthcare service portals are useful candidates for later accessibility audit discovery.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"telecom\",\n+        \"label\": \"Telecom\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"telekommunikation\", \"internet\", \"mobilfunk\", \"dsl\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Telecom providers commonly operate BFSG-relevant consumer portals and ordering flows.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"public_services_candidate\",\n+        \"label\": \"Public Services Candidate\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"stadtportal\", \"buergerdienst\", \"terminvereinbarung\", \"verwaltung\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Public service portals are discovery candidates, while legal applicability must be assessed later.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+]\n+\n+\n+def get_keyword_groups() -> list[dict[str, object]]:\n+    return deepcopy(KEYWORD_GROUPS)\n@@ -0,0 +1,134 @@\n+\"\"\"City and postal-code resolution for lead discovery.\n+\n+The resolver reads the raw Deutsche Post-style city data from\n+``data/orte_deutschland.csv``. The source is treated as mixed raw input: rows may\n+contain real places, organizations, or special postal recipients. Resolution is\n+therefore exact after normalization and never substring-based.\n+\"\"\"\n+\n+from __future__ import annotations\n+\n+import csv\n+import re\n+from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass\n+from pathlib import Path\n+\n+PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\n+DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH = PROJECT_ROOT / \"data\" / \"orte_deutschland.csv\"\n+REQUIRED_COLUMNS = {\"plz\", \"stadt\"}\n+POSTAL_CODE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"^\\d{5}$\")\n+WHITESPACE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"\\s+\")\n+UMLAUT_TRANSLATION = str.maketrans(\n+    {\n+        \"\u00e4\": \"ae\",\n+        \"\u00f6\": \"oe\",\n+        \"\u00fc\": \"ue\",\n+        \"\u00df\": \"ss\",\n+    }\n+)\n+\n+\n+class PlaceResolverError(ValueError):\n+    \"\"\"Base error for place resolver failures.\"\"\"\n+\n+\n+class PlaceDataError(PlaceResolverError):\n+    \"\"\"Raised when the configured city data file is missing or invalid.\"\"\"\n+\n+\n+class PlaceNotFoundError(PlaceResolverError):\n+    \"\"\"Raised when a city cannot be resolved from usable city rows.\"\"\"\n+\n+\n+@dataclass(frozen=True)\n+class PlaceMatch:\n+    city: str\n+    postal_code: str\n+    country: str = \"DE\"\n+\n+    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n+        return asdict(self)\n+\n+\n+def normalize_city_name(value: str) -> str:\n+    normalized = value.strip().casefold().translate(UMLAUT_TRANSLATION)\n+    return WHITESPACE_PATTERN.sub(\" \", normalized)\n+\n+\n+def _loose_umlaut_key(value: str) -> str:\n+    return value.replace(\"ae\", \"a\").replace(\"oe\", \"o\").replace(\"ue\", \"u\")\n+\n+\n+def _match_keys(value: str) -> set[str]:\n+    normalized = normalize_city_name(value)\n+    if not normalized:\n+        return set()\n+    return {normalized, _loose_umlaut_key(normalized)}\n+\n+\n+def _normalized_fieldnames(fieldnames: list[str] | None) -> dict[str, str]:\n+    if not fieldnames:\n+        return {}\n+    return {name.strip().casefold(): name for name in fieldnames if name}\n+\n+\n+def _is_usable_row(postal_code: str, city: str) -> bool:\n+    return bool(POSTAL_CODE_PATTERN.match(postal_code.strip()) and city.strip())\n+\n+\n+def load_places(data_path: Path | str = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH) -> list[PlaceMatch]:\n+    path = Path(data_path)\n+    if not path.is_file():\n+        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is missing: {path}\")\n+\n+    try:\n+        with path.open(\"r\", encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as handle:\n+            reader = csv.DictReader(handle, delimiter=\";\")\n+            fields = _normalized_fieldnames(reader.fieldnames)\n+            missing_columns = REQUIRED_COLUMNS - set(fields)\n+            if missing_columns:\n+                missing = \", \".join(sorted(missing_columns))\n+                raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is missing required columns: {missing}\")\n+\n+            postal_field = fields[\"plz\"]\n+            city_field = fields[\"stadt\"]\n+            places = []\n+            seen = set()\n+            for row in reader:\n+                postal_code = (row.get(postal_field) or \"\").strip()\n+                city = (row.get(city_field) or \"\").strip()\n+                if not _is_usable_row(postal_code, city):\n+                    continue\n+\n+                key = (postal_code, city)\n+                if key in seen:\n+                    continue\n+                seen.add(key)\n+                places.append(PlaceMatch(city=city, postal_code=postal_code))\n+    except UnicodeDecodeError as exc:\n+        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is not readable as UTF-8: {path}\") from exc\n+    except csv.Error as exc:\n+        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is not readable CSV: {path}\") from exc\n+\n+    if not places:\n+        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file has no usable city rows: {path}\")\n+\n+    return places\n+\n+\n+def resolve_city(city: str, data_path: Path | str = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH) -> list[PlaceMatch]:\n+    requested_keys = _match_keys(city)\n+    if not requested_keys:\n+        raise PlaceNotFoundError(\"No city name provided\")\n+\n+    matches = [\n+        place\n+        for place in load_places(data_path)\n+        if _match_keys(place.city) & requested_keys\n+    ]\n+    matches.sort(key=lambda place: (place.postal_code, place.city))\n+\n+    if not matches:\n+        raise PlaceNotFoundError(f\"No places found for city: {city}\")\n+\n+    return matches\n@@ -4,6 +4,7 @@\n from fastapi import FastAPI\n \n from app.api.compliance import router as compliance_router\n+from app.api.discovery import router as discovery_router\n from app.api.evidence import router as evidence_router\n from app.api.health import router as health_router\n from app.api.scans import router as scans_router\n@@ -27,3 +28,4 @@ async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:\n app.include_router(scans_router)\n app.include_router(compliance_router)\n app.include_router(evidence_router)\n+app.include_router(discovery_router)\n\n@@ -7,3 +7,5 @@ celery[redis]==5.4.0\n TestClient(app)\n+\n+\n+def test_keywords_endpoint_returns_structured_groups() -> None:\n+    client = make_client()\n+\n+    response = client.get(\"/discovery/keywords\")\n+\n+    assert response.status_code == 200\n+    groups = response.json()[\"groups\"]\n+    assert groups\n+    assert {\"group_id\", \"label\", \"keywords\", \"bfsg_relevance_reason\", \"enabled\"}.issubset(\n+
