+66,8 @@ docker-compose up --build\n \n Then open:\n \n-```text\n-http://localhost:8000/health\n+```bash\n+curl http://localhost:8000/health\n ```\n \n Expected response:\n@@ -67,7 +76,72 @@ Expected response:\n {\"status\":\"ok\"}\n ```\n \n-The API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the `leads`, `scans`, `findings`, and `compliance_findings` tables on startup.\n+The API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the database tables on startup.\n+\n+## Lead Discovery Foundation\n+\n+The canonical raw city data file is:\n+\n+```text\n+data/orte_deutschland.csv\n+```\n+\n+The file is semicolon-separated and must contain these columns:\n+\n+```text\n+plz;stadt\n+```\n+\n+The resolver treats this file as raw input. The `stadt` column may contain real city names as well as organizations or special postal recipients, so city resolution uses exact normalized city-name matching only. It does not use substring matching.\n+\n+Supported normalization includes case-insensitive matching and German umlaut handling:\n+\n+```text\n+Lübeck -> Luebeck -> lubeck\n+```\n+\n+Discovery endpoints:\n+\n+```bash\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Lübeck\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/lubeck\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords\n+```\n+\n+Example place response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n+  \"matches\": [\n+    {\n+      \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n+      \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n+      \"country\": \"DE\"\n+    }\n+  ]\n+}\n+```\n+\n+This is only the resolver and keyword foundation for later lead discovery. It is not a live Google Maps API integration. It does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers.\n+\n+## Guarded Pipeline\n+\n+The numbered guarded pipeline files validate that runtime dependencies are present and that the canonical city CSV is usable:\n+\n+```bash\n+python -m py_compile \\\n+  04_filter_quality.py \\\n+  05_run_pipeline.py \\\n+  13_city_guard.py \\\n+  14_evidence_gate.py \\\n+  15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n+\n+python 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n+```\n+\n+The guarded runner fails if `data/orte_deutschland.csv` is missing, unreadable, missing required columns, header-only, or contains zero usable rows.\n \n ## Async Scan Flow\n \n@@ -99,18 +173,6 @@ pending -> running -> processing -> done\n \n If task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n \n-Inspect scan status and evidence metadata:\n-\n-```bash\n-docker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT id, status, error_message, evidence_metadata->>'current_url' AS current_url FROM scans;\"\n-```\n-\n-Inspect stored findings:\n-\n-```bash\n-docker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, severity, wcag_refs, confidence_score FROM findings;\"\n-```\n-\n ## Compliance Mapping Flow\n \n Run deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n@@ -119,34 +181,8 @@ Run deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n ```\n \n-Expected response:\n-\n-```json\n-{\n-  \"scan_id\": \"<scan-id>\",\n-  \"mapping_version\": \"2026.05.08-001:<rules-hash>\",\n-  \"total_findings\": 3,\n-  \"mapped_findings\": 3,\n-  \"critical_count\": 0,\n-  \"high_count\": 2,\n-  \"compliance_coverage_score\": 1.0\n-}\n-```\n-\n Compliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n \n-Inspect enriched compliance findings:\n-\n-```bash\n-docker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"SELECT rule_id, normalized_severity, wcag_refs, en_refs, bfsg_refs, compliance_confidence_score FROM compliance_findings;\"\n-```\n-\n-The worker process can also be started independently:\n-\n-```bash\n-celery -A app.core.celery worker --loglevel=info\n-```\n-\n ## Browser Configuration\n \n The worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@\n+from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException, status\n+\n+from app.discovery.keywords import get_keyword_groups\n+from app.discovery.place_resolver import PlaceDataError, PlaceNotFoundError, resolve_city\n+\n+router = APIRouter(prefix=\"/discovery\", tags=[\"discovery\"])\n+\n+\n+@router.get(\"/places/{city}\")\n+def get_places(city: str) -> dict[str, object]:\n+    try:\n+        matches = resolve_city(city)\n+    except PlaceNotFoundError as exc:\n+        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n+    except PlaceDataError as exc:\n+        raise HTTPException(\n+            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n+            detail=str(exc),\n+        ) from exc\n+\n+    return {\"city\": city, \"matches\": [match.to_dict() for match in matches]}\n+\n+\n+@router.get(\"/keywords\")\n+def get_keywords() -> dict[str, object]:\n+    return {\"groups\": get_keyword_groups()}\n@@ -0,0 +1 @@\n+\"\"\"Lead discovery foundations for place resolution and search keywords.\"\"\"\n@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@\n+\"\"\"BFSG-relevant keyword groups for future lead discovery provider searches.\"\"\"\n+\n+from __future__ import annotations\n+\n+from copy import deepcopy\n+\n+KEYWORD_GROUPS = [\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"ecommerce\",\n+        \"label\": \"E-Commerce\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"online shop\", \"webshop\", \"e-commerce\", \"versandhandel\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Online retail services can fall within BFSG-relevant consumer-facing digital services.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"banking\",\n+        \"label\": \"Banking\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"bank\", \"sparkasse\", \"kredit\", \"online banking\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Banking and financial self-service channels are a core BFSG-relevant service area.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"transport\",\n+        \"label\": \"Transport\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"verkehrsbetriebe\", \"bahn\", \"bus\", \"ticket\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Passenger transport information and ticketing services are relevant for accessibility discovery.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"booking\",\n+        \"label\": \"Booking\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"buchung\", \"reservierung\", \"hotel\", \"reise\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Booking and reservation services can be consumer-facing digital transaction services.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"healthcare_services\",\n+        \"label\": \"Healthcare Services\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"terminbuchung\", \"arzt\", \"klinik\", \"apotheke\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Healthcare service portals are useful candidates for later accessibility audit discovery.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"telecom\",\n+        \"label\": \"Telecom\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"telekommunikation\", \"internet\", \"mobilfunk\", \"dsl\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Telecom providers commonly operate BFSG-relevant consumer portals and ordering flows.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"group_id\": \"public_services_candidate\",\n+        \"label\": \"Public Services Candidate\",\n+        \"keywords\": [\"stadtportal\", \"buergerdienst\", \"terminvereinbarung\", \"verwaltung\"],\n+        \"bfsg_relevance_reason\": \"Public service portals are discovery candidates, while legal applicability must be assessed later.\",\n+        \"enabled\": True,\n+    },\n+]\n+\n+\n+def get_keyword_groups() -> list[dict[str, object]]:\n+    return deepcopy(KEYWORD_GROUPS)\n@@ -0,0 +1,134 @@\n+\"\"\"City and postal-code resolution for lead discovery.\n+\n+The resolver reads the raw Deutsche Post-style city data from\n+``data/orte_deutschland.csv``. The source is treated as mixed raw input: rows may\n+contain real places, organizations, or special postal recipients. Resolution is\n+therefore exact after normalization and never substring-based.\n+\"\"\"\n+\n+from __future__ import annotations\n+\n+import csv\n+import re\n+from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass\n+from pathlib import Path\n+\n+PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\n+DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH = PROJECT_ROOT / \"data\" / \"orte_deutschland.csv\"\n+REQUIRED_COLUMNS = {\"plz\", \"stadt\"}\n+POSTAL_CODE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"^\\d{5}$\")\n+WHITESPACE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"\\s+\")\n+UMLAUT_TRANSLATION = str.maketrans(\n+    {\n+        \"ä\": \"ae\",\n+        \"ö\": \"oe\",\n+        \"ü\": \"ue\",\n+        \"ß\": \"ss\",\n+    }\n+)\n+\n+\n+class PlaceResolverError(ValueError):\n+    \"\"\"Base error for place resolver failures.\"\"\"\n+\n+\n+class PlaceDataError(PlaceResolverError):\n+    \"\"\"Raised when the configured city data file is missing or invalid.\"\"\"\n+\n+\n+class PlaceNotFoundError(PlaceResolverError):\n+    \"\"\"Raised when a city cannot be resolved from usable city rows.\"\"\"\n+\n+\n+@dataclass(frozen=True)\n+class PlaceMatch:\n+    city: str\n+    postal_code: str\n+    country: str = \"DE\"\n+\n+    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n+        return asdict(self)\n+\n+\n+def normalize_city_name(value: str) -> str:\n+    normalized = value.strip().casefold().translate(UMLAUT_TRANSLATION)\n+    return WHITESPACE_PATTERN.sub(\" \", normalized)\n+\n+\n+def _loose_umlaut_key(value: str) -> str:\n+    return value.replace(\"ae\", \"a\").replace(\"oe\", \"o\").replace(\"ue\", \"u\")\n+\n+\n+def _match_keys(value: str) -> set[str]:\n+    normalized = normalize_city_name(value)\n+    if not normalized:\n+        return set()\n+    return {normalized, _loose_umlaut_key(normalized)}\n+\n+\n+def _normalized_fieldnames(fieldnames: list[str] | None) -> dict[str, str]:\n+    if not fieldnames:\n+        return {}\n+    return {name.strip().casefold(): name for name in fieldnames if name}\n+\n+\n+def _is_usable_row(postal_code: str, city: str) -> bool:\n+    return bool(POSTAL_CODE_PATTERN.match(postal_code.strip()) and city.strip())\n+\n+\n+def load_places(data_path: Path | str = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH) -> list[PlaceMatch]:\n+    path = Path(data_path)\n+    if not path.is_file():\n+        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is missing: {path}\")\n+\n+    try:\n+        with path.open(\"r\", encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as handle:\n+            reader = csv.DictReader(handle, delimiter=\";\")\n+            fields = _normalized_fieldnames(reader.fieldnames)\n+            missing_columns = REQUIRED_COLUMNS - set(fields)\n+            if missing_columns:\n+                missing = \", \".join(sorted(missing_columns))\n+                raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is missing required columns: {missing}\")\n+\n+            postal_field = fields[\"plz\"]\n+            city_field = fields[\"stadt\"]\n+            places = []\n+            seen = set()\n+            for row in reader:\n+                postal_code = (row.get(postal_field) or \"\").strip()\n+                city = (row.get(city_field) or \"\").strip()\n+                if not _is_usable_row(postal_code, city):\n+                    continue\n+\n+                key = (postal_code, city)\n+                if key in seen:\n+                    continue\n+                seen.add(key)\n+                places.append(PlaceMatch(city=city, postal_code=postal_code))\n+    except UnicodeDecodeError as exc:\n+        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is not readable as UTF-8: {path}\") from exc\n+    except csv.Error as exc:\n+        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is not readable CSV: {path}\") from exc\n+\n+    if not places:\n+        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file has no usable city rows: {path}\")\n+\n+    return places\n+\n+\n+def resolve_city(city: str, data_path: Path | str = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH) -> list[PlaceMatch]:\n+    requested_keys = _match_keys(city)\n+    if not requested_keys:\n+        raise PlaceNotFoundError(\"No city name provided\")\n+\n+    matches = [\n+        place\n+        for place in load_places(data_path)\n+        if _match_keys(place.city) & requested_keys\n+    ]\n+    matches.sort(key=lambda place: (place.postal_code, place.city))\n+\n+    if not matches:\n+        raise PlaceNotFoundError(f\"No places found for city: {city}\")\n+\n+    return matches\n@@ -4,6 +4,7 @@\n from fastapi import FastAPI\n \n from app.api.compliance import router as compliance_router\n+from app.api.discovery import router as discovery_router\n from app.api.evidence import router as evidence_router\n from app.api.health import router as health_router\n from app.api.scans import router as scans_router\n@@ -27,3 +28,4 @@ async def lifespan(app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]:\n app.include_router(scans_router)\n app.include_router(compliance_router)\n app.include_router(evidence_router)\n+app.include_router(discovery_router)\n\n@@ -7,3 +7,5 @@ celery[redis]==5.4.0\n playwright>=1.49,<2.0\n axe-playwright-python==0.1.7\n boto3==1.35.90\n+pytest==8.3.4\n+httpx==0.28.1\n@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@\n+from fastapi import FastAPI\n+from fastapi.testclient import TestClient\n+\n+from app.api.discovery import router as discovery_router\n+\n+\n+def make_client() -> TestClient:\n+    app = FastAPI()\n+    app.include_router(discovery_router)\n+    return TestClient(app)\n+\n+\n+def test_keywords_endpoint_returns_structured_groups() -> None:\n+    client = make_client()\n+\n+    response = client.get(\"/discovery/keywords\")\n+\n+    assert response.status_code == 200\n+    groups = response.json()[\"groups\"]\n+    assert groups\n+    assert {\"group_id\", \"label\", \"keywords\", \"bfsg_relevance_reason\", \"enabled\"}.issubset(\n+        groups[0]\n+    )\n+    assert \"ecommerce\" in {group[\"group_id\"] for group in groups}\n+\n+\n+def test_places_endpoint_returns_luebeck_matches() -> None:\n+    client = make_client()\n+\n+    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/Lübeck\")\n+\n+    assert response.status_code == 200\n+    payload = response.json()\n+    assert payload[\"city\"] == \"Lübeck\"\n+    assert payload[\"matches\"]\n+    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in payload[\"matches\"]} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n+    assert any(match[\"postal_code\"] == \"23552\" for match in payload[\"matches\"])\n+\n+\n+def test_places_endpoint_accepts_luebeck_transliteration() -> None:\n+    client = make_client()\n+\n+    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/Luebeck\")\n+\n+    assert response.status_code == 200\n+    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in response.json()[\"matches\"]} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n@@ -0,0 +1,68 @@\n+from pathlib import Path\n+\n+import pytest\n+\n+from app.discovery.place_resolver import (\n+    PlaceDataError,\n+    PlaceNotFoundError,\n+    load_places,\n+    resolve_city,\n+)\n+\n+\n+def write_city_csv(path: Path, content: str) -> Path:\n+    path.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n+    return path\n+\n+\n+def test_luebeck_umlaut_resolves_from_canonical_data() -> None:\n+    matches = resolve_city(\"Lübeck\")\n+\n+    assert matches\n+    assert {match.city for match in matches} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n+    assert any(match.postal_code == \"23552\" for match in matches)\n+    assert all(match.country == \"DE\" for match in matches)\n+\n+\n+def test_luebeck_transliteration_resolves_from_canonical_data() -> None:\n+    matches = resolve_city(\"Luebeck\")\n+\n+    assert matches\n+    assert {match.city for match in matches} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n+\n+\n+def test_lubeck_plain_ascii_resolves_from_canonical_data() -> None:\n+    matches = resolve_city(\"lubeck\")\n+\n+    assert matches\n+    assert {match.city for match in matches} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n+\n+\n+def test_unknown_city_fails_clearly(tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n+    data_file = write_city_csv(tmp_path / \"places.csv\", \"plz;stadt\\n23552;Lübeck\\n\")\n+\n+    with pytest.raises(PlaceNotFoundError, match=\"No places found for city: Atlantis\"):\n+        resolve_city(\"Atlantis\", data_file)\n+\n+\n+def test_header_only_csv_fails_clearly(tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n+    data_file = write_city_csv(tmp_path / \"places.csv\", \"plz;stadt\\n\")\n+\n+    with pytest.raises(PlaceDataError, match=\"no usable city rows\"):\n+        load_places(data_file)\n+\n+\n+def test_substring_special_recipients_do_not_pollute_exact_city_match(tmp_path: Path) -> None:\n+    data_file = write_city_csv(\n+        tmp_path / \"places.csv\",\n+        \"plz;stadt\\n\"\n+        \"23552;Lübeck\\n\"\n+        \"23560;Stadtwerke Lübeck Gruppe GmbH\\n\"\n+        \"23558;Finanzamt Lübeck\\n\"\n+        \"23556;Agentur für Arbeit Lübeck\\n\",\n+    )\n+\n+    matches = resolve_city(\"Luebeck\", data_file)\n+\n+    assert [match.city for match in matches] == [\"Lübeck\"]\n+    assert [match.postal_code for match in matches] == [\"23552\"]",
>   "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/commit/a96df3553e40da565f6ded0cb6f7b71283bb253d",
>   "display_title": "Merge pull request #5 from DopeChicka/codex/lead-discovery-foundation\n\nAdd lead discovery city resolver foundation"

> ```text
> {"content": "\"\"\"City and postal-code resolution for lead discovery.\n\nThe resolver reads the raw Deutsche Post-style city data from\n``data/orte_deutschland.csv``. The source is treated as mixed raw input: rows may\ncontain real places, organizations, or special postal recipients. Resolution is\ntherefore exact after normalization and never substring-based.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport csv\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import asdict, dataclass\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nPROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\nDEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH = PROJECT_ROOT / \"data\" / \"orte_deutschland.csv\"\nREQUIRED_COLUMNS = {\"plz\", \"stadt\"}\nPOSTAL_CODE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"^\\d{5}$\")\nWHITESPACE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"\\s+\")\nUMLAUT_TRANSLATION = str.maketrans(\n    {\n        \"\u00e4\": \"ae\",\n        \"\u00f6\": \"oe\",\n        \"\u00fc\": \"ue\",\n        \"\u00df\": \"ss\",\n    }\n)\n\n\nclass PlaceResolverError(ValueError):\n    \"\"\"Base error for place resolver failures.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass PlaceDataError(PlaceResolverError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when the configured city data file is missing or invalid.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass PlaceNotFoundError(PlaceResolverError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when a city cannot be resolved from usable city rows.\"\"\"\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass PlaceMatch:\n    city: str\n    postal_code: str\n    country: str = \"DE\"\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, str]:\n        return asdict(self)\n\n\ndef normalize_city_name(value: str) -> str:\n    normalized = value.strip().casefold().translate(UMLAUT_TRANSLATION)\n    return WHITESPACE_PATTERN.sub(\" \", normalized)\n\n\ndef _loose_umlaut_key(value: str) -> str:\n    return value.replace(\"ae\", \"a\").replace(\"oe\", \"o\").replace(\"ue\", \"u\")\n\n\ndef _match_keys(value: str) -> set[str]:\n    normalized = normalize_city_name(value)\n    if not normalized:\n        return set()\n    return {normalized, _loose_umlaut_key(normalized)}\n\n\ndef _normalized_fieldnames(fieldnames: list[str] | None) -> dict[str, str]:\n    if not fieldnames:\n        return {}\n    return {name.strip().casefold(): name for name in fieldnames if name}\n\n\ndef _is_usable_row(postal_code: str, city: str) -> bool:\n    return bool(POSTAL_CODE_PATTERN.match(postal_code.strip()) and city.strip())\n\n\ndef load_places(data_path: Path | str = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH) -> list[PlaceMatch]:\n    path = Path(data_path)\n    if not path.is_file():\n        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is missing: {path}\")\n\n    try:\n        with path.open(\"r\", encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as handle:\n            reader = csv.DictReader(handle, delimiter=\";\")\n            fields = _normalized_fieldnames(reader.fieldnames)\n            missing_columns = REQUIRED_COLUMNS - set(fields)\n            if missing_columns:\n                missing = \", \".join(sorted(missing_columns))\n                raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is missing required columns: {missing}\")\n\n            postal_field = fields[\"plz\"]\n            city_field = fields[\"stadt\"]\n            places = []\n            seen = set()\n            for row in reader:\n                postal_code = (row.get(postal_field) or \"\").strip()\n                city = (row.get(city_field) or \"\").strip()\n                if not _is_usable_row(postal_code, city):\n                    continue\n\n                key = (postal_code, city)\n                if key in seen:\n                    continue\n                seen.add(key)\n                places.append(PlaceMatch(city=city, postal_code=postal_code))\n    except UnicodeDecodeError as exc:\n        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is not readable as UTF-8: {path}\") from exc\n    except csv.Error as exc:\n        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file is not readable CSV: {path}\") from exc\n\n    if not places:\n        raise PlaceDataError(f\"City data file has no usable city rows: {path}\")\n\n    return places\n\n\ndef resolve_city(city: str, data_path: Path | str = DEFAULT_CITY_DATA_PATH) -> list[PlaceMatch]:\n    requested_keys = _match_keys(city)\n    if not requested_keys:\n        raise PlaceNotFoundError(\"No city name provided\")\n\n    matches = [\n        place\n        for place in load_places(data_path)\n        if _match_keys(place.city) & requested_keys\n    ]\n    matches.sort(key=lambda place: (place.postal_code, place.city))\n\n    if not matches:\n        raise PlaceNotFoundError(f\"No places found for city: {city}\")\n\n    return matches\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "282896da1ecdf6992b1477527a07e8339afd4436", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/discovery/place_resolver.py", "display_title": "place_resolver.py"}
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException, status\n\nfrom app.discovery.keywords import get_keyword_groups\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import PlaceDataError, PlaceNotFoundError, resolve_city\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/discovery\", tags=[\"discovery\"])\n\n\n@router.get(\"/places/{city}\")\ndef get_places(city: str) -> dict[str, object]:\n    try:\n        matches = resolve_city(city)\n    except PlaceNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n    except PlaceDataError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            detail=str(exc),\n        ) from exc\n\n    return {\"city\": city, \"matches\": [match.to_dict() for match in matches]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/keywords\")\ndef get_keywords() -> dict[str, object]:\n    return {\"groups\": get_keyword_groups()}\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "8f944be32c70897cfb677d058123f2defc659623", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/api/discovery.py", "display_title": "discovery.py"}
> ```

>   "body": "## Summary\n- add canonical raw city data path at `data/orte_deutschland.csv`\n- add exact-match city/PLZ resolver with German umlaut normalization\n- add BFSG-relevant discovery keyword groups and `/discovery` API endpoints\n- update guarded pipeline checks to validate canonical city data\n- add pytest coverage for resolver, pollution resistance, and discovery API router\n\n## Validation\nCould not run local validation in this Codex environment because `python`, `python3`, `py`, `git`, and Docker are not available in PATH. > {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import DateTime, String, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass Lead(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"leads\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    domain: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False, index=True)\n    company_name: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n\n    scans: Mapped[list[\"Scan\"]] = relationship(\n        \"Scan\", back_populates=\"lead\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "98fcb5437d3be5e415a7e72bd42ae752da2a1a04", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/discovery-query-planner/app/models/lead.py", "display_title": "lead.py"}

> {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import JSON, DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey, String, Text, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass ScanStatus(str, enum.Enum):\n    pending = \"pending\"\n    running = \"running\"\n    processing = \"processing\"\n    done = \"done\"\n    failed = \"failed\"\n\n\nclass Scan(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"scans\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    lead_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"leads.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n    )\n    status: Mapped[ScanStatus] = mapped_column(\n        Enum(ScanStatus, name=\"scan_status\"), default=ScanStatus.pending, nullable=False\n    )\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n    started_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    completed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    failed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    error_message: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\n    evidence_metadata: Mapped[dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(\n        JSON, default=dict, nullable=False\n    )\n\n    lead: Mapped[\"Lead\"] = relationship(\"Lead\", back_populates=\"scans\")\n    findings: Mapped[list[\"Finding\"]] = relationship(\n        \"Finding\", back_populates=\"scan\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\n    )\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "541697de7f8a9dd519a80f196d2c3ebc8aae0eac", "display_url": > ```text
> {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import JSON, DateTime, Float, ForeignKey, String, Text, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass Finding(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"findings\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    scan_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"scans.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False, index=True\n    )\n    rule_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100), nullable=False, index=True)\n    severity: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(50), nullable=False)\n    description: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\n    help_url: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(500), nullable=True)\n    wcag_refs: Mapped[list[str]] = mapped_column(JSON, default=list, nullable=False)\n    confidence_score: Mapped[float | None] = mapped_column(Float, nullable=True)\n    evidence_metadata: Mapped[dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(\n        JSON, default=dict, nullable=False\n    )\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n\n    scan: Mapped[\"Scan\"] = relationship(\"Scan\", back_populates=\"findings\")\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "e65eb2a5d6519977f5e6b0e23a882edf7d0b4777", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/discovery-query-planner/app/models/finding.py", "display_title": "finding.py"}
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "from sqlalchemy import text\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.db.session import engine\nfrom app.models import ComplianceFinding, EvidenceBundle, Finding, Lead, Scan  # noqa: F401\n\n_SCAN_ENUM_UPDATES = (\n    \"ALTER TYPE scan_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'processing'\",\n    \"ALTER TYPE scan_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'failed'\",\n)\n\n_SCAN_COLUMN_UPDATES = (\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS started_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS failed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS error_message TEXT\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS evidence_metadata JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb\",\n)\n\n_FINDING_COLUMN_UPDATES = (\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS description TEXT\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS help_url VARCHAR(500)\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS wcag_refs JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS confidence_score DOUBLE PRECISION\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS evidence_metadata JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb\",\n)\n\n\ndef init_db() -> None:\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n    _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema()\n    _ensure_finding_evidence_schema()\n\n\ndef _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema() -> None:\n    if engine.dialect.name != \"postgresql\":\n        return\n\n    with engine.connect() as connection:\n        autocommit_connection = connection.execution_options(isolation_level=\"AUTOCOMMIT\")\n        for statement in _SCAN_ENUM_UPDATES:\n            autocommit_connection.execute(text(statement))\n\n    with engine.begin() as connection:\n        for statement in _SCAN_COLUMN_UPDATES:\n            connection.execute(text(statement))\n\n\ndef _ensure_finding_evidence_schema() -> None:\n    if engine.dialect.name != \"postgresql\":\n        return\n\n    with engine.begin() as connection:\n        for statement in _FINDING_COLUMN_UPDATES:\n            connection.execute(text(statement))\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "5f129243b7798b97133c7b81aec351076b7887fc", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/discovery-query-planner/app/db/init_db.py", "display_title": "init_db.py"}
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException, status\n\nfrom app.discovery.keywords import get_keyword_groups\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import PlaceDataError, PlaceNotFoundError, resolve_city\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/discovery\", tags=[\"discovery\"])\n\n\n@router.get(\"/places/{city}\")\ndef get_places(city: str) -> dict[str, object]:\n    try:\n        matches = resolve_city(city)\n    except PlaceNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n    except PlaceDataError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            detail=str(exc),\n        ) from exc\n\n    return {\"city\": city, \"matches\": [match.to_dict() for match in matches]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/keywords\")\ndef get_keywords() -> dict[str, object]:\n    return {\"groups\": get_keyword_groups()}\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "8f944be32c70897cfb677d058123f2defc659623", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/discovery-query-planner/app/api/discovery.py", "display_title": "discovery.py"}
> ```

> ```text
> {"content": "from collections.abc import Generator\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\n\nfrom app.core.config import settings\n\nengine = create_engine(settings.database_url, pool_pre_ping=True)\nSessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)\n\n\ndef get_db() -> Generator[Session, None, None]:\n    db = SessionLocal()\n    try:\n        yield db\n    finally:\n        db.close()\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "199bb49dbbc8f05c5b513dfcdb9faadbcee9c6f0", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/discovery-query-planner/app/db/session.py", "display_title": "session.py"}

> ```text
> {"content": "from fastapi import FastAPI\nfrom fastapi.testclient import TestClient\n\nfrom app.api.discovery import router as discovery_router\n\n\ndef make_client() -> TestClient:\n    app = FastAPI()\n    app.include_router(discovery_router)\n    return TestClient(app)\n\n\ndef test_keywords_endpoint_returns_structured_groups() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/keywords\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    groups = response.json()[\"groups\"]\n    assert groups\n    assert {\"group_id\", \"label\", \"keywords\", \"bfsg_relevance_reason\", \"enabled\"}.issubset(\n        groups[0]\n    )\n    assert \"ecommerce\" in {group[\"group_id\"] for group in groups}\n\n\ndef test_places_endpoint_returns_luebeck_matches() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/L\u00fcbeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    payload = response.json()\n    assert payload[\"city\"] == \"L\u00fcbeck\"\n    assert payload[\"matches\"]\n    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in payload[\"matches\"]} == {\"L\u00fcbeck\"}\n    assert any(match[\"postal_code\"] == \"23552\" for match in payload[\"matches\"])\n\n\ndef test_places_endpoint_accepts_luebeck_transliteration() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/Luebeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in response.json()[\"matches\"]} == {\"L\u00fcbeck\"}\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "fa1f005195012bc7b4ec896bfedd0c55834c0f89", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/discovery-query-planner/tests/test_discovery_api.py", "display_title": "test_discovery_api.py"}
> ```

> </details>
>
> Ich baue jetzt die nächste Schicht: zwei neue SQLAlchemy-Modelle, deterministic Query-Planner, Service und API-Routen. Ich vermeide bewusst Provider-Ausführung: es werden nur Query-Pläne gespeichert, keine externen Calls und keine Kandidaten erzeugt.
>

>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",

>   "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import JSON, DateTime, Enum, String, Text, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass DiscoveryRunStatus(str, enum.Enum):\n    pending = \"pending\"\n    running = \"running\"\n    done = \"done\"\n    failed = \"failed\"\n\n\nclass DiscoveryRun(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"discovery_runs\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    city: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False, index=True)\n    normalized_city: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False, index=True)\n    status: Mapped[DiscoveryRunStatus] = mapped_column(\n        Enum(DiscoveryRunStatus, name=\"discovery_run_status\"),\n        default=DiscoveryRunStatus.pending,\n        nullable=False,\n    )\n    keyword_groups: Mapped[list[dict[str, Any]]] = mapped_column(\n        JSON, default=list, nullable=False\n    )\n    postal_codes: Mapped[list[str]] = mapped_column(JSON, default=list, nullable=False)\n    query_plan: Mapped[list[dict[str, str]]] = mapped_column(\n        JSON, default=list, nullable=False\n    )\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n    completed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    error_message: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\n\n    candidates: Mapped[list[\"LeadCandidate\"]] = relationship(\n        \"LeadCandidate\",\n        back_populates=\"discovery_run\",\n        cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\",\n    )\n"

>   "content": "from app.models.compliance_finding import ComplianceFinding\nfrom app.models.discovery_run import DiscoveryRun, DiscoveryRunStatus\nfrom app.models.evidence_bundle import EvidenceBundle\nfrom app.models.finding import Finding\nfrom app.models.lead import Lead\nfrom app.models.lead_candidate import LeadCandidate\nfrom app.models.scan import Scan, ScanStatus\n\n__all__ = [\n    \"ComplianceFinding\",\n    \"DiscoveryRun\",\n    \"DiscoveryRunStatus\",\n    \"EvidenceBundle\",\n    \"Finding\",\n    \"Lead\",\n    \"LeadCandidate\",\n    \"Scan\",\n    \"ScanStatus\",\n]\n"

> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/discovery-query-planner",
>   "path": "app/db/init_db.py",
>   "sha": "5f129243b7798b97133c7b81aec351076b7887fc",
>   "message": "Wire discovery models",
>   "content": "from sqlalchemy import text\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.db.session import engine\nfrom app.models import (  # noqa: F401\n    ComplianceFinding,\n    DiscoveryRun,\n    EvidenceBundle,\n    Finding,\n    Lead,\n    LeadCandidate,\n    Scan,\n)\n\n_SCAN_ENUM_UPDATES = (\n    \"ALTER TYPE scan_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'processing'\",\n    \"ALTER TYPE scan_status ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'failed'\",\n)\n\n_SCAN_COLUMN_UPDATES = (\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS started_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS failed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS error_message TEXT\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE scans ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS evidence_metadata JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb\",\n)\n\n_FINDING_COLUMN_UPDATES = (\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS description TEXT\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS help_url VARCHAR(500)\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS wcag_refs JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS confidence_score DOUBLE PRECISION\",\n    \"ALTER TABLE findings ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS evidence_metadata JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb\",\n)\n\n\ndef init_db() -> None:\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n    _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema()\n    _ensure_finding_evidence_schema()\n\n\ndef _ensure_scan_lifecycle_schema() -> None:\n    if engine.dialect.name != \"postgresql\":\n        return\n\n    with engine.connect() as connection:\n        autocommit_connection = connection.execution_options(isolation_level=\"AUTOCOMMIT\")\n        for statement in _SCAN_ENUM_UPDATES:\n            autocommit_connection.execute(text(statement))\n\n    with engine.begin() as connection:\n        for statement in _SCAN_COLUMN_UPDATES:\n            connection.execute(text(statement))\n\n\ndef _ensure_finding_evidence_schema() -> None:\n    if engine.dialect.name != \"postgresql\":\n        return\n\n    with engine.begin() as connection:\n        for statement in _FINDING_COLUMN_UPDATES:\n            connection.execute(text(statement))\n"
> }
> ```

> Die Tabellen sind modelliert. Als Nächstes kommt der deterministische Planner: Resolver-Ergebnis rein, aktive Keyword-Gruppen rein, sortierte `city + PLZ + keyword`-Einträge raus.
>
> <details><summary>Called 2 tools</summary>
>
> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_create_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/discovery-query-planner",
>   "path": "app/discovery/query_planner.py",
>   "message": "Add deterministic discovery query planner",
>   "content": "\"\"\"Deterministic dry-run query planner for lead discovery.\n\nThis module prepares internal search queries only. It does not call external\nproviders and does not scrape websites.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n\nfrom app.discovery.keywords import get_keyword_groups\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import PlaceMatch, resolve_city\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass QueryPlan:\n    city: str\n    postal_codes: list[str]\n    keyword_groups: list[dict[str, object]]\n    query_plan: list[dict[str, str]]\n\n\ndef get_enabled_keyword_groups() -> list[dict[str, object]]:\n    return [group for group in get_keyword_groups() if group.get(\"enabled\") is True]\n\n\ndef _unique_places_by_postal_code(places: list[PlaceMatch]) -> list[PlaceMatch]:\n    by_postal_code = {place.postal_code: place for place in places}\n    return [by_postal_code[postal_code] for postal_code in sorted(by_postal_code)]\n\n\ndef build_query_plan(city: str) -> QueryPlan:\n    places = _unique_places_by_postal_code(resolve_city(city))\n    keyword_groups = get_enabled_keyword_groups()\n    query_entries: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n\n    for place in places:\n        for group in keyword_groups:\n            group_id = str(group[\"group_id\"])\n            for keyword in group[\"keywords\"]:\n                keyword_text = str(keyword)\n                query_entries.append(\n                    {\n                        \"city\": place.city,\n                        \"postal_code\": place.postal_code,\n                        \"keyword_group_id\": group_id,\n                        \"keyword\": keyword_text,\n                        \"query_text\": f\"{place.city} {place.postal_code} {keyword_text}\",\n                    }\n                )\n\n    return QueryPlan(\n        city=places[0].city,\n        postal_codes=[place.postal_code for place in places],\n        keyword_groups=keyword_groups,\n        query_plan=query_entries,\n    )\n"
> }
> ```

> ```

> codex_apps.github_create_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/discovery-query-planner",
>   "path": "app/services/discovery_service.py",
>   "message": "Persist discovery query plans",
>   "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import PlaceDataError, PlaceNotFoundError, normalize_city_name\nfrom app.discovery.query_planner import build_query_plan\nfrom app.models.discovery_run import DiscoveryRun, DiscoveryRunStatus\nfrom app.models.lead_candidate import LeadCandidate\n\n\nclass DiscoveryRunNotFoundError(LookupError):\n    pass\n\n\ndef create_discovery_run(db: Session, city: str) -> DiscoveryRun:\n    discovery_run = DiscoveryRun(\n        city=city,\n        normalized_city=normalize_city_name(city),\n        status=DiscoveryRunStatus.pending,\n        keyword_groups=[],\n        postal_codes=[],\n        query_plan=[],\n    )\n    db.add(discovery_run)\n    db.flush()\n\n    try:\n        plan = build_query_plan(city)\n    except (PlaceDataError, PlaceNotFoundError) as exc:\n        discovery_run.status = DiscoveryRunStatus.failed\n        discovery_run.completed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n        discovery_run.error_message = str(exc)\n        db.commit()\n        db.refresh(discovery_run)\n        raise\n\n    discovery_run.city = plan.city\n    discovery_run.normalized_city = normalize_city_name(plan.city)\n    discovery_run.status = DiscoveryRunStatus.done\n    discovery_run.keyword_groups = plan.keyword_groups\n    discovery_run.postal_codes = plan.postal_codes\n    discovery_run.query_plan = plan.query_plan\n    discovery_run.completed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n    discovery_run.error_message = None\n    db.commit()\n    db.refresh(discovery_run)\n    return discovery_run\n\n\ndef get_discovery_run(db: Session, run_id: str) -> DiscoveryRun:\n    discovery_run = db.get(DiscoveryRun, run_id)\n    if discovery_run is None:\n        raise DiscoveryRunNotFoundError(f\"Discovery run not found: {run_id}\")\n    return discovery_run\n\n\ndef list_lead_candidates(db: Session, run_id: str) -> list[LeadCandidate]:\n    get_discovery_run(db, run_id)\n    return (\n        db.query(LeadCandidate)\n        .filter(LeadCandidate.discovery_run_id == run_id)\n        .order_by(LeadCandidate.created_at.asc(), LeadCandidate.id.asc())\n        .all()\n    )\n"
> }
> ```

> Service und Planner sind angelegt. Ich erweitere jetzt nur den bestehenden Discovery-Router um Run-Erzeugung, Run-Read und Kandidaten-Liste; Kandidaten bleiben leer, solange kein späterer Provider explizit Daten liefert.

> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/discovery-query-planner",
>   "path": "app/api/discovery.py",
>   "sha": "8f944be32c70897cfb677d058123f2defc659623",
>   "message": "Add discovery run API endpoints",
>   "content": "from datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, status\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n\nfrom app.db.session import get_db\nfrom app.discovery.keywords import get_keyword_groups\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import PlaceDataError, PlaceNotFoundError, resolve_city\nfrom app.models.discovery_run import DiscoveryRun\nfrom app.models.lead_candidate import LeadCandidate\nfrom app.services.discovery_service import (\n    DiscoveryRunNotFoundError,\n    create_discovery_run,\n    get_discovery_run,\n    list_lead_candidates,\n)\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/discovery\", tags=[\"discovery\"])\n\n\ndef _format_datetime(value: datetime | None) -> str | None:\n    return value.isoformat() if value else None\n\n\ndef _serialize_discovery_run(discovery_run: DiscoveryRun) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    return {\n        \"id\": discovery_run.id,\n        \"city\": discovery_run.city,\n        \"normalized_city\": discovery_run.normalized_city,\n        \"status\": discovery_run.status.value,\n        \"postal_codes\": discovery_run.postal_codes,\n        \"keyword_groups\": discovery_run.keyword_groups,\n        \"query_plan\": discovery_run.query_plan,\n        \"created_at\": _format_datetime(discovery_run.created_at),\n        \"completed_at\": _format_datetime(discovery_run.completed_at),\n        \"error_message\": discovery_run.error_message,\n    }\n\n\ndef _serialize_candidate(candidate: LeadCandidate) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    return {\n        \"id\": candidate.id,\n        \"discovery_run_id\": candidate.discovery_run_id,\n        \"source\": candidate.source,\n        \"source_reference\": candidate.source_reference,\n        \"company_name\": candidate.company_name,\n        \"domain\": candidate.domain,\n        \"city\": candidate.city,\n        \"postal_code\": candidate.postal_code,\n        \"address\": candidate.address,\n        \"phone\": candidate.phone,\n        \"category\": candidate.category,\n        \"raw_data\": candidate.raw_data,\n        \"confidence_score\": candidate.confidence_score,\n        \"created_at\": _format_datetime(candidate.created_at),\n    }\n\n\n@router.get(\"/places/{city}\")\ndef get_places(city: str) -> dict[str, object]:\n    try:\n        matches = resolve_city(city)\n    except PlaceNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n    except PlaceDataError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            detail=str(exc),\n        ) from exc\n\n    return {\"city\": city, \"matches\": [match.to_dict() for match in matches]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/keywords\")\ndef get_keywords() -> dict[str, object]:\n    return {\"groups\": get_keyword_groups()}\n\n\n@router.post(\"/runs/{city}\")\ndef create_run(city: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, object]:\n    try:\n        discovery_run = create_discovery_run(db, city)\n    except PlaceNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n    except PlaceDataError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            detail=str(exc),\n        ) from exc\n\n    return {\n        \"discovery_run_id\": discovery_run.id,\n        \"city\": discovery_run.city,\n        \"status\": discovery_run.status.value,\n        \"postal_codes_count\": len(discovery_run.postal_codes),\n        \"query_count\": len(discovery_run.query_plan),\n    }\n\n\n@router.get(\"/runs/{run_id}\")\ndef read_run(run_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    try:\n        discovery_run = get_discovery_run(db, run_id)\n    except DiscoveryRunNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n    return _serialize_discovery_run(discovery_run)\n\n\n@router.get(\"/runs/{run_id}/candidates\")\ndef read_run_candidates(run_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, object]:\n    try:\n        candidates = list_lead_candidates(db, run_id)\n    except DiscoveryRunNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n    return {\"discovery_run_id\": run_id, \"candidates\": [_serialize_candidate(candidate) for candidate in candidates]}\n"
> }
> ```

> </details>
>
> API ist verdrahtet. Ich ergänze jetzt Tests für Planner, Service-Persistenz und die neuen Run-Endpunkte mit einer isolierten SQLite-Testdatenbank, damit keine externen Dienste oder Provider nötig sind.
>
> <details><summary>Called 3 tools</summary>
>
> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_create_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/discovery-query-planner",
>   "path": "tests/test_query_planner.py",
>   "message": "Add discovery run tests",
>   "content": "import socket\n\nfrom app.discovery.query_planner import build_query_plan, get_enabled_keyword_groups\n\n\ndef test_query_planner_returns_deterministic_plan_for_luebeck() -> None:\n    first = build_query_plan(\"Lübeck\")\n    second = build_query_plan(\"Luebeck\")\n\n    assert first.postal_codes == second.postal_codes\n    assert first.query_plan == second.query_plan\n    assert first.city == \"Lübeck\"\n    assert first.query_plan[0] == {\n        \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n        \"postal_code\": first.postal_codes[0],\n        \"keyword_group_id\": \"ecommerce\",\n        \"keyword\": \"online shop\",\n        \"query_text\": f\"Lübeck {first.postal_codes[0]} online shop\",\n    }\n\n\ndef test_query_plan_uses_postal_codes_and_enabled_keyword_groups() -> None:\n    plan = build_query_plan(\"lubeck\")\n    enabled_groups = get_enabled_keyword_groups()\n    enabled_keywords_count = sum(len(group[\"keywords\"]) for group in enabled_groups)\n\n    assert plan.postal_codes\n    assert all(entry[\"postal_code\"] in plan.postal_codes for entry in plan.query_plan)\n    assert len(plan.query_plan) == len(plan.postal_codes) * enabled_keywords_count\n    assert {entry[\"keyword_group_id\"] for entry in plan.query_plan} == {\n        str(group[\"group_id\"]) for group in enabled_groups\n    }\n\n\ndef test_query_planner_makes_no_external_network_calls(monkeypatch) -> None:\n    def fail_network(*args, **kwargs):\n        raise AssertionError(\"network access is not allowed in query planner\")\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(socket, \"create_connection\", fail_network)\n\n    plan = build_query_plan(\"Lübeck\")\n\n    assert plan.query_plan\n"
> }
> ```
>

> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/discovery-query-planner",
>   "path": "tests/test_discovery_service.py",
>   "message": "Add discovery run tests",
>   "content": "import pytest\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.models import DiscoveryRun, LeadCandidate  # noqa: F401\nfrom app.services.discovery_service import create_discovery_run\n\n\n@pytest.fixture()\ndef db_session() -> Session:\n    engine = create_engine(\"sqlite:///:memory:\")\n    TestingSessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine)\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n\n    session = TestingSessionLocal()\n    try:\n        yield session\n    finally:\n        session.close()\n        Base.metadata.drop_all(bind=engine)\n\n\ndef test_discovery_run_can_be_created_in_db(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    discovery_run = create_discovery_run(db_session, \"Lübeck\")\n\n    assert discovery_run.id\n    assert discovery_run.status.value == \"done\"\n    assert discovery_run.city == \"Lübeck\"\n    assert discovery_run.normalized_city == \"luebeck\"\n    assert discovery_run.postal_codes\n    assert discovery_run.keyword_groups\n    assert discovery_run.query_plan\n    assert discovery_run.error_message is None\n\n    stored = db_session.get(DiscoveryRun, discovery_run.id)\n    assert stored is not None\n    assert stored.query_plan == discovery_run.query_plan\n"
> }
> ```
>

> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/discovery-query-planner",
>   "path": "tests/test_discovery_api.py",
>   "sha": "fa1f005195012bc7b4ec896bfedd0c55834c0f89",
>   "message": "Add discovery run tests",
>   "content": "import pytest\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\nfrom fastapi.testclient import TestClient\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\n\nfrom app.api.discovery import router as discovery_router\nfrom app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.db.session import get_db\nfrom app.models import DiscoveryRun, LeadCandidate  # noqa: F401\n\n\n@pytest.fixture()\ndef db_session() -> Session:\n    engine = create_engine(\"sqlite:///:memory:\")\n    TestingSessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine)\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n\n    session = TestingSessionLocal()\n    try:\n        yield session\n    finally:\n        session.close()\n        Base.metadata.drop_all(bind=engine)\n\n\ndef make_client(db_session: Session | None = None) -> TestClient:\n    app = FastAPI()\n    app.include_router(discovery_router)\n    if db_session is not None:\n        app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = lambda: db_session\n    return TestClient(app)\n\n\ndef test_keywords_endpoint_returns_structured_groups() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/keywords\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    groups = response.json()[\"groups\"]\n    assert groups\n    assert {\"group_id\", \"label\", \"keywords\", \"bfsg_relevance_reason\", \"enabled\"}.issubset(\n        groups[0]\n    )\n    assert \"ecommerce\" in {group[\"group_id\"] for group in groups}\n\n\ndef test_places_endpoint_returns_luebeck_matches() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/Lübeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    payload = response.json()\n    assert payload[\"city\"] == \"Lübeck\"\n    assert payload[\"matches\"]\n    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in payload[\"matches\"]} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n    assert any(match[\"postal_code\"] == \"23552\" for match in payload[\"matches\"])\n\n\ndef test_places_endpoint_accepts_luebeck_transliteration() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/Luebeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in response.json()[\"matches\"]} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n\n\ndef test_create_discovery_run_endpoint_returns_done_and_query_count(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n\n    response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Lübeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    payload = response.json()\n    assert payload[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n    assert payload[\"city\"] == \"Lübeck\"\n    assert payload[\"status\"] == \"done\"\n    assert payload[\"postal_codes_count\"] > 0\n    assert payload[\"query_count\"] > 0\n\n\ndef test_get_discovery_run_endpoint_returns_stored_query_plan(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Lübeck\")\n    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n\n    response = client.get(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    payload = response.json()\n    assert payload[\"id\"] == run_id\n    assert payload[\"status\"] == \"done\"\n    assert payload[\"postal_codes\"]\n    assert payload[\"keyword_groups\"]\n    assert payload[\"query_plan\"]\n    assert payload[\"error_message\"] is None\n\n\ndef test_discovery_run_candidates_endpoint_returns_empty_list(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Lübeck\")\n    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n\n    response = client.get(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/candidates\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert response.json() == {\"discovery_run_id\": run_id, \"candidates\": []}\n\n\ndef test_create_discovery_run_unknown_city_returns_clear_error(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n\n    response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Atlantis\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 404\n    assert \"No places found for city: Atlantis\" in response.json()[\"detail\"]\n"
> }
> ```

>
> Ich sehe einen Test-Infra-Punkt: FastAPI TestClient kann SQLite in einem anderen Thread anfassen. Ich passe nur die Test-DB-Konfiguration auf `StaticPool`/`check_same_thread=False` an, damit die In-Memory-DB stabil ist.
>
> <details><summary>Called 4 tools</summary>
>
> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_update_file
>

> ````text
> {"content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nNorthAccessBFSG is a FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\nThe current scope includes backend infrastructure, asynchronous scans, raw accessibility findings, compliance mapping basics, evidence bundle storage, and a minimal Lead Discovery foundation for city and keyword resolution.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance.py\n    discovery.py\n    evidence.py\n    health.py\n    scans.py\n  compliance/\n    __init__.py\n    rule_engine.py\n    rules/\n      __init__.py\n      wcag_en_bfsg_map.json\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    browser_config.py\n    celery.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  discovery/\n    __init__.py\n    keywords.py\n    place_resolver.py\n  evidence/\n    __init__.py\n    hashing.py\n    storage.py\n    storage_backend.py\n  models/\n  services/\n  workers/\ndata/\n  orte_deutschland.csv\n04_filter_quality.py\n05_run_pipeline.py\n13_city_guard.py\n14_evidence_gate.py\n15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the database tables on startup.\n\n## Lead Discovery Foundation\n\nThe canonical raw city data file is:\n\n```text\ndata/orte_deutschland.csv\n```\n\nThe file is semicolon-separated and must contain these columns:\n\n```text\nplz;stadt\n```\n\nThe resolver treats this file as raw input. The `stadt` column may contain real city names as well as organizations or special postal recipients, so city resolution uses exact normalized city-name matching only. It does not use substring matching.\n\nSupported normalization includes case-insensitive matching and German umlaut handling:\n\n```text\nL\u00fcbeck -> Luebeck -> lubeck\n```\n\nDiscovery endpoints:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/L\u00fcbeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/lubeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords\n```\n\nExample place response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n  \"matches\": [\n    {\n      \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n      \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n      \"country\": \"DE\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\nThis is only the resolver and keyword foundation for later lead discovery. It is not a live Google Maps API integration. It does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers.\n\n## Guarded Pipeline\n\nThe numbered guarded pipeline files validate that runtime dependencies are present and that the canonical city CSV is usable:\n\n```bash\npython -m py_compile \\\n  04_filter_quality.py \\\n  05_run_pipeline.py \\\n  13_city_guard.py \\\n  14_evidence_gate.py \\\n  15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n\npython 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n```\n\nThe guarded runner fails if `data/orte_deutschland.csv` is missing, unreadable, missing required columns, header-only, or contains zero usable rows.\n\n## Async Scan Flow\n\nCreate a lead directly in the database while a public lead API does not exist yet:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n```\n\nRun a scan for the lead:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nExpected immediate response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker launches Playwright headless Chromium, loads the lead domain, runs axe-core, captures evidence metadata, and persists raw findings.\n\nLifecycle:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nIf task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n\n## Compliance Mapping Flow\n\nRun deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nCompliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Browser Configuration\n\nThe worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n\n```text\nBROWSER_NAME=chromium\nBROWSER_HEADLESS=true\nBROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS=30000\nBROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS=10000\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH=1440\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=900\nBROWSER_RETRIES=1\nBROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL=networkidle\n```\n\nWebKit is supported by configuration but is not installed in the default image.\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "f4264d5b7d5d67098a838d63385188374d5a709b", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/discovery-query-planner/README.md", "display_title": "README.md"}
> ````
>
> </details>
>
> Die Tests decken jetzt Planner, Service und API ab. Ich aktualisiere noch das README mit den neuen Dry-Run-Run-Endpunkten und dem klaren Hinweis: keine Google Maps API, keine Provider Calls, Kandidaten sind keine Rechtsbewertung.

> ````json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",

>   "message": "Document discovery query plan endpoints",
>   "content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nNorthAccessBFSG is a FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\nThe current scope includes backend infrastructure, asynchronous scans, raw accessibility findings, compliance mapping basics, evidence bundle storage, and a minimal Lead Discovery foundation for city and keyword resolution.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance.py\n    discovery.py\n    evidence.py\n    health.py\n    scans.py\n  compliance/\n    __init__.py\n    rule_engine.py\n    rules/\n      __init__.py\n      wcag_en_bfsg_map.json\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    browser_config.py\n    celery.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  discovery/\n    __init__.py\n    keywords.py\n    place_resolver.py\n    query_planner.py\n  evidence/\n    __init__.py\n    hashing.py\n    storage.py\n    storage_backend.py\n  models/\n    discovery_run.py\n    lead_candidate.py\n  services/\n    discovery_service.py\n  workers/\ndata/\n  orte_deutschland.csv\n04_filter_quality.py\n05_run_pipeline.py\n13_city_guard.py\n14_evidence_gate.py\n15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the database tables on startup.\n\n## Lead Discovery Foundation\n\nThe canonical raw city data file is:\n\n```text\ndata/orte_deutschland.csv\n```\n\nThe file is semicolon-separated and must contain these columns:\n\n```text\nplz;stadt\n```\n\nThe resolver treats this file as raw input. The `stadt` column may contain real city names as well as organizations or special postal recipients, so city resolution uses exact normalized city-name matching only. It does not use substring matching.\n\nSupported normalization includes case-insensitive matching and German umlaut handling:\n\n```text\nLübeck -> Luebeck -> lubeck\n```\n\nDiscovery endpoints:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Lübeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/lubeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords\n```\n\nExample place response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n  \"matches\": [\n    {\n      \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n      \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n      \"country\": \"DE\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n## Discovery Query Planner\n\nA discovery run is a dry-run planning record. It resolves a city to postal codes, combines those postal codes with enabled keyword groups, and persists a deterministic query plan for a later external provider integration.\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/Lübeck\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"discovery_run_id\": \"<run-id>\",\n  \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n  \"status\": \"done\",\n  \"postal_codes_count\": 12,\n  \"query_count\": 336\n}\n```\n\nFetch a stored run and its candidate list:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates\n```\n\nThis is only a resolver, keyword, and query-planner foundation. It is not a live Google Maps API integration. It does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers. `LeadCandidate` rows are candidate records only; they are not confirmed legal applicability, BFSG violations, or legal conclusions.\n\n## Guarded Pipeline\n\nThe numbered guarded pipeline files validate that runtime dependencies are present and that the canonical city CSV is usable:\n\n```bash\npython -m py_compile \\\n  04_filter_quality.py \\\n  05_run_pipeline.py \\\n  13_city_guard.py \\\n  14_evidence_gate.py \\\n  15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n\npython 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n```\n\nThe guarded runner fails if `data/orte_deutschland.csv` is missing, unreadable, missing required columns, header-only, or contains zero usable rows.\n\n## Async Scan Flow\n\nCreate a lead directly in the database while a public lead API does not exist yet:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n```\n\nRun a scan for the lead:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nExpected immediate response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker launches Playwright headless Chromium, loads the lead domain, runs axe-core, captures evidence metadata, and persists raw findings.\n\nLifecycle:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nIf task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n\n## Compliance Mapping Flow\n\nRun deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nCompliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Browser Configuration\n\nThe worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n\n```text\nBROWSER_NAME=chromium\nBROWSER_HEADLESS=true\nBROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS=30000\nBROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS=10000\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH=1440\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=900\nBROWSER_RETRIES=1\nBROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL=networkidle\n```\n\nWebKit is supported by configuration but is not installed in the default image.\n"
> }
> ````

1. **Branch name**

`codex/discovery-query-planner`

PR opened as draft: https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/pull/6

2. **Changed files**

```text
README.md
app/api/discovery.py
app/db/init_db.py
app/discovery/query_planner.py
app/models/__init__.py
app/models/discovery_run.py
app/models/lead_candidate.py
app/services/discovery_service.py
tests/test_discovery_api.py
tests/test_discovery_service.py
tests/test_query_planner.py
```

3. **What was added**

- `DiscoveryRun` and `LeadCandidate` models
- deterministic city + PLZ + keyword query planner
- persisted dry-run discovery runs
- endpoints:
  - `POST /discovery/runs/{city}`
  - `GET /discovery/runs/{run_id}`
  - `GET /discovery/runs/{run_id}/candidates`
- tests for planner, API, DB persistence, unknown city handling, and no network calls
- README docs for dry-run planner scope

4. **Validation results**

Blocked in this shell:

```text
python3: command not found
docker: command not found
```

So `py_compile`, guarded pipeline, pytest, and Docker validation could not run here.

5. **Manual curl results**

Not executed here because Docker is unavailable in this shell.

- Needs local validation with Python/Docker before merge.
- New DB tables rely on startup `Base.metadata.create_all`, consistent with current project style.
- No external API calls, scraping, Playwright changes, reporting, or legal scoring added.

7. **safe to merge: no**

Only because validation could not be executed in this environment.

> # Role
> You are a senior backend/platform engineer working on:
>
> DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG
>
> # Project Naming Rule
> The project is called NorthAccessBFSG.
> Do NOT introduce bfsg-auditor-pro.
> Do NOT rename the project.
>
> # Base
> Use current main as base.
> Create a new branch.
>
> # Current State
> main is validated and contains:
> - Discovery place resolver
> - keyword groups
> - DiscoveryRun model
> - LeadCandidate model
> - deterministic query planner
> - persisted dry-run discovery runs
> - endpoints:
>   - POST /discovery/runs/{city}
>   - GET /discovery/runs/{run_id}
>   - GET /discovery/runs/{run_id}/candidates
>
> Latest main validation:
> - pytest: 17 passed
> - guarded pipeline: passed
>
> # Goal
> Build the next minimal provider execution foundation:
>
> DiscoveryRun query_plan -> provider adapter -> LeadCandidate rows.
>
> Important:
> Do NOT add live Google Maps API calls yet.
> Do NOT scrape.
> Do NOT add Playwright changes.
> Do NOT add reporting.
> Do NOT add legal scoring.
> Do NOT overbuild.
>
> This step must create the provider abstraction and a deterministic mock provider so the pipeline can persist candidate leads safely before connecting a real external provider.
>
> # Required Work
>
> ## 1. Add provider abstraction
>
> Create:
>
> app/discovery/providers/base.py
>
> Define:
> - DiscoveryProvider protocol or abstract base class
> - ProviderResult data object
>
> ProviderResult fields:
> - source
> - source_reference nullable
> - company_name nullable
> - domain nullable
> - city nullable
> - postal_code nullable
> - address nullable
> - phone nullable
> - category nullable
> - raw_data dict
> - confidence_score nullable
>
> No external calls.
>
> ## 2. Add mock provider
>
> Create:
>
> app/discovery/providers/mock_provider.py
>
> The mock provider should:
> - accept query_plan entries
> - return deterministic fake-but-clearly-marked mock ProviderResult objects
> - generate a small bounded number of candidates
> - not create legal claims
> - not claim real companies
> - use names like "Mock Candidate Lübeck 23552 Online Shop"
> - include source="mock"
> - include raw_data with query_text and keyword_group_id
>
> Important:
> Mock data must be obvious test data, not real businesses.
>
> ## 3. Add provider execution service
>
> Extend or create service:
>
> app/services/provider_execution_service.py
>
> It should:
> - load a DiscoveryRun by ID
> - fail clearly if run not found
> - execute mock provider against the stored query_plan
> - persist LeadCandidate rows
> - avoid duplicate candidates for same discovery_run_id + source + source_reference
> - return summary:
>   - discovery_run_id
>   - provider
>   - candidates_created
>   - candidates_total
>
> No external calls.
>
> ## 4. Add API endpoint
>
> Add endpoint under existing discovery router:
>
> POST /discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/mock
>
> Response example:
> {
>   "discovery_run_id": "...",
>   "provider": "mock",
>   "candidates_created": 10,
>   "candidates_total": 10
> }
>
> Existing:
> GET /discovery/runs/{run_id}/candidates
>
> should now return persisted mock candidates after execution.
>
> ## 5. Tests
>
> Add tests for:
> - mock provider returns deterministic ProviderResult list
> - provider execution persists LeadCandidate rows
> - duplicate execution does not create duplicate candidates
> - endpoint POST /discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/mock works
> - candidates endpoint returns candidates after mock provider execution
> - unknown run_id fails clearly
> - no external calls are made
>
> Use existing pytest setup.
>
> ## 6. README
>
> Update README:
> - document mock provider execution
> - make clear it creates mock/test candidates only
> - explain Google Maps/live providers are not integrated yet
> - explain candidate data is not a legal conclusion
>
> ## 7. Validation
>
> Run:
>
> python3 -m py_compile \
>   04_filter_quality.py \
>   05_run_pipeline.py \
>   13_city_guard.py \
>   14_evidence_gate.py \
>   15_run_pipeline_guarded.py
>
> python3 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py
>
> python3 -m pytest -q
>
> docker compose up --build
>
> Manual curl flow:
>
> curl http://localhost:8000/health
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/Luebeck
>
> Use returned discovery_run_id:
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/providers/mock
>
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates
>
> # Constraints
> - No Google Maps API yet.
> - No external calls.
> - No scraping.
> - No Playwright changes.
> - No report generation.
> - No legal scoring.
> - No broad refactor.
> - Keep PR small and reviewable.
> - Project name remains NorthAccessBFSG.
>

It does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers.\n+## Discovery Query Planner\n+\n+A discovery run is a dry-run planning record. It resolves a city to postal codes, combines those postal codes with enabled keyword groups, and persists a deterministic query plan for a later external provider integration.\n+\n+```bash\n+curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/L\u00fcbeck\n+```\n+\n+Example response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"discovery_run_id\": \"<run-id>\",\n+  \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n+  \"status\": \"done\",\n+  \"postal_codes_count\": 12,\n+  \"query_count\": 336\n+}\n+```\n+\n+Fetch a stored run and its candidate list:\n+\n+```bash\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates\n+```\n+\n+This is only a resolver, keyword, and query-planner foundation. It is not a live Google Maps API integration. It does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers. `LeadCandidate` rows are candidate records only; they are not confirmed legal applicability, BFSG violations, or legal @@\n+\"\"\"Deterministic dry-run query planner for lead discovery.\n+\n+This module prepares internal search queries only. It does not call external\n+providers and does not scrape websites.\n+\"\"\"\n+\n+from __future__ import annotations\n+\n+from dataclasses import dataclass\n+\n+from app.discovery.keywords import get_keyword_groups\n+from app.discovery.place_resolver import PlaceMatch, resolve_city\n+\n+\n+@dataclass(frozen=True)\n+class QueryPlan:\n+    city: str\n+    postal_codes: list[str]\n+    keyword_groups: list[dict[str, object]]\n+    query_plan: list[dict[str, str]]\n+\n+\n+def get_enabled_keyword_groups() -> list[dict[str, object]]:\n+    return [group for group in get_keyword_groups() if group.get(\"enabled\") is True]\n+\n+\n+def _unique_places_by_postal_code(places: list[PlaceMatch]) -> list[PlaceMatch]:\n+    by_postal_code = {place.postal_code: place for place in places}\n+    return [by_postal_code[postal_code] for postal_code in sorted(by_postal_code)]\n+\n+\n+def build_query_plan(city: str) -> QueryPlan:\n+    places = _unique_places_by_postal_code(resolve_city(city))\n+    keyword_groups = get_enabled_keyword_groups()\n+    query_entries: list[dict[str, str]] = []\n+\n+    for place in places:\n+        for group in keyword_groups:\n+            group_id = str(group[\"group_id\"])\n+            for keyword in group[\"keywords\"]:\n+                keyword_text = str(keyword)\n+                query_entries.append(\n+                    {\n+                        \"city\": place.city,\n+                        \"postal_code\": place.postal_code,\n+                        \"keyword_group_id\": group_id,\n+                        \"keyword\": keyword_text,\n+                        \"query_text\": f\"{place.city} {place.postal_code} {keyword_text}\",\n+                    }\n+                )\n+\n+    return QueryPlan(\n+        city=places[0].city,\n+        postal_codes=[place.postal_code for place in places],\n+        keyword_groups=keyword_groups,\n+        query_plan=query_entries,\n+    )\n@@ -1,14 +1,19 @@\n from app.models.compliance_finding import ComplianceFinding\n+from app.models.discovery_run import DiscoveryRun, DiscoveryRunStatus\n from __tablename__ = \"discovery_runs\"\n+\n+    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n+        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n+    )\n+    city: Mapped[str] =
