> ```text
> {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport enum\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import JSON, DateTime, Enum, String, Text, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass DiscoveryRunStatus(str, enum.Enum):\n    pending = \"pending\"\n    running = \"running\"\n    done = \"done\"\n    failed = \"failed\"\n\n\nclass DiscoveryRun(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"discovery_runs\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    city: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False, index=True)\n    normalized_city: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False, index=True)\n    status: Mapped[DiscoveryRunStatus] = mapped_column(\n        Enum(DiscoveryRunStatus, name=\"discovery_run_status\"),\n        default=DiscoveryRunStatus.pending,\n        nullable=False,\n    )\n    keyword_groups: Mapped[list[dict[str, Any]]] = mapped_column(\n        JSON, default=list, nullable=False\n    )\n    postal_codes: Mapped[list[str]] = mapped_column(JSON, default=list, nullable=False)\n    query_plan: Mapped[list[dict[str, str]]] = mapped_column(\n        JSON, default=list, nullable=False\n    )\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n    completed_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\n    )\n    error_message: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\n\n    candidates: Mapped[list[\"LeadCandidate\"]] = relationship(\n        \"LeadCandidate\",\n        back_populates=\"discovery_run\",\n        cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\",\n    )\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "14fe2db21f04a9237fd9d55c5b00f06ddc6dec8b", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/models/discovery_run.py", "display_title": "discovery_run.py"}

> ```text
> {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nimport uuid\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom sqlalchemy import JSON, DateTime, Float, ForeignKey, String, Text, func\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\n\n\nclass LeadCandidate(Base):\n    __tablename__ = \"lead_candidates\"\n\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\n    )\n    discovery_run_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\n        String(36),\n        ForeignKey(\"discovery_runs.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"),\n        nullable=False,\n        index=True,\n    )\n    source: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(100), nullable=False)\n    source_reference: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(500), nullable=True)\n    company_name: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=True)\n    domain: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=True)\n    city: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=True)\n    postal_code: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(20), nullable=True)\n    address: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\n    phone: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(100), nullable=True)\n    category: Mapped[str | None] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=True)\n    raw_data: Mapped[dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(JSON, default=dict, nullable=False)\n    confidence_score: Mapped[float | None] = mapped_column(Float, nullable=True)\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\n        DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False\n    )\n\n    discovery_run: Mapped[\"DiscoveryRun\"] = relationship(\n        \"DiscoveryRun\", back_populates=\"candidates\"\n    )\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "2df2bd61a2f4180c134aa034072c8f4d73d8b4ac", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/models/lead_candidate.py", "display_title": "lead_candidate.py"}

> ```text
> {"content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom datetime import UTC, datetime\n\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import PlaceDataError, PlaceNotFoundError, normalize_city_name\nfrom app.discovery.query_planner import build_query_plan\nfrom app.models.discovery_run import DiscoveryRun, DiscoveryRunStatus\nfrom app.models.lead_candidate import LeadCandidate\n\n\nclass DiscoveryRunNotFoundError(LookupError):\n    pass\n\n\ndef create_discovery_run(db: Session, city: str) -> DiscoveryRun:\n    discovery_run = DiscoveryRun(\n        city=city,\n        normalized_city=normalize_city_name(city),\n        status=DiscoveryRunStatus.pending,\n        keyword_groups=[],\n        postal_codes=[],\n        query_plan=[],\n    )\n    db.add(discovery_run)\n    db.flush()\n\n    try:\n        plan = build_query_plan(city)\n    except (PlaceDataError, PlaceNotFoundError) as exc:\n        discovery_run.status = DiscoveryRunStatus.failed\n        discovery_run.completed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n        discovery_run.error_message = str(exc)\n        db.commit()\n        db.refresh(discovery_run)\n        raise\n\n    discovery_run.city = plan.city\n    discovery_run.normalized_city = normalize_city_name(plan.city)\n    discovery_run.status = DiscoveryRunStatus.done\n    discovery_run.keyword_groups = plan.keyword_groups\n    discovery_run.postal_codes = plan.postal_codes\n    discovery_run.query_plan = plan.query_plan\n    discovery_run.completed_at = datetime.now(UTC)\n    discovery_run.error_message = None\n    db.commit()\n    db.refresh(discovery_run)\n    return discovery_run\n\n\ndef get_discovery_run(db: Session, run_id: str) -> DiscoveryRun:\n    discovery_run = db.get(DiscoveryRun, run_id)\n    if discovery_run is None:\n        raise DiscoveryRunNotFoundError(f\"Discovery run not found: {run_id}\")\n    return discovery_run\n\n\ndef list_lead_candidates(db: Session, run_id: str) -> list[LeadCandidate]:\n    get_discovery_run(db, run_id)\n    return (\n        db.query(LeadCandidate)\n        .filter(LeadCandidate.discovery_run_id == run_id)\n        .order_by(LeadCandidate.created_at.asc(), LeadCandidate.id.asc())\n        .all()\n    )\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "9e1918c90f46174003a12a21fb8249c15efd48a3", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/services/discovery_service.py", "display_title": "discovery_service.py"}

> ```text
> {"content": "from datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, status\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n\nfrom app.db.session import get_db\nfrom app.discovery.keywords import get_keyword_groups\nfrom app.discovery.place_resolver import PlaceDataError, PlaceNotFoundError, resolve_city\nfrom app.models.discovery_run import DiscoveryRun\nfrom app.models.lead_candidate import LeadCandidate\nfrom app.services.discovery_service import (\n    DiscoveryRunNotFoundError,\n    create_discovery_run,\n    get_discovery_run,\n    list_lead_candidates,\n)\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/discovery\", tags=[\"discovery\"])\n\n\ndef _format_datetime(value: datetime | None) -> str | None:\n    return value.isoformat() if value else None\n\n\ndef _serialize_discovery_run(discovery_run: DiscoveryRun) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    return {\n        \"id\": discovery_run.id,\n        \"city\": discovery_run.city,\n        \"normalized_city\": discovery_run.normalized_city,\n        \"status\": discovery_run.status.value,\n        \"postal_codes\": discovery_run.postal_codes,\n        \"keyword_groups\": discovery_run.keyword_groups,\n        \"query_plan\": discovery_run.query_plan,\n        \"created_at\": _format_datetime(discovery_run.created_at),\n        \"completed_at\": _format_datetime(discovery_run.completed_at),\n        \"error_message\": discovery_run.error_message,\n    }\n\n\ndef _serialize_candidate(candidate: LeadCandidate) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    return {\n        \"id\": candidate.id,\n        \"discovery_run_id\": candidate.discovery_run_id,\n        \"source\": candidate.source,\n        \"source_reference\": candidate.source_reference,\n        \"company_name\": candidate.company_name,\n        \"domain\": candidate.domain,\n        \"city\": candidate.city,\n        \"postal_code\": candidate.postal_code,\n        \"address\": candidate.address,\n        \"phone\": candidate.phone,\n        \"category\": candidate.category,\n        \"raw_data\": candidate.raw_data,\n        \"confidence_score\": candidate.confidence_score,\n        \"created_at\": _format_datetime(candidate.created_at),\n    }\n\n\n@router.get(\"/places/{city}\")\ndef get_places(city: str) -> dict[str, object]:\n    try:\n        matches = resolve_city(city)\n    except PlaceNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n    except PlaceDataError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            detail=str(exc),\n        ) from exc\n\n    return {\"city\": city, \"matches\": [match.to_dict() for match in matches]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/keywords\")\ndef get_keywords() -> dict[str, object]:\n    return {\"groups\": get_keyword_groups()}\n\n\n@router.post(\"/runs/{city}\")\ndef create_run(city: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, object]:\n    try:\n        discovery_run = create_discovery_run(db, city)\n    except PlaceNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n    except PlaceDataError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            detail=str(exc),\n        ) from exc\n\n    return {\n        \"discovery_run_id\": discovery_run.id,\n        \"city\": discovery_run.city,\n        \"status\": discovery_run.status.value,\n        \"postal_codes_count\": len(discovery_run.postal_codes),\n        \"query_count\": len(discovery_run.query_plan),\n    }\n\n\n@router.get(\"/runs/{run_id}\")\ndef read_run(run_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    try:\n        discovery_run = get_discovery_run(db, run_id)\n    except DiscoveryRunNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n    return _serialize_discovery_run(discovery_run)\n\n\n@router.get(\"/runs/{run_id}/candidates\")\ndef read_run_candidates(run_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, object]:\n    try:\n        candidates = list_lead_candidates(db, run_id)\n    except DiscoveryRunNotFoundError as exc:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n    return {\"discovery_run_id\": run_id, \"candidates\": [_serialize_candidate(candidate) for candidate in candidates]}\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "a5adfec132b2ccae9f22f3f7cbad0cabcfe2a1af", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/app/api/discovery.py", "display_title": "discovery.py"}

> ````text
> {"content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nNorthAccessBFSG is a FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\nThe current scope includes backend infrastructure, asynchronous scans, raw accessibility findings, compliance mapping basics, evidence bundle storage, and a minimal Lead Discovery foundation for city and keyword resolution.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance.py\n    discovery.py\n    evidence.py\n    health.py\n    scans.py\n  compliance/\n    __init__.py\n    rule_engine.py\n    rules/\n      __init__.py\n      wcag_en_bfsg_map.json\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    browser_config.py\n    celery.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  discovery/\n    __init__.py\n    keywords.py\n    place_resolver.py\n    query_planner.py\n  evidence/\n    __init__.py\n    hashing.py\n    storage.py\n    storage_backend.py\n  models/\n    discovery_run.py\n    lead_candidate.py\n  services/\n    discovery_service.py\n  workers/\ndata/\n  orte_deutschland.csv\n04_filter_quality.py\n05_run_pipeline.py\n13_city_guard.py\n14_evidence_gate.py\n15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the database tables on startup.\n\n## Lead Discovery Foundation\n\nThe canonical raw city data file is:\n\n```text\ndata/orte_deutschland.csv\n```\n\nThe file is semicolon-separated and must contain these columns:\n\n```text\nplz;stadt\n```\n\nThe resolver treats this file as raw input. The `stadt` column may contain real city names as well as organizations or special postal recipients, so city resolution uses exact normalized city-name matching only. It does not use substring matching.\n\nSupported normalization includes case-insensitive matching and German umlaut handling:\n\n```text\nL\u00fcbeck -> Luebeck -> lubeck\n```\n\nDiscovery endpoints:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/L\u00fcbeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/lubeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords\n```\n\nExample place response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n  \"matches\": [\n    {\n      \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n      \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n      \"country\": \"DE\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n## Discovery Query Planner\n\nA discovery run is a dry-run planning record. It resolves a city to postal codes, combines those postal codes with enabled keyword groups, and persists a deterministic query plan for a later external provider integration.\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/L\u00fcbeck\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"discovery_run_id\": \"<run-id>\",\n  \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n  \"status\": \"done\",\n  \"postal_codes_count\": 12,\n  \"query_count\": 336\n}\n```\n\nFetch a stored run and its candidate list:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates\n```\n\nThis is only a resolver, keyword, and query-planner foundation. It is not a live Google Maps API integration. It does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers. `LeadCandidate` rows are candidate records only; they are not confirmed legal applicability, BFSG violations, or legal conclusions.\n\n## Guarded Pipeline\n\nThe numbered guarded pipeline files validate that runtime dependencies are present and that the canonical city CSV is usable:\n\n```bash\npython -m py_compile \\\n  04_filter_quality.py \\\n  05_run_pipeline.py \\\n  13_city_guard.py \\\n  14_evidence_gate.py \\\n  15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n\npython 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n```\n\nThe guarded runner fails if `data/orte_deutschland.csv` is missing, unreadable, missing required columns, header-only, or contains zero usable rows.\n\n## Async Scan Flow\n\nCreate a lead directly in the database while a public lead API does not exist yet:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n```\n\nRun a scan for the lead:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nExpected immediate response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker launches Playwright headless Chromium, loads the lead domain, runs axe-core, captures evidence metadata, and persists raw findings.\n\nLifecycle:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nIf task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n\n## Compliance Mapping Flow\n\nRun deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nCompliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Browser Configuration\n\nThe worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n\n```text\nBROWSER_NAME=chromium\nBROWSER_HEADLESS=true\nBROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS=30000\nBROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS=10000\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH=1440\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=900\nBROWSER_RETRIES=1\nBROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL=networkidle\n```\n\nWebKit is supported by configuration but is not installed in the default image.\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "bc572ede6293b650435c0e3196235a28ab7ae060", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/main/README.md", "display_title": "README.md"}
> ````
>
> </details>
>
> `main` ist bestätigt auf dem Stand mit DiscoveryRun/LeadCandidate/Query-Planner. Ich lege jetzt einen neuen Branch direkt von `main` an und ergänze nur die Mock-Provider-Ausführungsschicht.

>   "branch": "codex/mock-provider-execution",
>   "path": "app/discovery/providers/base.py",
>   "message": "Add mock discovery provider execution",
>   "content": "\"\"\"Provider interface for discovery candidate sources.\n\nProvider adapters transform stored query-plan entries into candidate-like result\nobjects. Implementations must not make legal claims about applicability or\nviolations.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import asdict, dataclass\nfrom typing import Any, Protocol\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass ProviderResult:\n    source: str\n    source_reference: str | None = None\n    company_name: str | None = None\n    domain: str | None = None\n    city: str | None = None\n    postal_code: str | None = None\n    address: str | None = None\n    phone: str | None = None\n    category: str | None = None\n    raw_data: dict[str, Any] | None = None\n    confidence_score: float | None = None\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        data = asdict(self)\n        data[\"raw_data\"] = self.raw_data or {}\n        return data\n\n\nclass DiscoveryProvider(Protocol):\n    source: str\n\n    def search(self, query_plan: list[dict[str, str]]) -> list[ProviderResult]:\n        \"\"\"Return provider results for query-plan entries.\"\"\"\n"
> }
> ```

> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/mock-provider-execution",
>   "path": "app/discovery/providers/mock_provider.py",
>   "message": "Add mock discovery provider execution",
>   "content": "\"\"\"Deterministic mock provider for discovery candidate persistence tests.\n\nThis adapter creates obvious test data only. It does not call external services,\ndoes not scrape, and does not represent real businesses.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\n\nfrom app.discovery.providers.base import ProviderResult\n\nMAX_MOCK_RESULTS = 10\n_SLUG_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"[^a-z0-9]+\")\n\n\ndef _title_keyword(keyword: str) -> str:\n    return \" \".join(part.capitalize() for part in keyword.split())\n\n\ndef _slug(value: str) -> str:\n    normalized = value.casefold().replace(\"ä\", \"ae\").replace(\"ö\", \"oe\").replace(\"ü\", \"ue\").replace(\"ß\", \"ss\")\n    slug = _SLUG_PATTERN.sub(\"-\", normalized).strip(\"-\")\n    return slug or \"unknown\"\n\n\nclass MockDiscoveryProvider:\n    source = \"mock\"\n\n    def __init__(self, max_results: int = MAX_MOCK_RESULTS) -> None:\n        self.max_results = max_results\n\n    def search(self, query_plan: list[dict[str, str]]) -> list[ProviderResult]:\n        results: list[ProviderResult] = []\n        for entry in query_plan[: self.max_results]:\n            city = entry.get(\"city\") or \"Unknown City\"\n            postal_code = entry.get(\"postal_code\") or \"00000\"\n            keyword = entry.get(\"keyword\") or \"unknown\"\n            keyword_group_id = entry.get(\"keyword_group_id\") or \"unknown\"\n            query_text = entry.get(\"query_text\") or \"\"\n            reference = f\"mock:{_slug(city)}:{postal_code}:{_slug(keyword_group_id)}:{_slug(keyword)}\"\n\n            results.append(\n                ProviderResult(\n                    source=self.source,\n                    source_reference=reference,\n                    company_name=f\"Mock Candidate {city} {postal_code} {_title_keyword(keyword)}\",\n                    domain=None,\n                    city=city,\n                    postal_code=postal_code,\n                    address=None,\n                    phone=None,\n                    category=keyword_group_id,\n                    raw_data={\n                        \"provider\": self.source,\n                        \"mock\": True,\n                        \"query_text\": query_text,\n                        \"keyword_group_id\": keyword_group_id,\n                        \"keyword\": keyword,\n                    },\n                    confidence_score=0.5,\n                )\n            )\n        return results\n"
> }
> ```

> Provider-Abstraktion und Mock sind drin. Jetzt ergänze ich den Ausführungsservice mit Idempotenz: gleiche `discovery_run_id + source + source_reference` wird nicht doppelt persistiert.
>
> <details><summary>Called 1 tool</summary>
>
> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_create_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/mock-provider-execution",
>   "path": "app/services/provider_execution_service.py",
>   "message": "Persist mock provider candidates",
>   "content": "from __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n\nfrom app.discovery.providers.base import ProviderResult\nfrom app.discovery.providers.mock_provider import MockDiscoveryProvider\nfrom app.models.lead_candidate import LeadCandidate\nfrom app.services.discovery_service import DiscoveryRunNotFoundError, get_discovery_run\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass ProviderExecutionSummary:\n    discovery_run_id: str\n    provider: str\n    candidates_created: int\n    candidates_total: int\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, str | int]:\n        return {\n            \"discovery_run_id\": self.discovery_run_id,\n            \"provider\": self.provider,\n            \"candidates_created\": self.candidates_created,\n            \"candidates_total\": self.candidates_total,\n        }\n\n\ndef execute_mock_provider(db: Session, discovery_run_id: str) -> ProviderExecutionSummary:\n    discovery_run = get_discovery_run(db, discovery_run_id)\n    provider = MockDiscoveryProvider()\n    results = provider.search(discovery_run.query_plan)\n\n    candidates_created = 0\n    for result in results:\n        if result.source_reference and _candidate_exists(db, discovery_run_id, result):\n            continue\n\n        db.add(_candidate_from_provider_result(discovery_run_id, result))\n        candidates_created += 1\n\n    db.commit()\n    candidates_total = (\n        db.query(LeadCandidate)\n        .filter(\n            LeadCandidate.discovery_run_id == discovery_run_id,\n            LeadCandidate.source == provider.source,\n        )\n        .count()\n    )\n    return ProviderExecutionSummary(\n        discovery_run_id=discovery_run_id,\n        provider=provider.source,\n        candidates_created=candidates_created,\n        candidates_total=candidates_total,\n    )\n\n\ndef _candidate_exists(db: Session, discovery_run_id: str, result: ProviderResult) -> bool:\n    return (\n        db.query(LeadCandidate.id)\n        .filter(\n            LeadCandidate.discovery_run_id == discovery_run_id,\n            LeadCandidate.source == result.source,\n            LeadCandidate.source_reference == result.source_reference,\n        )\n        .first()\n        is not None\n    )\n\n\ndef _candidate_from_provider_result(\n    discovery_run_id: str, result: ProviderResult\n) -> LeadCandidate:\n    return LeadCandidate(\n        discovery_run_id=discovery_run_id,\n        source=result.source,\n        source_reference=result.source_reference,\n        company_name=result.company_name,\n        domain=result.domain,\n        city=result.city,\n        postal_code=result.postal_code,\n        address=result.address,\n        phone=result.phone,\n        category=result.category,\n        raw_data=result.raw_data or {},\n        confidence_score=result.confidence_score,\n    )\n\n\n__all__ = [\n    \"DiscoveryRunNotFoundError\",\n    \"ProviderExecutionSummary\",\n    \"execute_mock_provider\",\n]\n"
> }
> ```

list_lead_candidates,\n)\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/discovery\", tags=[\"discovery\"])\n\n\ndef _format_datetime(value: datetime | None) -> str | None:\n    return value.isoformat() if value else None\n\n\ndef _serialize_discovery_run(discovery_run: DiscoveryRun) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    return {\n        \"id\": discovery_run.id,\n        \"city\": discovery_run.city,\n [match.to_dict() for match in matches]}\n\n\n@router.get(\"/keywords\")\ndef get_keywords() -> dict[str, object]:\n    return {\"groups\": len(discovery_run.query_plan),\n    }\n\n\n@router.get(\"/runs/{run_id}\")\ndef read_run(run_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    try:\n        discovery_run = >   "path": "tests/test_mock_provider.py",
>   "message": "Add mock provider execution tests",
>   "content": "import socket\n\nfrom app.discovery.providers.mock_provider import MockDiscoveryProvider\n\n\nQUERY_PLAN = [\n    {\n        \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n        \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n        \"keyword_group_id\": \"ecommerce\",\n        \"keyword\": \"online shop\",\n        \"query_text\": \"Lübeck 23552 online shop\",\n    },\n    {\n        \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n        \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n        \"keyword_group_id\": \"banking\",\n        \"keyword\": \"bank\",\n        \"query_text\": \"Lübeck 23552 bank\",\n    },\n]\n\n\ndef test_mock_provider_returns_deterministic_results() -> None:\n    provider = MockDiscoveryProvider(max_results=2)\n\n    first = provider.search(QUERY_PLAN)\n    second = provider.search(QUERY_PLAN)\n\n    assert first == second\n    assert len(first) == 2\n    assert first[0].source == \"mock\"\n    assert first[0].source_reference == \"mock:luebeck:23552:ecommerce:online-shop\"\n    assert first[0].company_name == \"Mock Candidate Lübeck 23552 Online Shop\"\n    assert first[0].domain is None\n    assert first[0].raw_data == {\n        \"provider\": \"mock\",\n        \"mock\": True,\n        \"query_text\": \"Lübeck 23552 online shop\",\n        \"keyword_group_id\": \"ecommerce\",\n        \"keyword\": \"online shop\",\n    }\n\n\ndef test_mock_provider_makes_no_external_network_calls(monkeypatch) -> None:\n    def fail_network(*args, **kwargs):\n        raise AssertionError(\"network access is not allowed in mock provider\")\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(socket, \"create_connection\", fail_network)\n\n    results = MockDiscoveryProvider(max_results=1).search(QUERY_PLAN)\n\n    assert len(results) == 1\n"
> }
> ```

> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/mock-provider-execution",
>   "path": "tests/test_provider_execution_service.py",
>   "message": "Add mock provider execution tests",
>   "content": "import pytest\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.models import DiscoveryRun, LeadCandidate  # noqa: F401\nfrom app.services.discovery_service import DiscoveryRunNotFoundError, create_discovery_run\nfrom app.services.provider_execution_service import execute_mock_provider\n\n\n@pytest.fixture()\ndef db_session() -> Session:\n    engine = create_engine(\"sqlite:///:memory:\")\n    TestingSessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine)\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n\n    session = TestingSessionLocal()\n    try:\n        yield session\n    finally:\n        session.close()\n        Base.metadata.drop_all(bind=engine)\n\n\ndef test_provider_execution_persists_lead_candidates(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    discovery_run = create_discovery_run(db_session, \"Lübeck\")\n\n    summary = execute_mock_provider(db_session, discovery_run.id)\n\n    assert summary.discovery_run_id == discovery_run.id\n    assert summary.provider == \"mock\"\n    assert summary.candidates_created == 10\n    assert summary.candidates_total == 10\n\n    candidates = db_session.query(LeadCandidate).all()\n    assert len(candidates) == 10\n    assert candidates[0].source == \"mock\"\n    assert candidates[0].company_name.startswith(\"Mock Candidate Lübeck\")\n    assert candidates[0].raw_data[\"mock\"] is True\n\n\ndef test_duplicate_provider_execution_does_not_duplicate_candidates(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    discovery_run = create_discovery_run(db_session, \"Lübeck\")\n\n    first = execute_mock_provider(db_session, discovery_run.id)\n    second = execute_mock_provider(db_session, discovery_run.id)\n\n    assert first.candidates_created == 10\n    assert first.candidates_total == 10\n    assert second.candidates_created == 0\n    assert second.candidates_total == 10\n    assert db_session.query(LeadCandidate).count() == 10\n\n\ndef test_provider_execution_unknown_run_fails_clearly(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    with pytest.raises(DiscoveryRunNotFoundError, match=\"Discovery run not found: missing\"):\n        execute_mock_provider(db_session, \"missing\")\n"
> }

> {"content": "import pytest\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\nfrom fastapi.testclient import TestClient\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\nfrom sqlalchemy.pool import StaticPool\n\nfrom app.api.discovery import router as discovery_router\nfrom app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.db.session import get_db\nfrom app.models import DiscoveryRun, LeadCandidate  # noqa: F401\n\n\n@pytest.fixture()\ndef db_session() -> Session:\n    engine = create_engine(\n        \"sqlite://\",\n        connect_args={\"check_same_thread\": False},\n        poolclass=StaticPool,\n    )\n    TestingSessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine)\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n\n    session = TestingSessionLocal()\n    try:\n        yield session\n    finally:\n        session.close()\n        Base.metadata.drop_all(bind=engine)\n\n\ndef make_client(db_session: Session | None = None) -> TestClient:\n    app = FastAPI()\n    app.include_router(discovery_router)\n    if db_session is not None:\n        app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = lambda: db_session\n    return TestClient(app)\n\n\ndef test_keywords_endpoint_returns_structured_groups() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/keywords\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    groups = response.json()[\"groups\"]\n    assert groups\n    assert {\"group_id\", \"label\", \"keywords\", \"bfsg_relevance_reason\", \"enabled\"}.issubset(\n        groups[0]\n    )\n    assert \"ecommerce\" in {group[\"group_id\"] for group in groups}\n\n\ndef test_places_endpoint_returns_luebeck_matches() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/L\u00fcbeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    payload = response.json()\n    assert payload[\"city\"] == \"L\u00fcbeck\"\n    assert payload[\"matches\"]\n    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in payload[\"matches\"]} == {\"L\u00fcbeck\"}\n    assert any(match[\"postal_code\"] == \"23552\" for match in payload[\"matches\"])\n\n\ndef test_places_endpoint_accepts_luebeck_transliteration() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/Luebeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in response.json()[\"matches\"]} == {\"L\u00fcbeck\"}\n\n\ndef test_create_discovery_run_endpoint_returns_done_and_query_count(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n\n    response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/L\u00fcbeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    payload = response.json()\n    assert payload[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n    assert payload[\"city\"] == \"L\u00fcbeck\"\n    assert payload[\"status\"] == \"done\"\n    assert payload[\"postal_codes_count\"] > 0\n    assert payload[\"query_count\"] > 0\n\n\ndef test_get_discovery_run_endpoint_returns_stored_query_plan(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/L\u00fcbeck\")\n    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n\n    response = client.get(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    payload = response.json()\n    assert payload[\"id\"] == run_id\n    assert payload[\"status\"] == \"done\"\n    assert payload[\"postal_codes\"]\n    assert payload[\"keyword_groups\"]\n    assert payload[\"query_plan\"]\n    assert payload[\"error_message\"] is None\n\n\ndef test_discovery_run_candidates_endpoint_returns_empty_list(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/L\u00fcbeck\")\n    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n\n    response = client.get(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/candidates\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert response.json() == {\"discovery_run_id\": run_id, \"candidates\": []}\n\n\ndef test_create_discovery_run_unknown_city_returns_clear_error(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n\n > Die Tests decken jetzt den API-Flow ab. Ich aktualisiere noch das README mit dem Mock-Provider-Schritt und dem klaren Hinweis, dass es nur Testkandidaten sind.

> <details><summary>Called 2 tools</summary>

>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "ref": "codex/mock-provider-execution",
>   "path": "README.md"
> }
> ```
>
> ````text
> {"content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nNorthAccessBFSG is a FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\nThe current scope includes backend infrastructure, asynchronous scans, raw accessibility findings, compliance mapping basics, evidence bundle storage, and a minimal Lead Discovery foundation for city and keyword resolution.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance.py\n    discovery.py\n    evidence.py\n    health.py\n    scans.py\n  compliance/\n    __init__.py\n    rule_engine.py\n    rules/\n      __init__.py\n      wcag_en_bfsg_map.json\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    browser_config.py\n    celery.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  discovery/\n    __init__.py\n    keywords.py\n    place_resolver.py\n    query_planner.py\n  evidence/\n    __init__.py\n    hashing.py\n    storage.py\n    storage_backend.py\n  models/\n    discovery_run.py\n    lead_candidate.py\n  services/\n    discovery_service.py\n  workers/\ndata/\n  orte_deutschland.csv\n04_filter_quality.py\n05_run_pipeline.py\n13_city_guard.py\n14_evidence_gate.py\n15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the database tables on startup.\n\n## Lead Discovery Foundation\n\nThe canonical raw city data file is:\n\n```text\ndata/orte_deutschland.csv\n```\n\nThe file is semicolon-separated and must contain these columns:\n\n```text\nplz;stadt\n```\n\nThe resolver treats this file as raw input. The `stadt` column may contain real city names as well as organizations or special postal recipients, so city resolution uses exact normalized city-name matching only. It does not use substring matching.\n\nSupported normalization includes case-insensitive matching and German umlaut handling:\n\n```text\nL\u00fcbeck -> Luebeck -> lubeck\n```\n\nDiscovery endpoints:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/L\u00fcbeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/lubeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords\n```\n\nExample place response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n  \"matches\": [\n    {\n      \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n      \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n      \"country\": \"DE\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n## Discovery Query Planner\n\nA discovery run is a dry-run planning record. It resolves a city to postal codes, combines those postal codes with enabled keyword groups, and persists a deterministic query plan for a later external provider integration.\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/L\u00fcbeck\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"discovery_run_id\": \"<run-id>\",\n  \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n  \"status\": \"done\",\n  \"postal_codes_count\": 12,\n  \"query_count\": 336\n}\n```\n\nFetch a stored run and its candidate list:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates\n```\n\nThis is only a resolver, keyword, and query-planner foundation. It is not a live Google Maps API integration. It does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers. `LeadCandidate` rows are candidate records only; they are not confirmed legal applicability, BFSG violations, or legal conclusions.\n\n## Guarded Pipeline\n\nThe numbered guarded pipeline files validate that runtime dependencies are present and that the canonical city CSV is usable:\n\n```bash\npython -m py_compile \\\n  04_filter_quality.py \\\n  05_run_pipeline.py \\\n  13_city_guard.py \\\n  14_evidence_gate.py \\\n  15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n\npython 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n```\n\nThe guarded runner fails if `data/orte_deutschland.csv` is missing, unreadable, missing required columns, header-only, or contains zero usable rows.\n\n## Async Scan Flow\n\nCreate a lead directly in the database while a public lead API does not exist yet:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n```\n\nRun a scan for the lead:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nExpected immediate response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker launches Playwright headless Chromium, loads the lead domain, runs axe-core, captures evidence metadata, and persists raw findings.\n\nLifecycle:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nIf task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n\n## Compliance Mapping Flow\n\nRun deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nCompliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Browser Configuration\n\nThe worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n\n```text\nBROWSER_NAME=chromium\nBROWSER_HEADLESS=true\nBROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS=30000\nBROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS=10000\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH=1440\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=900\nBROWSER_RETRIES=1\nBROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL=networkidle\n```\n\nWebKit is supported by configuration but is not installed in the default image.\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "bc572ede6293b650435c0e3196235a28ab7ae060", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/mock-provider-execution/README.md", "display_title": "README.md"}
> ````
>

>

> {

>   "path": "README.md",
>   "sha": "bc572ede6293b650435c0e3196235a28ab7ae060",
>   "message": "Document mock discovery provider",
>   "content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nNorthAccessBFSG is a FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\nThe current scope includes backend infrastructure, asynchronous scans, raw accessibility findings, compliance mapping basics, evidence bundle storage, and a minimal Lead Discovery foundation for city and keyword resolution.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance.py\n    discovery.py\n    evidence.py\n    health.py\n    scans.py\n  compliance/\n    __init__.py\n    rule_engine.py\n    rules/\n      __init__.py\n      wcag_en_bfsg_map.json\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    browser_config.py\n    celery.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  discovery/\n    __init__.py\n    keywords.py\n    place_resolver.py\n    providers/\n      __init__.py\n      base.py\n      mock_provider.py\n    query_planner.py\n  evidence/\n    __init__.py\n    hashing.py\n    storage.py\n    storage_backend.py\n  models/\n    discovery_run.py\n    lead_candidate.py\n  services/\n    discovery_service.py\n    provider_execution_service.py\n  workers/\ndata/\n  orte_deutschland.csv\n04_filter_quality.py\n05_run_pipeline.py\n13_city_guard.py\n14_evidence_gate.py\n15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the database tables on startup.\n\n## Lead Discovery Foundation\n\nThe canonical raw city data file is:\n\n```text\ndata/orte_deutschland.csv\n```\n\nThe file is semicolon-separated and must contain these columns:\n\n```text\nplz;stadt\n```\n\nThe resolver treats this file as raw input. The `stadt` column may contain real city names as well as organizations or special postal recipients, so city resolution uses exact normalized city-name matching only. It does not use substring matching.\n\nSupported normalization includes case-insensitive matching and German umlaut handling:\n\n```text\nLübeck -> Luebeck -> lubeck\n```\n\nDiscovery endpoints:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Lübeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/lubeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords\n```\n\nExample place response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n  \"matches\": [\n    {\n      \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n      \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n      \"country\": \"DE\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n## Discovery Query Planner\n\nA discovery run is a dry-run planning record. It resolves a city to postal codes, combines those postal codes with enabled keyword groups, and persists a deterministic query plan for a later external provider integration.\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/Lübeck\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"discovery_run_id\": \"<run-id>\",\n  \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n  \"status\": \"done\",\n  \"postal_codes_count\": 12,\n  \"query_count\": 336\n}\n```\n\nFetch a stored run and its candidate list:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates\n```\n\n## Mock Discovery Provider\n\nThe mock provider executes a stored discovery run query plan and persists bounded, deterministic test candidates. It creates obvious mock rows only, for example `Mock Candidate Lübeck 23552 Online Shop`.\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/providers/mock\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates\n```\n\nExample provider response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"discovery_run_id\": \"<run-id>\",\n  \"provider\": \"mock\",\n  \"candidates_created\": 10,\n  \"candidates_total\": 10\n}\n```\n\nThis is only a resolver, keyword, query-planner, and mock-provider persistence foundation. Google Maps and live providers are not integrated yet. The system does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers. `LeadCandidate` rows are candidate records only; they are not confirmed legal applicability, BFSG violations, or legal conclusions.\n\n## Guarded Pipeline\n\nThe numbered guarded pipeline files validate that runtime dependencies are present and that the canonical city CSV is usable:\n\n```bash\npython -m py_compile \\\n  04_filter_quality.py \\\n  05_run_pipeline.py \\\n  13_city_guard.py \\\n  14_evidence_gate.py \\\n  15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n\npython 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n```\n\nThe guarded runner fails if `data/orte_deutschland.csv` is missing, unreadable, missing required columns, header-only, or contains zero usable rows.\n\n## Async Scan Flow\n\nCreate a lead directly in the database while a public lead API does not exist yet:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n```\n\nRun a scan for the lead:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nExpected immediate response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker launches Playwright headless Chromium, loads the lead domain, runs axe-core, captures evidence metadata, and persists raw findings.\n\nLifecycle:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nIf task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n\n## Compliance Mapping Flow\n\nRun deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nCompliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Browser Configuration\n\nThe worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n\n```text\nBROWSER_NAME=chromium\nBROWSER_HEADLESS=true\nBROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS=30000\nBROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS=10000\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH=1440\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=900\nBROWSER_RETRIES=1\nBROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL=networkidle\n```\n\nWebKit is supported by configuration but is not installed in the default image.\n"
> }
> ````

- README docs for mock/test candidate flow

> # Role
> You are a senior backend/platform engineer working on:
>
> DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG
>
> # Project Naming Rule
> The project is called NorthAccessBFSG.
> Do NOT introduce bfsg-auditor-pro.
> Do NOT rename the project.
>
> # Base
> Use current main as base.
> Create a new branch.
>
> # Current State
> main is validated and contains:
> - Discovery place resolver
> - DiscoveryRun model
> - LeadCandidate model
> - deterministic query planner
> - provider abstraction
> - mock provider execution
> - endpoint:
>   - POST /discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/mock
>
> Latest main validation:
> - pytest: 24 passed
> - guarded pipeline: passed
>
> # Goal
> Add a Google Places API provider adapter foundation AND prepare the company qualification gate.
>
> Important:
> Google Places / Places API is ONLY a seed source.
> It must NOT be treated as proof that a company is BFSG-relevant or legally obligated.
>
> The intended future pipeline is:
>
> City + keyword query plan
> -> Google Places API seed candidates
> -> candidate deduplication
> -> company enrichment / Northdata-style company check
> -> BFSG applicability precheck
> -> only then qualified audit candidates
> -> only then website flow detection / accessibility scanning
>
> This PR must prepare the provider integration and the qualification foundation safely.
>
> Do NOT execute real Google Places API calls in tests.
> Do NOT implement live Northdata calls yet.
> Do NOT scrape.
> Do NOT add Playwright changes.
> Do NOT add reporting.
> Do NOT add legal scoring as a final conclusion.
> Do NOT overbuild.
>
> # Critical Business Rule
> Do NOT treat all Places API results as valid targets.
>
> Places results are only raw LeadCandidate seeds.
>
> Before a LeadCandidate can later move toward scan/audit/letter generation, the system must support checking:
> - plausible company identity
> - legal form / registry signals if available
> - employee count signal
> - revenue or balance-sheet signal
> - B2C / consumer-facing signal
> - BFSG-relevant product/service category signal
> - website/domain availability
> - human review requirement for uncertainty
>
> Northdata or comparable company-data enrichment must happen BEFORE a candidate is treated as qualified.
>
> In this PR:
> - create the schema/service foundation for qualification
> - do NOT implement live Northdata API calls
> - do NOT make legal conclusions
> - store only signals, confidence, and review status
>
> # Required Work
>
> ## 1. Add config
>
> Extend app/core/config.py with:
> - google_places_api_key: str | None
> - google_places_enabled: bool = False
> - google_places_timeout_seconds: int = 10
> - google_places_max_results_per_query: int = 5
>
> Also add qualification config constants:
> - bfsg_microenterprise_employee_threshold: int = 10
> - bfsg_microenterprise_revenue_threshold_eur: int = 2000000
> - bfsg_microenterprise_balance_threshold_eur: int = 2000000
>
> Important:
> - Google Places must be disabled by default.
> - No API key required for normal tests.
> - Do not print API keys.
> - Thresholds are used only as applicability signals, not legal conclusions.
>
> ## 2. Rename wording to Google Places API
>
> Use “Google Places API” or “Places API” wording in code/docs.
> Do not call it only “Google Maps provider” internally unless needed for compatibility.
>
> Preferred provider source value:
> google_places
>
> Endpoint can be:
> POST /discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/google-places
>
> Do not create confusing duplicate google-maps naming unless backwards compatibility requires it.
>
> ## 3. Add Google Places provider module
>
> Create:
>
> app/discovery/providers/google_places_provider.py
>
> Implement a provider class using the existing provider abstraction.
>
> It should:
> - accept query_plan entries
> - require google_places_enabled=True and google_places_api_key present before live use
> - fail clearly if disabled or missing key
> - convert Google Places Text Search-like response data into ProviderResult
> - map:
>   - place_id -> source_reference
>   - displayName.text or name -> company_name
>   - websiteUri or website -> domain
>   - formattedAddress or formatted_address -> address
>   - nationalPhoneNumber / internationalPhoneNumber / formatted_phone_number -> phone
>   - types -> category/raw_data
>   - source = "google_places"
> - keep raw provider payload in raw_data
> - include query_text and keyword_group_id in raw_data when available
> - avoid legal claims
>
> Important:
> - If actual HTTP client is added, isolate it behind a small method that can be mocked.
> - Use httpx only if already available in requirements.
> - No hard dependency explosion.
> - No real network calls in tests.
>
> ## 4. Add company qualification foundation
>
> Create model:
>
> app/models/company_qualification.py
>
> Fields:
> - id
> - lead_candidate_id FK
> - status:
>   - pending
>   - needs_company_data
>   - likely_not_applicable
>   - possible_bfsg_candidate
>   - needs_human_review
>   - rejected
> - company_name nullable
> - legal_form nullable
> - registry_id nullable
> - northdata_url nullable
> - employee_count nullable
> - annual_revenue_eur nullable
> - balance_sheet_total_eur nullable
> - is_microenterprise nullable
> - b2c_signal nullable
> - bfsg_category_signal nullable
> - website_signal nullable
> - confidence_score nullable
> - evidence JSON
> - notes nullable
> - created_at
> - updated_at
>
> Important:
> - This is a precheck signal table.
> - Do NOT call it legal compliance.
> - Do NOT say “legally obligated”.
> - Do NOT create final violation/legal conclusions.
> - This table prepares future Northdata/company enrichment.
>
> Wire model into:
> - app/models/__init__.py
> - app/db/init_db.py
>
> Use SQLAlchemy style consistent with the current codebase.
>
> ## 5. Add company qualification service
>
> Create:
>
> app/services/company_qualification_service.py
>
> It should expose:
>
> precheck_candidate(candidate: LeadCandidate) -> CompanyQualification
>
> For this PR:
> - no Northdata API call
> - no external calls
> - use only existing candidate fields and raw_data
> - if company data is missing, status should be needs_company_data or needs_human_review
> - if candidate source is google_places/mock and has company_name + city/category, mark as pending or needs_company_data
> - if clearly mock data, keep status as needs_company_data or needs_human_review and evidence should say it is mock/test data
> - calculate is_microenterprise only if employee/revenue/balance data is present
> - otherwise leave is_microenterprise null
>
> Add helper logic:
> - if employee_count < threshold AND revenue/balance data <= threshold, set is_microenterprise=True
> - if employee_count >= threshold OR revenue/balance data > threshold, set is_microenterprise=False
> - if data missing, set is_microenterprise=None and status needs_company_data
>
> No legal conclusions.
>
> ## 6. Add optional qualification endpoint
>
> Add endpoint:
>
> POST /discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification/precheck
>
> Response:
> {
>   "candidate_id": "...",
>   "qualification_id": "...",
>   "status": "needs_company_data",
>   "is_microenterprise": null,
>   "confidence_score": 0.3
> }
>
> Also add:
>
> GET /discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification
>
> Returns latest qualification/precheck if present.
>
> If this is too large, implement only the POST endpoint and tests.
>
> ## 7. Add provider execution endpoint
>
> Add endpoint:
>
> POST /discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/google-places
>
> Behavior:
> - If google_places_enabled is false, return clear 503 or 400 error.
> - If API key missing, return clear error.
> - If enabled and key exists, execute provider against stored query_plan.
> - Persist LeadCandidate rows through existing provider execution flow.
> - Deduplicate by discovery_run_id + source + source_reference.
> - Do NOT auto-mark candidates as qualified.
> - Candidates remain raw seeds until company qualification precheck is run.
>
> Do not break existing mock endpoint.
>
> ## 8. Refactor provider execution service minimally
>
> If needed, allow provider execution service to accept provider instance/name cleanly:
> - mock
> - google_places
>
> Do not break:
> POST /discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/mock
>
> ## 9. Tests
>
> Add tests for:
> - Google Places provider fails clearly when disabled
> - Google Places provider fails clearly when API key missing
> - Google Places response mapping to ProviderResult
> - google-places endpoint returns clear error when disabled
> - mock provider still works
> - no real network calls are made in tests
> - CompanyQualification model/service can create a precheck for a LeadCandidate
> - missing employee/revenue/balance data results in needs_company_data or needs_human_review, not final qualification
> - microenterprise helper works with provided employee/revenue/balance values
> - mock candidate qualification does not create a legal conclusion
>
> ## 10. README
>
> Update README:
> - document Google Places API provider is disabled by default
> - document required env vars:
>   - GOOGLE_PLACES_ENABLED
>   - GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY
>   - GOOGLE_PLACES_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
>   - GOOGLE_PLACES_MAX_RESULTS_PER_QUERY
> - document that tests do not call Google
> - document endpoint:
>   POST /discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/google-places
> - document qualification/precheck endpoint if added
> - clarify:
>   - Places results are raw seed candidates
>   - Northdata/company enrichment is required before qualification
>   - LeadCandidate is not a legal conclusion
>   - CompanyQualification is only a signal/precheck layer
>   - no scraping is performed
>

>
> Run:
>
> python3 -m py_compile \
>   04_filter_quality.py \
>   05_run_pipeline.py \
>   13_city_guard.py \
>   14_evidence_gate.py \
>   15_run_pipeline_guarded.py
>
> python3 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py
>
> python3 -m pytest -q
>
> docker compose up --build
>
> Manual curl checks:
>
> curl http://localhost:8000/health
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/Luebeck
>
> Use returned discovery_run_id:
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/providers/mock
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates
>
> Also test disabled Google Places endpoint:
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/providers/google-places
>
> Expected:
> - clear disabled/missing config error
> - no server crash
> - no real external call
>
> If qualification endpoint is implemented:
> - take one candidate_id from candidates endpoint
> - call:
>   curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification/precheck
>   curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification
>
> Expected:
> - status is needs_company_data or needs_human_review for mock/test candidates
> - no legal conclusion
>
> # Constraints
> - No scraping.
> - No Playwright changes.
> - No report generation.
> - No legal scoring as final conclusion.
> - No real API call during tests.
> - No live Northdata API call in this PR.
> - Keep PR small and reviewable.
> - Project name remains NorthAccessBFSG.
> - Google Places results are seed candidates only.
> - Company qualification/Northdata check must exist before scan/audit promotion in future architecture.
>
> # Expected Output
> Return:
> 1. Branch name
> 2. PR link
> 3. Changed files
> 4. What was added
> 5. Validation results
> 6. Manual curl results
> 7. Risks/TODOs
> 8. safe to merge: yes/no

import ProviderResult\n+\n+MAX_MOCK_RESULTS = 10\n+_SLUG_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"[^a-z0-9]+\")\n+\n+\n+def _title_keyword(keyword: str) -> str:\n+    return \" \".join(part.capitalize() for part in keyword.split())\n+\n+\n+def _slug(value: str) -> str:\n+    normalized = value.casefold().replace(\"\u00e4\", \"ae\").replace(\"\u00f6\", \"oe\").replace(\"\u00fc\", \"ue\").replace(\"\u00df\", \"ss\")\n+    slug = _SLUG_PATTERN.sub(\"-\", normalized).strip(\"-\")\n+    return slug or \"unknown\"\n+\n+\n+class MockDiscoveryProvider:\n+    source = \"mock\"\n+\n+    def __init__(self, max_results: int = MAX_MOCK_RESULTS) -> None:\n+        self.max_results = max_results\n+\n+    def search(self, query_plan: list[dict[str, str]]) -> list[ProviderResult]:\n+        results: list[ProviderResult] = []\n+        for entry in query_plan[: self.max_results]:\n+            city = entry.get(\"city\") or \"Unknown City\"\n+            postal_code = entry.get(\"postal_code\") or \"00000\"\n+            keyword = entry.get(\"keyword\") or \"unknown\"\n+            keyword_group_id = entry.get(\"keyword_group_id\") or \"unknown\"\n+            query_text = entry.get(\"query_text\") or \"\"\n+            reference = f\"mock:{_slug(city)}:{postal_code}:{_slug(keyword_group_id)}:{_slug(keyword)}\"\n+\n+            results.append(\n+                ProviderResult(\n+                    source=self.source,\n+                    source_reference=reference,\n+                    company_name=f\"Mock Candidate {city} {postal_code}                \"query_text\": query_text,\n+                        \"keyword_group_id\": keyword_group_id,\n+                        \"keyword\": keyword,\n+                    },\n+ import LeadCandidate\n+from app.services.discovery_service import DiscoveryRunNotFoundError, get_discovery_run\n+\n+\n+@dataclass(frozen=True)\n+class ProviderExecutionSummary:\n+    discovery_run_id: _candidate_exists(db, discovery_run_id, result):\n+            continue\n+\n+        db.add(_candidate_from_provider_result(discovery_run_id, result))\n+        candidates_created += 1\n+\n+    db.commit()\n+    candidates_total = (\n+        db.query(LeadCandidate)\n+        .filter(\n+            LeadCandidate.discovery_run_id == discovery_run_id,\n+            LeadCandidate.source == candidates_created=candidates_created,\n+        candidates_total=candidates_total,\n+    )\n+\n+\n+def _candidate_exists(db: Session, discovery_run_id: str, result: ProviderResult) -> bool:\n+ source_reference=result.source_reference,\n+        company_name=result.company_name,\n+        domain=result.domain,\n+        city=result.city,\n+        postal_code=result.postal_code,\n+        address=result.address,\n+        phone=result.phone,\n+        category=result.category,\n+        raw_data=result.raw_data or {},\n+        confidence_score=result.confidence_score,\n+ first.candidates_total == 10\n+    assert second.candidates_created == 0\n+    assert second.candidates_total == 10\n+    assert db_session.query(LeadCandidate).count() == 10\n+\n+\n+def test_provider_execution_unknown_run_fails_clearly(db_session: Session) -> None:\n+    with pytest.raises(DiscoveryRunNotFoundError, match=\"Discovery run not found: missing\"):\n+    __init__.py\n+      base.py\n+      mock_provider.py\n     query_planner.py\n   evidence/\n     __init__.py\n@@ -48,6 +52,7 @@ app/\n     lead_candidate.py\n   services/\n It is not a live Google Maps API integration. It does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers. `LeadCandidate` rows are candidate records only; they are not confirmed legal applicability, BFSG violations, or legal conclusions.\n+## Mock Discovery Provider\n+\n+The mock provider executes a stored discovery run query plan and persists bounded, deterministic test candidates. It creates obvious mock rows only, for example `Mock Candidate L\u00fcbeck 23552 Online Shop`.\n+\n+```bash\n+curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/providers/mock\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates\n+```\n+\n+Example provider response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"discovery_run_id\": \"<run-id>\",\n+  \"provider\": \"mock\",\n+  \"candidates_created\": 10,\n+  \"candidates_total\": 10\n+}\n+```\n+\n+This is only a resolver, keyword, query-planner, and mock-provider persistence foundation. Google Maps and live providers are not integrated yet. The system does not scrape websites and does not call external discovery providers. `LeadCandidate` rows are candidate records only; they are not confirmed legal applicability, BFSG violations, or legal conclusions.\n \n ## Guarded Pipeline\n "}, {"filename": "app/api/discovery.py", "patch": "@@ -15,6 +15,7 @@\n     get_discovery_run,\n     list_lead_candidates,\n )\n+from app.services.provider_execution_service import execute_mock_provider\n \n router = APIRouter(prefix=\"/discovery\", tags=[\"discovery\"])\n \n@@ -107,6 +108,15 @@ def read_run(run_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, Any]:\n     return _serialize_discovery_run(discovery_run)\n \n \n+@router.post(\"/runs/{run_id}/providers/mock\")\n+def run_mock_provider(run_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, str | int]:\n+    try:\n+        summary = execute_mock_provider(db, run_id)\n+    except DiscoveryRunNotFoundError as exc:\n+        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail=str(exc)) from exc\n+    return summary.to_dict()\n+\n+\n @router.get(\"/runs/{run_id}/candidates\")\n def read_run_candidates(run_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)) -> dict[str, object]:\n     try:"}, {"filename": "app/discovery/providers/__init__.py", "patch": "@@ -0,0 +1 @@\n+\"\"\"Discovery provider adapters.\"\"\""}, {"filename": "app/discovery/providers/base.py", "patch": "@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@\n+\"\"\"Provider interface for discovery candidate sources.\n+\n+Provider adapters transform stored query-plan entries into candidate-like result\n+objects. Implementations must not make legal claims about applicability or\n+violations.\n+\"\"\"\n+\n+from __future__ import annotations\n+\n+from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass\n+from typing import Any, Protocol\n+\n+\n+@dataclass(frozen=True)\n+class ProviderResult:\n+    source: str\n+    source_reference: str | None = None\n+    company_name: str | None = None\n+    domain: str | None = None\n+    city: str | None = None\n+    postal_code: str | None = None\n+    address: str | None = None\n+    phone: str | None = None\n+    category: str | None = None\n+    raw_data: dict[str, Any] | None = None\n+    confidence_score: float | None = None\n+\n+    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n+        data = asdict(self)\n+        data[\"raw_data\"] = self.raw_data or {}\n+        return data\n+\n+\n+class DiscoveryProvider(Protocol):\n+    source: str\n+\n+    def search(self, query_plan: list[dict[str, str]]) -> list[ProviderResult]:\n+        \"\"\"Return provider results for query-plan entries.\"\"\""}, {"filename": "app/discovery/providers/mock_provider.py", "patch": "@@ -0,0 +1,64 @@\n+\"\"\"Deterministic mock provider for discovery candidate persistence tests.\n+\n+This adapter creates obvious test data only. It does not call external services,\n+does not scrape, and does not represent real businesses.\n+\"\"\"\n+\n+from __future__ import annotations\n+\n+import re\n+\n+from app.discovery.providers.base import ProviderResult\n+\n+MAX_MOCK_RESULTS = 10\n+_SLUG_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"[^a-z0-9]+\")\n+\n+\n+def _title_keyword(keyword: str) -> str:\n+    return \" \".join(part.capitalize() for part in keyword.split())\n+\n+\n+def _slug(value: str) -> str:\n+    normalized = value.casefold().replace(\"\u00e4\", \"ae\").replace(\"\u00f6\", \"oe\").replace(\"\u00fc\", \"ue\").replace(\"\u00df\", \"ss\")\n+    slug = _SLUG_PATTERN.sub(\"-\", normalized).strip(\"-\")\n+    return slug or \"unknown\"\n+\n+\n+class MockDiscoveryProvider:\n+    source = \"mock\"\n+\n+    def __init__(self, max_results: int = MAX_MOCK_RESULTS) -> None:\n+        self.max_results = max_results\n+\n+    def search(self, query_plan: list[dict[str, str]]) -> list[ProviderResult]:\n+        results: list[ProviderResult] = []\n+        for entry in query_plan[: self.max_results]:\n+            city = entry.get(\"city\") or \"Unknown City\"\n+            postal_code = entry.get(\"postal_code\") or \"00000\"\n+            keyword = entry.get(\"keyword\") or \"unknown\"\n+            keyword_group_id = entry.get(\"keyword_group_id\") or \"unknown\"\n+            query_text = entry.get(\"query_text\") or \"\"\n+            reference = f\"mock:{_slug(city)}:{postal_code}:{_slug(keyword_group_id)}:{_slug(keyword)}\"\n+\n+            results.append(\n+                ProviderResult(\n+                    source=self.source,\n+                    source_reference=reference,\n+                    company_name=f\"Mock Candidate {city} {postal_code} {_title_keyword(keyword)}\",\n+                    domain=None,\n+                    city=city,\n+                    postal_code=postal_code,\n+                    address=None,\n+                    phone=None,\n+                    category=keyword_group_id,\n+                    raw_data={\n+                        \"provider\": self.source,\n+                        \"mock\": True,\n+                        \"query_text\": query_text,\n+                        \"keyword_group_id\": keyword_group_id,\n+                        \"keyword\": keyword,\n+                    },\n+                    confidence_score=0.5,\n+                )\n+            )\n+        return results"}, {"filename": "app/services/provider_execution_service.py", "patch": "@@ -0,0 +1,95 @@\n+from __future__ import annotations\n+\n+from dataclasses import dataclass\n+\n+from sqlalchemy.orm import Session\n+\n+from app.discovery.providers.base import ProviderResult\n+from app.discovery.providers.mock_provider import MockDiscoveryProvider\n+from app.models.lead_candidate import LeadCandidate\n+from app.services.discovery_service import DiscoveryRunNotFoundError, get_discovery_run\n+\n+\n+@dataclass(frozen=True)\n+class ProviderExecutionSummary:\n+    discovery_run_id: str\n+    provider: str\n+    candidates_created: int\n+    candidates_total: int\n+\n+    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, str | int]:\n+        return {\n+            \"discovery_run_id\": self.discovery_run_id,\n+            \"provider\": self.provider,\n+            \"candidates_created\": self.candidates_created,\n+            \"candidates_total\": self.candidates_total,\n+        }\n+\n+\n+def execute_mock_provider(db: Session, discovery_run_id: str) -> ProviderExecutionSummary:\n+    discovery_run = get_discovery_run(db, discovery_run_id)\n+    provider = MockDiscoveryProvider()\n+    results = provider.search(discovery_run.query_plan)\n+\n+    candidates_created = 0\n+    for result in results:\n+        if result.source_reference and _candidate_exists(db, discovery_run_id, result):\n+            continue\n+\n+        db.add(_candidate_from_provider_result(discovery_run_id, result))\n+        candidates_created += 1\n+\n+    db.commit()\n+    candidates_total = (\n+        db.query(LeadCandidate)\n+        .filter(\n+            LeadCandidate.discovery_run_id == discovery_run_id,\n+            LeadCandidate.source == provider.source,\n+        )\n+        .count()\n+    )\n+    return ProviderExecutionSummary(\n+        discovery_run_id=discovery_run_id,\n+        provider=provider.source,\n+        candidates_created=candidates_created,\n+        candidates_total=candidates_total,\n+    )\n+\n+\n+def _candidate_exists(db: Session, discovery_run_id: str, result: ProviderResult) -> bool:\n+    return (\n+        db.query(LeadCandidate.id)\n+        .filter(\n+            LeadCandidate.discovery_run_id == discovery_run_id,\n+            LeadCandidate.source == result.source,\n+            LeadCandidate.source_reference == result.source_reference,\n+        )\n+        .first()\n+        is not None\n+    )\n+\n+\n+def _candidate_from_provider_result(\n+    discovery_run_id: str, result: ProviderResult\n+) -> LeadCandidate:\n+    return LeadCandidate(\n+        discovery_run_id=discovery_run_id,\n+        source=result.source,\n+        source_reference=result.source_reference,\n+        company_name=result.company_name,\n+        domain=result.domain,\n+        city=result.city,\n+        postal_code=result.postal_code,\n+        address=result.address,\n+        phone=result.phone,\n+        category=result.category,\n+        raw_data=result.raw_data or {},\n+        confidence_score=result.confidence_score,\n+    )\n+\n+\n+__all__ = [\n+    \"DiscoveryRunNotFoundError\",\n+    \"ProviderExecutionSummary\",\n+    \"execute_mock_provider\",\n+]"}, {"filename": "tests/test_discovery_api.py", "patch": "@@ -115,6 +115,37 @@ def test_discovery_run_candidates_endpoint_returns_empty_list(db_session: Sessio\n     assert response.json() == {\"discovery_run_id\": run_id, \"candidates\": []}\n \n \n+def test_mock_provider_endpoint_persists_candidates(db_session: Session) -> None:\n+    client = make_client(db_session)\n+    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/L\u00fcbeck\")\n+    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n+\n+    provider_response = client.post(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/mock\")\n+    candidates_response = client.get(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/candidates\")\n+\n+    assert provider_response.status_code == 200\n+    assert provider_response.json() == {\n+        \"discovery_run_id\": run_id,\n+        \"provider\": \"mock\",\n+        \"candidates_created\": 10,\n+        \"candidates_total\": 10,\n+    }\n+    candidates = candidates_response.json()[\"candidates\"]\n+    assert len(candidates) == 10\n+    assert candidates[0][\"source\"] == \"mock\"\n+    assert candidates[0][\"company_name\"].startswith(\"Mock Candidate L\u00fcbeck\")\n+    assert candidates[0][\"raw_data\"][\"mock\"] is True\n+\n+\n+def test_mock_provider_endpoint_unknown_run_returns_clear_error(db_session: Session) -> None:\n+    client = make_client(db_session)\n+\n+    response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/missing/providers/mock\")\n+\n+    assert response.status_code == 404\n+    assert \"Discovery run not found: missing\" in response.json()[\"detail\"]\n+\n+\n def test_create_discovery_run_unknown_city_returns_clear_error(db_session: Session) -> None:\n     client = make_client(db_session)\n "}, {"filename": "tests/test_mock_provider.py", "patch": "@@ -0,0 +1,53 @@\n+import socket\n+\n+from app.discovery.providers.mock_provider import MockDiscoveryProvider\n+\n+\n+QUERY_PLAN = [\n+    {\n+        \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n+        \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n+        \"keyword_group_id\": \"ecommerce\",\n+        \"keyword\": \"online shop\",\n+        \"query_text\": \"L\u00fcbeck 23552 online shop\",\n+    },\n+    {\n+        \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n+        \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n+        \"keyword_group_id\": \"banking\",\n+        \"keyword\": \"bank\",\n+        \"query_text\": \"L\u00fcbeck 23552 bank\",\n+    },\n+]\n+\n+\n+def test_mock_provider_returns_deterministic_results() -> None:\n+    provider = MockDiscoveryProvider(max_results=2)\n+\n+    first = provider.search(QUERY_PLAN)\n+    second = provider.search(QUERY_PLAN)\n+\n+    assert first == second\n+    assert len(first) == 2\n+    assert first[0].source == \"mock\"\n+    assert first[0].source_reference == \"mock:luebeck:23552:ecommerce:online-shop\"\n+    assert first[0].company_name == \"Mock Candidate L\u00fcbeck 23552 Online Shop\"\n+    assert first[0].domain is None\n+    assert first[0].raw_data == {\n+        \"provider\": \"mock\",\n+        \"mock\": True,\n+        \"query_text\": \"L\u00fcbeck 23552 online shop\",\n+        \"keyword_group_id\": \"ecommerce\",\n+        \"keyword\": \"online shop\",\n+    }\n+\n+\n+def test_mock_provider_makes_no_external_network_calls(monkeypatch) -> None:\n+    def fail_network(*args, **kwargs):\n+        raise AssertionError(\"network access is not allowed in mock provider\")\n+\n+    monkeypatch.setattr(socket, \"create_connection\", fail_network)\n+\n+    results = MockDiscoveryProvider(max_results=1).search(QUERY_PLAN)\n+\n+    assert len(results) == 1"}, {"filename": "tests/test_provider_execution_service.py", "patch": "@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@\n+import pytest\n+from sqlalchemy import create_engine\n+from sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\n+\n+from app.db.base import Base\n+from app.models import DiscoveryRun, LeadCandidate  # noqa: F401\n+from app.services.discovery_service import DiscoveryRunNotFoundError, create_discovery_run\n+from app.services.provider_execution_service import execute_mock_provider\n+\n+\n+@pytest.fixture()\n+def db_session() -> Session:\n+    engine = create_engine(\"sqlite:///:memory:\")\n+    TestingSessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine)\n+    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n+\n+    session = TestingSessionLocal()\n+    try:\n+        yield session\n+    finally:\n+        session.close()\n+        Base.metadata.drop_all(bind=engine)\n+\n+\n+def test_provider_execution_persists_lead_candidates(db_session: Session) -> None:\n+    discovery_run = create_discovery_run(db_session, \"L\u00fcbeck\")\n+\n+    summary = execute_mock_provider(db_session, discovery_run.id)\n+\n+    assert summary.discovery_run_id == discovery_run.id\n+    assert summary.provider == \"mock\"\n+    assert summary.candidates_created == 10\n+    assert summary.candidates_total == 10\n+\n+    candidates = db_session.query(LeadCandidate).all()\n+    assert len(candidates) == 10\n+    assert candidates[0].source == \"mock\"\n+    assert candidates[0].company_name.startswith(\"Mock Candidate L\u00fcbeck\")\n+    assert candidates[0].raw_data[\"mock\"] is True\n+\n+\n+def test_duplicate_provider_execution_does_not_duplicate_candidates(db_session: Session) -> None:\n+    discovery_run = create_discovery_run(db_session, \"L\u00fcbeck\")\n+\n+    first = execute_mock_provider(db_session, discovery_run.id)\n+    second = execute_mock_provider(db_session, discovery_run.id)\n+\n+    assert first.candidates_created == 10\n+    assert first.candidates_total == 10\n+    assert second.candidates_created == 0\n+    assert second.candidates_total == 10\n+    assert db_session.query(LeadCandidate).count() == 10\n+\n+\n+def test_provider_execution_unknown_run_fails_clearly(db_session: Session) -> None:\n+    with pytest.raises(DiscoveryRunNotFoundError, match=\"Discovery run not found: missing\"):\n+        execute_mock_provider(db_session, \"missing\")"}], "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG", "comments": null, "author": {"login": str\n+    provider: str\n+    candidates_created: int\n+    candidates_total: int\n+\n+    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, str | int]:\n+        return {\n+            \"discovery_run_id\":
