> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG",
>   "branch": "codex/promotion-gate-foundation",
>   "path": "tests/test_promotion_service.py",
>   "message": "Test promotion gate service",
>   "content": "import json\nimport socket\n\nimport pytest\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\n\nfrom app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.models import (  # noqa: F401\n    CompanyEnrichment,\n    CompanyQualification,\n    CompanyQualificationStatus,\n    DiscoveryRun,\n    DiscoveryRunStatus,\n    LeadCandidate,\n    PromotionDecision,\n)\nfrom app.services.company_qualification_service import LeadCandidateNotFoundError\nfrom app.services.promotion_service import evaluate_candidate_for_promotion\n\n\n@pytest.fixture()\ndef db_session() -> Session:\n    engine = create_engine(\"sqlite:///:memory:\")\n    TestingSessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine)\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n\n    session = TestingSessionLocal()\n    try:\n        yield session\n    finally:\n        session.close()\n        Base.metadata.drop_all(bind=engine)\n\n\ndef _create_run(db_session: Session) -> DiscoveryRun:\n    discovery_run = DiscoveryRun(\n        city=\"Lübeck\",\n        normalized_city=\"luebeck\",\n        status=DiscoveryRunStatus.done,\n        keyword_groups=[],\n        postal_codes=[\"23552\"],\n        query_plan=[],\n    )\n    db_session.add(discovery_run)\n    db_session.commit()\n    db_session.refresh(discovery_run)\n    return discovery_run\n\n\ndef _create_candidate(\n    db_session: Session,\n    *,\n    source: str = \"google_places\",\n    domain: str | None = \"https://seed.example\",\n    category: str | None = \"store\",\n    raw_data: dict | None = None,\n) -> LeadCandidate:\n    discovery_run = _create_run(db_session)\n    candidate = LeadCandidate(\n        discovery_run_id=discovery_run.id,\n        source=source,\n        source_reference=\"places/seed\",\n        company_name=\"Seed Candidate GmbH\",\n        domain=domain,\n        city=\"Lübeck\",\n        postal_code=\"23552\",\n        category=category,\n        raw_data=raw_data or {},\n    )\n    db_session.add(candidate)\n    db_session.commit()\n    db_session.refresh(candidate)\n    return candidate\n\n\ndef _add_qualification(\n    db_session: Session,\n    candidate: LeadCandidate,\n    *,\n    status: CompanyQualificationStatus,\n    is_microenterprise: bool | None = None,\n    website_signal: bool | None = None,\n    b2c_signal: bool | None = None,\n    bfsg_category_signal: str | None = None,\n) -> CompanyQualification:\n    qualification = CompanyQualification(\n        lead_candidate_id=candidate.id,\n        status=status,\n        company_name=candidate.company_name,\n        is_microenterprise=is_microenterprise,\n        b2c_signal=b2c_signal,\n        bfsg_category_signal=bfsg_category_signal,\n        website_signal=website_signal,\n        confidence_score=0.6,\n        evidence={\"test\": True},\n    )\n    db_session.add(qualification)\n    db_session.commit()\n    db_session.refresh(qualification)\n    return qualification\n\n\ndef test_unknown_candidate_fails_clearly(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    with pytest.raises(LeadCandidateNotFoundError, match=\"Lead candidate not found: missing\"):\n        evaluate_candidate_for_promotion(db_session, \"missing\")\n\n\ndef test_mock_candidate_results_in_needs_review(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    candidate = _create_candidate(db_session, source=\"mock\", raw_data={\"mock\": True})\n\n    decision = evaluate_candidate_for_promotion(db_session, candidate.id)\n\n    assert decision.status.value == \"needs_review\"\n    assert decision.reason_code == \"mock_or_test_data\"\n    assert decision.confidence_score == 0.2\n    assert decision.reasons[\"no_legal_conclusion\"] is True\n\n\ndef test_missing_qualification_results_in_missing_company_data(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    candidate = _create_candidate(db_session)\n\n    decision = evaluate_candidate_for_promotion(db_session, candidate.id)\n\n    assert decision.status.value == \"needs_review\"\n    assert decision.reason_code == \"missing_company_data\"\n    assert decision.company_qualification_id is None\n\n\ndef test_likely_not_applicable_qualification_results_in_rejected(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    candidate = _create_candidate(db_session)\n    qualification = _add_qualification(\n        db_session,\n        candidate,\n        status=CompanyQualificationStatus.likely_not_applicable,\n        is_microenterprise=True,\n    )\n\n    decision = evaluate_candidate_for_promotion(db_session, candidate.id)\n\n    assert decision.status.value == \"rejected\"\n    assert decision.reason_code == \"likely_microenterprise\"\n    assert decision.company_qualification_id == qualification.id\n\n\ndef test_possible_candidate_with_domain_is_ready_for_website_probe(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    candidate = _create_candidate(db_session, domain=\"https://seed.example\")\n    _add_qualification(\n        db_session,\n        candidate,\n        status=CompanyQualificationStatus.possible_bfsg_candidate,\n        is_microenterprise=False,\n        website_signal=True,\n        b2c_signal=True,\n        bfsg_category_signal=\"store\",\n    )\n\n    decision = evaluate_candidate_for_promotion(db_session, candidate.id)\n\n    assert decision.status.value == \"ready_for_website_probe\"\n    assert decision.reason_code == \"possible_bfsg_candidate\"\n    assert decision.reasons[\"has_domain\"] is True\n\n\ndef test_possible_candidate_without_website_needs_review(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    candidate = _create_candidate(db_session, domain=None)\n    _add_qualification(\n        db_session,\n        candidate,\n        status=CompanyQualificationStatus.possible_bfsg_candidate,\n        is_microenterprise=False,\n        website_signal=False,\n        b2c_signal=True,\n        bfsg_category_signal=\"store\",\n    )\n\n    decision = evaluate_candidate_for_promotion(db_session, candidate.id)\n\n    assert decision.status.value == \"needs_review\"\n    assert decision.reason_code == \"missing_website\"\n    assert decision.reasons[\"website_signal\"] is False\n\n\ndef test_reason_fields_do_not_contain_forbidden_legal_claims(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    candidate = _create_candidate(db_session, source=\"mock\", raw_data={\"mock\": True})\n\n    decision = evaluate_candidate_for_promotion(db_session, candidate.id)\n    reason_text = json.dumps(\n        {\"reason_code\": decision.reason_code, \"reasons\": decision.reasons},\n        sort_keys=True,\n    ).casefold()\n\n    assert \"legally obligated\" not in reason_text\n    assert \"violation\" not in reason_text\n    assert \"violates\" not in reason_text\n\n\ndef test_promotion_gate_makes_no_external_network_calls(monkeypatch, db_session: Session) -> None:\n    def fail_network(*args, **kwargs):\n        raise AssertionError(\"network access is not allowed in promotion gate\")\n\n    monkeypatch.setattr(socket, \"create_connection\", fail_network)\n    candidate = _create_candidate(db_session)\n\n    decision = evaluate_candidate_for_promotion(db_session, candidate.id)\n\n    assert decision.status.value == \"needs_review\"\n"

>   "content": "import pytest\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\nfrom fastapi.testclient import TestClient\nfrom sqlalchemy import create_engine\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import Session, sessionmaker\nfrom sqlalchemy.pool import StaticPool\n\nfrom app.api.discovery import router as discovery_router\nfrom app.db.base import Base\nfrom app.db.session import get_db\nfrom app.models import (  # noqa: F401\n    CompanyEnrichment,\n    CompanyQualification,\n    DiscoveryRun,\n    LeadCandidate,\n    PromotionDecision,\n)\n\n\n@pytest.fixture()\ndef db_session() -> Session:\n    engine = create_engine(\n        \"sqlite://\",\n        connect_args={\"check_same_thread\": False},\n        poolclass=StaticPool,\n    )\n    TestingSessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine)\n    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)\n\n    session = TestingSessionLocal()\n    try:\n        yield session\n    finally:\n        session.close()\n        Base.metadata.drop_all(bind=engine)\n\n\ndef make_client(db_session: Session | None = None) -> TestClient:\n    app = FastAPI()\n    app.include_router(discovery_router)\n    if db_session is not None:\n        app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = lambda: db_session\n    return TestClient(app)\n\n\ndef test_keywords_endpoint_returns_structured_groups() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/keywords\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    groups = response.json()[\"groups\"]\n    assert groups\n    assert {\"group_id\", \"label\", \"keywords\", \"bfsg_relevance_reason\", \"enabled\"}.issubset(\n        groups[0]\n    )\n    assert \"ecommerce\" in {group[\"group_id\"] for group in groups}\n\n\ndef test_places_endpoint_returns_luebeck_matches() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/Lübeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    payload = response.json()\n    assert payload[\"city\"] == \"Lübeck\"\n    assert payload[\"matches\"]\n    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in payload[\"matches\"]} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n    assert any(match[\"postal_code\"] == \"23552\" for match in payload[\"matches\"])\n\n\ndef test_places_endpoint_accepts_luebeck_transliteration() -> None:\n    client = make_client()\n\n    response = client.get(\"/discovery/places/Luebeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert {match[\"city\"] for match in response.json()[\"matches\"]} == {\"Lübeck\"}\n\n\ndef test_create_discovery_run_endpoint_returns_done_and_query_count(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n\n    response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Lübeck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    payload = response.json()\n    assert payload[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n    assert payload[\"city\"] == \"Lübeck\"\n    assert payload[\"status\"] == \"done\"\n    assert payload[\"postal_codes_count\"] > 0\n    assert payload[\"query_count\"] > 0\n\n\ndef test_get_discovery_run_endpoint_returns_stored_query_plan(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Lübeck\")\n    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n\n    response = client.get(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    payload = response.json()\n    assert payload[\"id\"] == run_id\n    assert payload[\"status\"] == \"done\"\n    assert payload[\"postal_codes\"]\n    assert payload[\"keyword_groups\"]\n    assert payload[\"query_plan\"]\n    assert payload[\"error_message\"] is None\n\n\ndef test_discovery_run_candidates_endpoint_returns_empty_list(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Lübeck\")\n    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n\n    response = client.get(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/candidates\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 200\n    assert response.json() == {\"discovery_run_id\": run_id, \"candidates\": []}\n\n\ndef test_mock_provider_endpoint_persists_candidates(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Lübeck\")\n    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n\n    provider_response = client.post(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/mock\")\n    candidates_response = client.get(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/candidates\")\n\n    assert provider_response.status_code == 200\n    assert provider_response.json() == {\n        \"discovery_run_id\": run_id,\n        \"provider\": \"mock\",\n        \"candidates_created\": 10,\n        \"candidates_total\": 10,\n    }\n    candidates = candidates_response.json()[\"candidates\"]\n    assert len(candidates) == 10\n    assert candidates[0][\"source\"] == \"mock\"\n    assert candidates[0][\"company_name\"].startswith(\"Mock Candidate Lübeck\")\n    assert candidates[0][\"raw_data\"][\"mock\"] is True\n\n\ndef test_google_places_endpoint_disabled_returns_clear_error(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Lübeck\")\n    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n\n    response = client.post(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/google-places\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 503\n    assert response.json()[\"detail\"] == \"Google Places API provider is disabled\"\n\n\ndef test_candidate_enrichment_mock_endpoint_persists_and_reads_latest(\n    db_session: Session,\n) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Lübeck\")\n    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n    client.post(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/mock\")\n    candidate_id = client.get(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/candidates\").json()[\"candidates\"][0][\"id\"]\n\n    enrichment_response = client.post(\n        f\"/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/enrichment/mock\"\n    )\n    read_response = client.get(f\"/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/enrichment\")\n\n    assert enrichment_response.status_code == 200\n    enrichment_summary = enrichment_response.json()\n    assert enrichment_summary[\"candidate_id\"] == candidate_id\n    assert enrichment_summary[\"source\"] == \"mock_company_data\"\n    assert enrichment_summary[\"confidence_score\"] == 0.2\n\n    assert read_response.status_code == 200\n    enrichment = read_response.json()\n    assert enrichment[\"id\"] == enrichment_summary[\"enrichment_id\"]\n    assert enrichment[\"candidate_id\"] == candidate_id\n    assert enrichment[\"raw_data\"][\"mock\"] is True\n    assert enrichment[\"raw_data\"][\"profile\"] == \"missing\"\n\n\ndef test_candidate_enrichment_unknown_candidate_returns_clear_error(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n\n    response = client.post(\"/discovery/candidates/missing/enrichment/mock\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 404\n    assert \"Lead candidate not found: missing\" in response.json()[\"detail\"]\n\n\ndef test_candidate_qualification_precheck_for_mock_candidate(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Lübeck\")\n    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n    client.post(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/mock\")\n    candidates = client.get(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/candidates\").json()[\"candidates\"]\n    candidate_id = candidates[0][\"id\"]\n\n    precheck_response = client.post(\n        f\"/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification/precheck\"\n    )\n    read_response = client.get(f\"/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification\")\n\n    assert precheck_response.status_code == 200\n    precheck = precheck_response.json()\n    assert precheck[\"candidate_id\"] == candidate_id\n    assert precheck[\"status\"] == \"needs_human_review\"\n    assert precheck[\"is_microenterprise\"] is None\n    assert precheck[\"confidence_score\"] == 0.1\n\n    assert read_response.status_code == 200\n    qualification = read_response.json()\n    assert qualification[\"candidate_id\"] == candidate_id\n    assert qualification[\"status\"] == \"needs_human_review\"\n    assert qualification[\"evidence\"][\"is_mock_or_test_data\"] is True\n    assert qualification[\"evidence\"][\"requires_company_enrichment\"] is True\n\n\ndef test_candidate_promotion_endpoint_evaluates_and_reads_latest(\n    db_session: Session,\n) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n    create_response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Lübeck\")\n    run_id = create_response.json()[\"discovery_run_id\"]\n    client.post(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/providers/mock\")\n    candidate_id = client.get(f\"/discovery/runs/{run_id}/candidates\").json()[\"candidates\"][0][\"id\"]\n    client.post(f\"/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/enrichment/mock\")\n    client.post(f\"/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification/precheck\")\n\n    evaluate_response = client.post(\n        f\"/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/promotion/evaluate\"\n    )\n    read_response = client.get(f\"/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/promotion\")\n\n    assert evaluate_response.status_code == 200\n    evaluation = evaluate_response.json()\n    assert evaluation[\"candidate_id\"] == candidate_id\n    assert evaluation[\"status\"] == \"needs_review\"\n    assert evaluation[\"reason_code\"] == \"mock_or_test_data\"\n    assert evaluation[\"confidence_score\"] == 0.2\n\n    assert read_response.status_code == 200\n    promotion = read_response.json()\n    assert promotion[\"id\"] == evaluation[\"promotion_decision_id\"]\n    assert promotion[\"candidate_id\"] == candidate_id\n    assert promotion[\"status\"] == \"needs_review\"\n    assert promotion[\"reasons\"][\"no_legal_conclusion\"] is True\n\n\ndef test_promotion_unknown_candidate_returns_clear_error(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n\n client.post(\"/discovery/candidates/missing/qualification/precheck\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 404\n    assert \"Lead candidate not found: missing\" in response.json()[\"detail\"]\n\n\ndef test_mock_provider_endpoint_unknown_run_returns_clear_error(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n\n    response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/missing/providers/mock\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 404\n    assert \"Discovery run not found: missing\" in response.json()[\"detail\"]\n\n\ndef test_create_discovery_run_unknown_city_returns_clear_error(db_session: Session) -> None:\n    client = make_client(db_session)\n\n    response = client.post(\"/discovery/runs/Atlantis\")\n\n    assert response.status_code == 404\n    assert \"No places found for city: Atlantis\" in response.json()[\"detail\"]\n"

> ````text
> {"content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nNorthAccessBFSG is a FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\nThe current scope includes backend infrastructure, asynchronous scans, raw accessibility findings, compliance mapping basics, evidence bundle storage, and a minimal Lead Discovery foundation for city, keyword, provider seed, company enrichment, and company precheck workflows.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance.py\n    discovery.py\n    evidence.py\n    health.py\n    scans.py\n  compliance/\n    __init__.py\n    rule_engine.py\n    rules/\n      __init__.py\n      wcag_en_bfsg_map.json\n  core/\n    __init__.py\n    browser_config.py\n    celery.py\n    config.py\n  db/\n    __init__.py\n    base.py\n    init_db.py\n    session.py\n  discovery/\n    __init__.py\n    keywords.py\n    place_resolver.py\n    providers/\n      __init__.py\n      base.py\n      google_places_provider.py\n      mock_provider.py\n    query_planner.py\n  enrichment/\n    __init__.py\n    providers/\n      __init__.py\n      base.py\n      mock_company_provider.py\n  evidence/\n    __init__.py\n    hashing.py\n    storage.py\n    storage_backend.py\n  models/\n    company_enrichment.py\n    company_qualification.py\n    discovery_run.py\n    lead_candidate.py\n  services/\n    company_enrichment_service.py\n    company_qualification_service.py\n    discovery_service.py\n    provider_execution_service.py\n  workers/\ndata/\n  orte_deutschland.csv\n04_filter_quality.py\n05_run_pipeline.py\n13_city_guard.py\n14_evidence_gate.py\n15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nrequirements.txt\n.env.example\n```\n\n## Run\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose up --build\n```\n\nThen open:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/health\n```\n\nExpected response:\n\n```json\n{\"status\":\"ok\"}\n```\n\nThe API waits for PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the database tables on startup.\n\n## Lead Discovery Foundation\n\nThe canonical raw city data file is:\n\n```text\ndata/orte_deutschland.csv\n```\n\nThe file is semicolon-separated and must contain these columns:\n\n```text\nplz;stadt\n```\n\nThe resolver treats this file as raw input. The `stadt` column may contain real city names as well as organizations or special postal recipients, so city resolution uses exact normalized city-name matching only. It does not use substring matching.\n\nSupported normalization includes case-insensitive matching and German umlaut handling:\n\n```text\nL\u00fcbeck -> Luebeck -> lubeck\n```\n\nDiscovery endpoints:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/L\u00fcbeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/lubeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords\n```\n\nExample place response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n  \"matches\": [\n    {\n      \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n      \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n      \"country\": \"DE\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n## Discovery Query Planner\n\nA discovery run is a dry-run planning record. It resolves a city to postal codes, combines those postal codes with enabled keyword groups, and persists a deterministic query plan for a later external provider integration.\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/L\u00fcbeck\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"discovery_run_id\": \"<run-id>\",\n  \"city\": \"L\u00fcbeck\",\n  \"status\": \"done\",\n  \"postal_codes_count\": 12,\n  \"query_count\": 336\n}\n```\n\nFetch a stored run and its candidate list:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates\n```\n\n## Discovery Providers\n\nProvider adapters execute a stored query plan and persist `LeadCandidate` rows. These rows are seed candidates only. They are not confirmed BFSG applicability, accessibility findings, legal obligations, violations, or legal conclusions.\n\n### Mock Provider\n\nThe mock provider creates bounded, deterministic test candidates with obvious test names, for example `Mock Candidate L\u00fcbeck 23552 Online Shop`.\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/providers/mock\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates\n```\n\nExample provider response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"discovery_run_id\": \"<run-id>\",\n  \"provider\": \"mock\",\n  \"candidates_created\": 10,\n  \"candidates_total\": 10\n}\n```\n\n### Google Places API Provider\n\nThe Google Places API provider is disabled by default. Tests do not call Google, and normal local runs do not require an API key.\n\nRequired environment settings for live use:\n\n```text\nGOOGLE_PLACES_ENABLED=false\nGOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY=\nGOOGLE_PLACES_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=10\nGOOGLE_PLACES_MAX_RESULTS_PER_QUERY=5\n```\n\nEndpoint:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/providers/google-places\n```\n\nIf disabled or missing configuration, the endpoint returns a clear error and does not make an external call. If enabled with a key, Places API responses are mapped into raw `LeadCandidate` seed rows with `source=\"google_places\"`.\n\nGoogle Places API results are only seed candidates. Northdata-style or comparable company enrichment is required before any candidate can be treated as qualified for downstream audit consideration.\n\n## Company Enrichment Foundation\n\n`CompanyEnrichment` stores company-data signals for a `LeadCandidate`: legal form, registry ID, source URL, employee count, annual revenue, balance sheet total, raw source payload, and confidence. These records are evidence/signal data only. They do not make legal conclusions or final BFSG applicability decisions.\n\nThe mock enrichment provider creates deterministic test data only. It does not call Northdata, scrape company registers, scrape websites, or claim real company data.\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/enrichment/mock\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/enrichment\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"candidate_id\": \"<candidate-id>\",\n  \"enrichment_id\": \"<enrichment-id>\",\n  \"source\": \"mock_company_data\",\n  \"confidence_score\": 0.5\n}\n```\n\nA future Northdata or company-register integration should live behind the enrichment provider abstraction. Live company-data calls are not implemented in this foundation step.\n\n## Company Qualification Precheck\n\n`CompanyQualification` stores precheck signals for a `LeadCandidate`. It is a signal and review layer only. It does not make legal conclusions and it does not call Northdata or any external company-data provider yet.\n\nPrecheck endpoints:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification/precheck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification\n```\n\nThe precheck uses the latest `CompanyEnrichment` when available. Missing company data results in `needs_company_data` or `needs_human_review`. Microenterprise-like data can signal `likely_not_applicable` or review need. Non-microenterprise-like data can signal `possible_bfsg_candidate` only when category/B2C and website signals are present. These are review signals, not legal scoring or final applicability decisions.\n\nConfigured microenterprise signal thresholds:\n\n```text\nBFSG_MICROENTERPRISE_EMPLOYEE_THRESHOLD=10\nBFSG_MICROENTERPRISE_REVENUE_THRESHOLD_EUR=2000000\nBFSG_MICROENTERPRISE_BALANCE_THRESHOLD_EUR=2000000\n```\n\nThese thresholds are only used as precheck signals.\n\nThis discovery layer does not scrape websites, does not perform reporting, and does not run accessibility scans for seed candidates automatically.\n\n## Guarded Pipeline\n\nThe numbered guarded pipeline files validate that runtime dependencies are present and that the canonical city CSV is usable:\n\n```bash\npython -m py_compile \\\n  04_filter_quality.py \\\n  05_run_pipeline.py \\\n  13_city_guard.py \\\n  14_evidence_gate.py \\\n  15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n\npython 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n```\n\nThe guarded runner fails if `data/orte_deutschland.csv` is missing, unreadable, missing required columns, header-only, or contains zero usable rows.\n\n## Async Scan Flow\n\nCreate a lead directly in the database while a public lead API does not exist yet:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n```\n\nRun a scan for the lead:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nExpected immediate response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker launches Playwright headless Chromium, loads the lead domain, runs axe-core, captures evidence metadata, and persists raw findings.\n\nLifecycle:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nIf task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n\n## Compliance Mapping Flow\n\nRun deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nCompliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Browser Configuration\n\nThe worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n\n```text\nBROWSER_NAME=chromium\nBROWSER_HEADLESS=true\nBROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS=30000\nBROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS=10000\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH=1440\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=900\nBROWSER_RETRIES=1\nBROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL=networkidle\n```\n\nWebKit is supported by configuration but is not installed in the default image.\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "781f74a3270387494193f436b5445d265ae746b5", "display_url": "https://github.com/DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG/blob/codex/promotion-gate-foundation/README.md", "display_title": "README.md"}
> ````
>
> </details>
>
> Die Gate-Tests sind ergänzt. Ich aktualisiere noch die Dokumentation: wo der Promotion Gate sitzt, welche Endpunkte er hat, und dass `ready_for_website_probe` ausdrücklich nur ein interner nächster Schritt ist.
>

>   "content": "# NorthAccessBFSG\n\nNorthAccessBFSG is a FastAPI backend foundation for a BFSG/WCAG compliance audit platform.\n\nThe current scope includes backend infrastructure, asynchronous and internal promotion routing workflows.\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```text\napp/\n  __init__.py\n  main.py\n  api/\n    __init__.py\n    compliance.py\n    discovery.py\n    evidence.py\n PostgreSQL and Redis to become healthy, connects with SQLAlchemy, and auto-creates the database tables on startup.\n\n## Lead Discovery Foundation\n\nThe canonical raw city data file is:\n\n```text\ndata/orte_deutschland.csv\n```\n\nThe file is semicolon-separated and must contain these columns:\n\n```text\nplz;stadt\n```\n\nThe resolver treats this file as raw input. The `stadt` column may contain real city names as well as organizations or special postal recipients, so city resolution uses exact normalized city-name matching only. It does not use substring matching.\n\nSupported normalization includes case-insensitive matching and German umlaut handling:\n\n```text\nLübeck -> Luebeck -> lubeck\n```\n\nDiscovery endpoints:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Lübeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/Luebeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/places/lubeck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/keywords\n```\n\nExample place response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n  \"matches\": [\n    {\n      \"postal_code\": \"23552\",\n      \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n      \"country\": \"DE\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n## Discovery Query Planner\n\nA discovery run is a dry-run planning record. It resolves a city to postal codes, combines those postal codes with enabled keyword groups, and persists a deterministic query plan for a later external provider integration.\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/Lübeck\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"discovery_run_id\": \"<run-id>\",\n  \"city\": \"Lübeck\",\n  \"status\": \"done\",\n  \"postal_codes_count\": 12,\n  \"query_count\": 336\n}\n```\n\nFetch a stored run and its candidate list:\n\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates\n```\n\n## Discovery Providers\n\nProvider adapters execute a stored query plan and persist `LeadCandidate` rows. These rows are seed candidates only. They are not confirmed BFSG applicability, accessibility findings, legal obligations, violations, or legal conclusions.\n\n### Mock Provider\n\nThe mock provider creates bounded, deterministic test candidates with obvious test names, for example `Mock Candidate Lübeck 23552 Online Shop`.\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/providers/mock\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates\n```\n\nExample provider response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"discovery_run_id\": \"<run-id>\",\n  \"provider\": \"mock\",\n  \"candidates_created\": 10,\n  \"candidates_total\": 10\n}\n```\n\n### Google Places API Provider\n\nThe Google Places API provider is disabled by default. Tests do not call Google, and normal local runs do not require an API key.\n\nRequired environment settings for live use:\n\n```text\nGOOGLE_PLACES_ENABLED=false\nGOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY=\nGOOGLE_PLACES_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=10\nGOOGLE_PLACES_MAX_RESULTS_PER_QUERY=5\n```\n\nEndpoint:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/providers/google-places\n```\n\nIf disabled or missing configuration, the endpoint returns a clear error and does not make an external call. If enabled with a key, Places API responses are mapped into raw `LeadCandidate` seed rows with `source=\"google_places\"`.\n\nGoogle Places API results are only seed candidates. Northdata-style or comparable company enrichment is required before any candidate can be treated as qualified for downstream audit consideration.\n\n## Company Enrichment Foundation\n\n`CompanyEnrichment` stores company-data signals for a `LeadCandidate`: legal form, registry ID, source URL, employee count, annual revenue, balance sheet total, raw source payload, and confidence. These records are evidence/signal data only. They do not make legal conclusions or final BFSG applicability decisions.\n\nThe mock enrichment provider creates deterministic test data only. It does not call Northdata, scrape company registers, scrape websites, or claim real company data.\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/enrichment/mock\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/enrichment\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"candidate_id\": \"<candidate-id>\",\n  \"enrichment_id\": \"<enrichment-id>\",\n  \"source\": \"mock_company_data\",\n  \"confidence_score\": 0.5\n}\n```\n\nA future Northdata or company-register integration should live behind the enrichment provider abstraction. Live company-data calls are not implemented in this foundation step.\n\n## Company Qualification Precheck\n\n`CompanyQualification` stores precheck signals for a `LeadCandidate`. It is a signal and review layer only. It does not make legal conclusions and it does not call Northdata or any external company-data provider yet.\n\nPrecheck endpoints:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification/precheck\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification\n```\n\nThe precheck uses the latest `CompanyEnrichment` when available. Missing company data results in `needs_company_data` or `needs_human_review`. Microenterprise-like data can signal `likely_not_applicable` or review need. Non-microenterprise-like data can signal `possible_bfsg_candidate` only when category/B2C and website signals are present. These are review signals, not legal scoring or final applicability decisions.\n\nConfigured microenterprise signal thresholds:\n\n```text\nBFSG_MICROENTERPRISE_EMPLOYEE_THRESHOLD=10\nBFSG_MICROENTERPRISE_REVENUE_THRESHOLD_EUR=2000000\nBFSG_MICROENTERPRISE_BALANCE_THRESHOLD_EUR=2000000\n```\n\nThese thresholds are only used as precheck signals.\n\n## Promotion Gate\n\n`PromotionDecision` stores an internal routing decision for a `LeadCandidate` after company enrichment and qualification precheck. The possible routing statuses are:\n\n```text\nrejected\nneeds_review\nready_for_website_probe\n```\n\nPromotion endpoints:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/promotion/evaluate\ncurl http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/promotion\n```\n\nExample response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"candidate_id\": \"<candidate-id>\",\n  \"promotion_decision_id\": \"<decision-id>\",\n  \"status\": \"needs_review\",\n  \"reason_code\": \"mock_or_test_data\",\n  \"confidence_score\": 0.2\n}\n```\n\nThe Promotion Gate is only internal routing. `ready_for_website_probe` means the candidate can move to a future Website Probe stage. It does not mean a BFSG obligation, an accessibility issue, or any violation has been established. The Website Probe stage is not implemented in this step.\n\nThis discovery layer does not scrape websites, does not perform reporting, and does not run accessibility scans for seed candidates automatically.\n\n## Guarded Pipeline\n\nThe numbered guarded pipeline files validate that runtime dependencies are present and that the canonical city CSV is usable:\n\n```bash\npython -m py_compile \\\n  04_filter_quality.py \\\n  05_run_pipeline.py \\\n  13_city_guard.py \\\n  14_evidence_gate.py \\\n  15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n\npython 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py\n```\n\nThe guarded runner fails if `data/orte_deutschland.csv` is missing, unreadable, missing required columns, header-only, or contains zero usable rows.\n\n## Async Scan Flow\n\nCreate a lead directly in the database while a public lead API does not exist yet:\n\n```bash\ndocker-compose exec db psql -U northaccess -d northaccessbfsg -c \"INSERT INTO leads (id, domain, company_name) VALUES ('11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111', 'https://example.com', 'Example') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;\"\n```\n\nRun a scan for the lead:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/scans/run/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111\n```\n\nExpected immediate response:\n\n```json\n{\"scan_id\":\"<scan-id>\"}\n```\n\nThe API creates a `pending` scan and queues a Celery task without blocking. The worker launches Playwright headless Chromium, loads the lead domain, runs axe-core, captures evidence metadata, and persists raw findings.\n\nLifecycle:\n\n```text\npending -> running -> processing -> done\n```\n\nIf task execution fails, the worker marks the scan as `failed` and stores `error_message`.\n\n## Compliance Mapping Flow\n\nRun deterministic compliance mapping for a completed scan:\n\n```bash\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/compliance/run/{scan_id}\n```\n\nCompliance results are stored separately in `compliance_findings`. Raw `findings` rows are never overwritten. Re-running the endpoint updates the same `finding_id` + `mapping_version` compliance rows and removes stale rows for that scan/version.\n\n## Browser Configuration\n\nThe worker defaults to Chromium and also installs Firefox:\n\n```text\nBROWSER_NAME=chromium\nBROWSER_HEADLESS=true\nBROWSER_NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT_MS=30000\nBROWSER_ACTION_TIMEOUT_MS=10000\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_WIDTH=1440\nBROWSER_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT=900\nBROWSER_RETRIES=1\nBROWSER_WAIT_UNTIL=networkidle\n```\n\nWebKit is supported by configuration but is not installed in the default image.\n"

> # Role
> You are a senior backend/platform engineer working on:
>
> DopeChicka/NorthAccessBFSG
>
> # Project Naming Rule
> The project is called NorthAccessBFSG.
> Do NOT introduce bfsg-auditor-pro.
> Do NOT rename the project.
>
> # Base
> Use current main as base.
> Create a new branch.
>
> # Current State
> main is validated and contains:
> - Discovery place resolver
> - DiscoveryRun model
> - LeadCandidate model
> - deterministic query planner
> - provider abstraction
> - mock provider execution
> - disabled-by-default Google Places API seed provider
> - CompanyEnrichment model/service/endpoints
> - CompanyQualification model/service/endpoints
> - PromotionDecision model/service/endpoints
> - Promotion Gate statuses:
>   - rejected
>   - needs_review
>   - ready_for_website_probe
>
> Latest main validation:
> - pytest: 56 passed
> - guarded pipeline: passed
>
> # Goal
> Add Website Probe foundation.
>
> The Website Probe checks whether a promoted candidate has a reachable website/domain and basic BFSG-relevant web-flow signals.
>
> Important:
> This is NOT a full accessibility audit.
> This is NOT Playwright scanning.
> This is NOT legal scoring.
> This is NOT report generation.
> This is only lightweight website signal detection before deciding whether a full scan is worth scheduling.
>
> Future pipeline:
>
> LeadCandidate
> -> CompanyEnrichment
> -> CompanyQualification
> -> PromotionDecision
> -> WebsiteProbe
> -> Scan/Audit candidate
>
> This PR only adds WebsiteProbe model/service/endpoints and deterministic tests.
>
> # Required Work
>
> ## 1. Add WebsiteProbe model
>
> Create:
>
> app/models/website_probe.py
>
> Fields:
> - id
> - lead_candidate_id FK
> - promotion_decision_id nullable FK
> - url nullable
> - normalized_domain nullable
> - status:
>   - pending
>   - skipped
>   - reachable
>   - unreachable
>   - needs_review
> - http_status nullable
> - has_homepage_signal nullable
> - has_impressum_signal nullable
> - has_login_signal nullable
> - has_shop_signal nullable
> - has_booking_signal nullable
> - has_checkout_signal nullable
> - has_b2c_transaction_signal nullable
> - evidence JSON
> - confidence_score nullable
> - created_at
> - updated_at
>
> Important:
> - Signals only.
> - No legal conclusion.
> - No accessibility result.
> - No BFSG violation.
>
> Wire into:
> - app/models/__init__.py
> - app/db/init_db.py
>
> ## 2. Add website probe provider abstraction
>
> Create:
>
> app/website_probe/providers/base.py
>
> Define:
> - WebsiteProbeProvider protocol or abstract base class
> - WebsiteProbeResult data object
>
> Fields:
> - url
> - normalized_domain
> - status
> - http_status nullable
> - has_homepage_signal nullable
> - has_impressum_signal nullable
> - has_login_signal nullable
> - has_shop_signal nullable
> - has_booking_signal nullable
> - has_checkout_signal nullable
> - has_b2c_transaction_signal nullable
> - evidence dict
> - confidence_score nullable
>
> ## 3. Add mock website probe provider
>
> Create:
>
> app/website_probe/providers/mock_provider.py
>
> It should:
> - accept LeadCandidate
> - return deterministic probe signals
> - if candidate has no domain:
>   - status = skipped or needs_review
>   - evidence explains "missing_domain"
> - if candidate has domain:
>   - status = reachable
>   - http_status = 200
>   - set signal booleans based on category/raw_data:
>     - ecommerce -> shop + checkout + b2c transaction likely
>     - booking -> booking + b2c transaction likely
>     - banking -> login likely
>     - transport -> booking/ticket signal likely
> - clearly mark raw evidence as mock/test data
> - no external calls
>
> ## 4. Add website probe service
>
> Create:
>
> app/services/website_probe_service.py
>
> Expose:
> - run_mock_website_probe(db, candidate_id: str) -> WebsiteProbe
> - get_latest_website_probe(db, candidate_id: str)
>
> Logic:
> - fail clearly if candidate not found
> - if latest PromotionDecision exists and status is rejected:
>   - create skipped WebsiteProbe with reason rejected_by_promotion_gate
> - if promotion status is needs_review:
>   - still allow mock probe, but evidence should include promotion_status
> - if candidate has no domain:
>   - status skipped or needs_review with reason missing_domain
> - persist WebsiteProbe
> - idempotent where practical
> - no legal conclusions
>
> ## 5. Add API endpoints
>
> Add:
>
> POST /discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/website-probe/mock
>
> Response:
> {
>   "candidate_id": "...",
>   "website_probe_id": "...",
>   "status": "skipped",
>   "confidence_score": 0.2
> }
>
> Add:
>
> GET /discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/website-probe
>
> Returns latest WebsiteProbe if present.
>
> ## 6. Tests
>
> Add tests for:
> - unknown candidate fails clearly
> - candidate with no domain produces skipped/needs_review with missing_domain evidence
> - ecommerce candidate with domain produces shop/checkout/b2c signals
> - rejected promotion produces skipped probe
> - needs_review promotion is included in evidence
> - endpoint POST website-probe/mock works
> - endpoint GET website-probe returns latest
> - no external calls are made
> - no legal conclusion wording appears
>
> ## 7. README
>
> Update README:
> - document Website Probe endpoints
> - clarify it is mock/lightweight signal foundation
> - clarify no Playwright/a11y scan yet
> - clarify no legal conclusion
> - clarify future full scan comes later
>
> ## 8. Validation
>
> Run:
>
> python3 -m py_compile \
>   04_filter_quality.py \
>   05_run_pipeline.py \
>   13_city_guard.py \
>   14_evidence_gate.py \
>   15_run_pipeline_guarded.py
>
> python3 15_run_pipeline_guarded.py
>
> python3 -m pytest -q
>
> docker compose up --build
>
> Manual curl flow:
>
> curl http://localhost:8000/health
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/Luebeck
>
> Use returned discovery_run_id:
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/providers/mock
>
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/runs/{discovery_run_id}/candidates
>
> Use one candidate_id:
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/enrichment/mock
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/qualification/precheck
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/promotion/evaluate
>
> curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/website-probe/mock
>
> curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/website-probe
>
> Expected for mock candidate without domain:
> - status skipped or needs_review
> - evidence includes missing_domain
> - no legal conclusion
> - no server crash
>
> # Constraints
> - No live website fetching.
> - No scraping.
> - No Playwright changes.
> - No full accessibility audit.
> - No report generation.
> - No legal scoring as final conclusion.
> - No real network calls during tests.
> - Keep PR small and reviewable.
> - Project name remains NorthAccessBFSG.
>
> # Expected Output
> Return:
> 1. Branch name
> 2. PR link
> 3. Changed files
> 4. What was added
> 5. Validation results
> 6. Manual curl results
> 7. Risks/TODOs
> 8. safe to merge: yes/no

The possible routing statuses are:\n+\n+```text\n+rejected\n+needs_review\n+ready_for_website_probe\n+```\n+\n+Promotion endpoints:\n+\n+```bash\n+curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/promotion/evaluate\n+curl http://localhost:8000/discovery/candidates/{candidate_id}/promotion\n+```\n+\n+Example response:\n+\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"candidate_id\": \"<candidate-id>\",\n+  \"promotion_decision_id\": \"<decision-id>\",\n+  \"status\": \"needs_review\",\n+  \"reason_code\": \"mock_or_test_data\",\n+  \"confidence_score\": 0.2\n+}\n+```\n+\n+The Promotion Gate is only internal routing. `ready_for_website_probe` means the candidate can move to a future Website Probe stage. It does not mean a BFSG obligation, an accessibility issue, or any violation has been established. The Website Probe stage is not implemented in this step.\n+\n This discovery layer does not scrape websites, does not perform reporting, and does not run accessibility scans for seed candidates automatically.\n \n ## Guarded Pipeline\n@@ -15,6 +15,7 @@\n from app.models.company_qualification import CompanyQualification\n from app.models.discovery_run import DiscoveryRun\n from \"id\": decision.id,\n+        \"candidate_id\": decision.lead_candidate_id,\n+        \"company_qualification_id\": decision.company_qualification_id,\n+        \"company_enrichment_id\": decision.company_enrichment_id,\n+        \"status\": decision.status.value,\n+        \"reason_code\": decision.reason_code,\n+        \"reasons\": decision.reasons,\n+        \"confidence_score\": decision.confidence_score,\n+        \"created_at\": _format_datetime(decision.created_at),\n+        \"updated_at\": _format_datetime(decision.updated_at),\n+    }\n+\n+\n @router.get(\"/places/{city}\")\n def get_places(city: str) -> dict[str, object]:\n     try:\n@@ -266,3 +286,36 @@ def read_candidate_qualification(\n             detail=f\"Company qualification not found for candidate: {candidate_id}\",\n         )\n     return _serialize_qualification(qualification)\n+\n+\n+@router.post(\"/candidates/{candidate_id}/promotion/evaluate\")\n+def evaluate_candidate_promotion(\n+    candidate_id: str, db: Session = Depends(get_db)\n+) -> dict[str, object]:\n+    try:\n+        decision = evaluate_candidate_for_promotion(db, candidate_id)\n+    \"promotion_decision_id\": decision.id,\n+        \"status\": decision.status.value,\n+        \"reason_code\": decision.reason_code,\n+        \"confidence_score\": decision.confidence_score,\n+ exc\n+    if decision is None:\n+        raise HTTPException(\n+            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,\n+            detail=f\"Promotion decision not found for candidate:
